exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar and grasp it with a reverse (underhand, palms facing you) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with feet flat, knees secured under the thigh pads and torso upright (seated position).
Attach a straight bar and grasp it with a reverse (underhand, palms facing you) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
Start with arms fully extended overhead, shoulders relaxed and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back, bringing the bar to the upper chest while keeping the scapulae down and squeezing the lats.
Pause briefly at the bottom to feel the contraction in the lats and biceps; avoid excessive backward lean.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled movement until arms are fully extended.
Keep wrists neutral and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso; reduce weight if hips or torso begin to move.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while placing extra demand on the biceps and forearms. Pull the bar down to the upper chest with elbows driving down and back while keeping the torso stable.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced flat on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
Set your chest up and maintain a neutral spine with a slight forward lean from the hips — avoid excessive torso rocking or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause for 1 second at the peak contraction with elbows pulled past the torso and the upper back engaged.
Slowly extend the arms to return the handle to the start under control until you feel a gentle stretch in the upper back; do not let the weight slam forward.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders, using excessive torso momentum, or rounding the lower back; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row using a wide grip attachment that emphasizes the upper back by driving the elbows outward to retract the shoulder blades. Performed seated with feet braced, it develops thicker upper-back musculature and posterior shoulder strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Lean forward from the hips and reach to grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders rounded forward (scapulae protracted).
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent; select an appropriate weight.
Lean forward from the hips and reach to grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders rounded forward (scapulae protracted).
Keep a neutral spine and a slight chest-up posture; initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades (pull shoulders down and back) before bending the elbows.

Pull the handle toward your torso (near the lower chest/upper abdomen) driving the elbows back and keeping them close to your sides; squeeze the shoulder blades together at the end of the concentric phase.
Pause briefly at full contraction, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding excessive torso swing or leaning back.
Slowly extend the arms and allow the shoulder blades to protract as you return to the starting position with control, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining neutral spine, controlled tempo, and full scapular retraction on each rep.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed from a seated position that primarily targets the latissimus dorsi while working the mid/upper back and arms. It develops scapular retraction and elbow flexion strength through a controlled pull and release.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench with knees secured under the pads and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with a neutral spine and chest slightly lifted; lean back only a few degrees (small recline) — do not arch the lower back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/clavicle while leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Hold and squeeze the lats briefly at the bottom of the movement without leaning excessively backward.
Slowly and under control allow the bar to return to the start position until the arms are nearly fully extended and the shoulder blades re-expand.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining controlled tempo and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar targets the latissimus dorsi with a wide overhand grip, pulling the bar down toward the upper chest. The movement also engages the biceps, rear delts and upper back muscles to stabilize and complete the pull.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Scapula Row
exercises.notes: Set up with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (kneeling on bench), the opposite foot planted on the floor for stability; grip a dumbbell in the free hand with the arm hanging straight toward the floor.
instructions: Set up with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (kneeling on bench), the opposite foot planted on the floor for stability; grip a dumbbell in the free hand with the arm hanging straight toward the floor.
Hinge forward at the hips so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a neutral spine and bracing your core; maintain a slight bend in the supporting knee.

Begin the movement by retracting and depressing the scapula of the working side (pull the shoulder blade back and down) while keeping the elbow nearly straight or allowing only a small bend; do not initiate the movement by bending the elbow.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second, feeling the contraction across the upper back and between the shoulder blades.
Slowly allow the scapula to protract and return the arm to the fully extended starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides so the previously supporting hand/knee becomes the working side. Maintain neutral spine and avoid torso rotation throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell row that emphasizes scapular retraction to target the upper back and mid-trapezius/rhomboid area. Performed from a kneeling/bent-over position with a single-arm dumbbell, focusing on pulling the shoulder blade back and down.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Assisted Pull-up
exercises.notes: Set the assistance weight on the machine so you can complete the desired reps with good form.
instructions: Set the assistance weight on the machine so you can complete the desired reps with good form.
Kneel on the machine's knee pad (or place knees on the assistance platform) and reach up to grasp the pull-up bar with a shoulder-width, pronated (overhand) grip; keep head neutral.
Before pulling, pull your shoulder blades down and back (scapular retraction) to engage the lats and upper back.
Pull your chest upward toward the bar by driving the elbows down and back, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive swinging.
Pause briefly at the top with your chin over the bar, maintaining scapular retraction.
Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position until your arms are fully extended, maintaining control throughout the eccentric phase.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, and adjust assistance if reps become too easy or too difficult.
exercises.description: Kneeling on an assisted pull-up machine, the exerciser pulls their torso up toward an overhead bar using a counterweight to reduce load; this movement primarily trains the latissimus dorsi and upper back pulling muscles. Control the descent to emphasize the eccentric phase.

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
instructions: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; maintain a neutral spine and soft knees.
Keep your torso upright and immobile; do not lean or use leg drive throughout the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears by driving the scapulae upward—think of trying to touch your shoulders to your ears.
Hold the top position and squeeze the traps and levator scapulae for 1–2 seconds while keeping the arms straight and grip firm (forearms engaged).
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control until the traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders and avoid excessive neck extension—keep your head neutral and eyes forward.
exercises.description: Standing plate-loaded lever shrugs target the upper trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding the machine handles; the lift is a vertical scapular elevation with a controlled lowering phase.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Rope Crossover Seated Row
exercises.notes: Set the low pulley and load an appropriate weight; sit on the bench or seat facing the machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Set the low pulley and load an appropriate weight; sit on the bench or seat facing the machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Reach forward and grasp the rope with both hands using a neutral grip (palms facing each other) while keeping the spine neutral and chest open; start with arms fully extended.
Lean the torso slightly forward from the hips (not rounding the lower back) to establish a stable starting position.

Exhale and pull the rope toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and down, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end of the concentric phase.
At the peak contraction, optionally separate the rope ends slightly to increase rear delt and upper-back engagement, hold for 1–2 seconds while maintaining a braced core.
Inhale and slowly extend the arms back to the start position under control, maintaining scapular control and a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and avoid jerking the torso or using momentum; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row performed with a rope attachment that emphasizes scapular retraction and the upper back while also engaging the lats and posterior delts. The lifter sits facing the low pulley and pulls the rope toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
Keep your arms straight (no shrugging with the elbows) and maintain a neutral spine and head position throughout.
Exhale and lift (shrug) both shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can, focusing on contracting the top of the traps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1–2 seconds, avoiding rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position with control until arms are fully relaxed at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement vertical and controlled; increase weight only if you can maintain form.
exercises.description: Standing exercise using dumbbells to raise the shoulders toward the ears, primarily working the trapezius muscles to develop upper-back and neck strength. Performed with a short vertical shrugging motion while keeping the arms straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.

exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Barbell Behind The Back Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright in a stable stance (feet about hip-width) with a loaded barbell held behind your thighs using an overhand grip; hands should be roughly shoulder-width apart and arms straight.
instructions: Stand upright in a stable stance (feet about hip-width) with a loaded barbell held behind your thighs using an overhand grip; hands should be roughly shoulder-width apart and arms straight. Keep a slight bend in the knees, chest up, and core braced (neutral spine).
Maintain the bar close to your body and keep your arms straight (do not bend the elbows).
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears by contracting the traps; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders — move only at the shoulder girdle (scapular elevation).
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds and actively squeeze the traps and levator scapulae.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control until the traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining a neutral spine, tight core, and straight arms; adjust grip width or load as needed to maintain proper form.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug with the bar held behind the body; raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to emphasize the upper trapezius/neck region, pause, then lower back down under control. The behind-the-back position slightly changes shoulder/scapular mechanics compared with front shrugs.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

exercises.name: Cable one arm lat pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley. Sit upright with a slight natural arch in the lower back and grasp the handle with one hand using a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your working arm fully extended overhead and your torso upright (a very slight lean back is optional), opposite hand resting on your thigh or the machine for stability.
Exhale and pull the handle straight down and slightly toward the side of your ribcage by driving your elbow down and back; lead the movement with the elbow rather than the hand.
At the bottom of the movement, pause and squeeze the lat and upper-back muscles, keeping the shoulder blade pulled down and back; avoid shrugging the shoulder up.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position with a controlled motion, allowing the lat to stretch but maintaining tension in the cable.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides. Avoid twisting the torso or using momentum — keep the movement controlled and driven by the lat.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pulldown performed from a seated position to isolate each lat muscle while limiting trunk rotation. The exercise pulls a handle from overhead down toward the side of the chest, emphasizing a strong squeeze of the lats.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

instructions: Sit upright on the lat pulldown bench with feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Reach up and take a wide, pronated (overhand) grip on the cable bar, hands wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with chest up and a slight natural arch in the lower back; lean back slightly at the hips (about 10–20 degrees) to create a stable pulling angle.
Exhale and pull the bar straight down toward the upper chest, driving the elbows down and back and leading the movement with the elbows rather than the hands.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the bottom of the movement, keeping the shoulders down and avoiding shrugging.
Pause briefly (about 1 second) at the bottom while maintaining tension, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the fully extended starting position with controlled resistance.

Performed seated with knees braced under the pads and a controlled, full range of motion.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it securely on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it securely on the floor.
Select a pair of dumbbells and sit at the end of the bench, then carefully lie prone with your chest supported on the bench and feet on the floor or toes braced for stability.
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a neutral or palms-facing-in grip on the dumbbells; maintain a neutral neck and spine.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades back and down to initiate the movement.
Drive your elbows up and back, pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs/waist while keeping elbows close to the body and squeezing the upper back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining scapular retraction and avoid shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Lower the dumbbells slowly under control to the starting position until the arms are nearly extended, keeping tension in the back throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the concentric (pull) and breathing in on the eccentric (lower).
Keep movements controlled, avoid torso rotation or using momentum, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain scapular control.
exercises.description: A prone row performed on an incline bench where the chest is supported and dumbbells are pulled toward the torso to emphasize the upper back. It uses a controlled scapular retraction and elbow drive to work the mid/upper back and pulling musculature.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent (seated, upright position).
Grab the handle (V-bar or close grip) with a neutral or overhand grip and extend your arms so the weight stack is lifted and shoulders are protracted; maintain a neutral spine and chest slightly up.
Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
Keep the torso stable—avoid excessive leaning or using momentum; maintain a slight forward trunk angle only if required to reach the handle, then return to a tall, braced posture as you pull.

Squeeze the shoulder blades and lats at the end range for a brief 1–2 second pause, then slowly extend the arms forward under control to the start position while allowing the scapulae to move forward.
Breathe out while pulling and inhale while returning to the start; choose a weight that allows controlled form and full elbow extension between reps.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed sitting with feet braced that primarily targets the lats while working the middle/upper back and arm pulling muscles. It emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive to pull the handle to the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Arm Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight up over your chest with elbows bent roughly 90 degrees; keep the elbows fixed throughout the movement.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the shoulders before beginning the descent.

Slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing shoulder extension (keeping the elbow angle constant) until you feel a stretch in the lats/upper chest area or until the bar reaches a comfortable range of motion.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the lats and serratus anterior, then pull the bar back over the chest by driving the shoulders into flexion using your lats and chest (exhale on the concentric).
Keep the movement smooth and controlled, avoiding excessive lower back arch; maintain the bent-elbow position and avoid turning the elbows outward or using momentum.
Perform the desired number of reps and then safely rack the barbell, or have a spotter assist when lowering the weight off your chest.
exercises.description: A supine barbell pullover performed with the elbows bent that moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, stretching and loading the latissimus region while also recruiting the chest and triceps. Performed with a fixed elbow angle to emphasize shoulder extension and thoracic mobility.

exercises.name: Barbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (neutral spine) with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand upright (neutral spine) with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Hold a loaded barbell with a shoulder-width, pronated (overhand) grip so the bar rests behind your thighs at arm's length.
Keep arms straight (no elbow bend) and head/neck in a neutral position throughout the set.
Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears by contracting the traps; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the top of the contraction for 1–2 seconds, focusing on the traps.
Lower the shoulders back down slowly and under control until arms are fully extended and traps are relaxed.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining upright posture and minimal torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrugs isolate the trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a loaded barbell. The movement focuses on a vertical shrugging motion with minimal elbow or torso movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench with knees secured under the pads and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with a neutral spine and chest slightly lifted; lean back only a few degrees (small recline) — do not arch the lower back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/clavicle while leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Hold and squeeze the lats briefly at the bottom of the movement without leaning excessively backward.
Slowly and under control allow the bar to return to the start position until the arms are nearly fully extended and the shoulder blades re-expand.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining controlled tempo and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar targets the latissimus dorsi with a wide overhand grip, pulling the bar down toward the upper chest. The movement also engages the biceps, rear delts and upper back muscles to stabilize and complete the pull.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arms Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your head, shoulders and upper back supported, feet flat on the floor and hips pressed down (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your head, shoulders and upper back supported, feet flat on the floor and hips pressed down (supine position).
Grasp a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral or palms-facing grip and extend the arm straight above your chest with a slight bend in the elbow.
Brace your abs and keep your ribs down to prevent excessive lower-back arching throughout the movement.

Slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing the shoulder to extend, keeping the elbow slightly bent and fixed in angle; stop when you feel a comfortable stretch in the lats/upper chest or when the upper arm is roughly parallel with the bench.
Pause briefly at the stretch, then contract the lats and chest to pull the dumbbell back in the same arc to the start position above the chest while exhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch arms. Maintain slow, controlled tempo and avoid shrugging the shoulder or using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm dumbbell pullover performed lying on a bench where the weight is moved in an arc from above the chest to behind the head, emphasizing shoulder extension to load the lats and upper chest while the core stabilizes the body.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced flat on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
Set your chest up and maintain a neutral spine with a slight forward lean from the hips — avoid excessive torso rocking or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause for 1 second at the peak contraction with elbows pulled past the torso and the upper back engaged.
Slowly extend the arms to return the handle to the start under control until you feel a gentle stretch in the upper back; do not let the weight slam forward.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders, using excessive torso momentum, or rounding the lower back; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row using a wide grip attachment that emphasizes the upper back by driving the elbows outward to retract the shoulder blades. Performed seated with feet braced, it develops thicker upper-back musculature and posterior shoulder strength.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown (pro lat bar)
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Sit on the pulldown bench with knees secured under the thigh pads and feet flat on the floor (seated, knees braced).
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Begin tall with a neutral spine and a slight lean back from the hips (not from the lower back), chest lifted and eyes forward.
Inhale, then exhale and initiate the movement by pulling the shoulder blades down and together (scapular retraction) to engage the lats.
Pull the bar down in a controlled arc toward the upper chest (sternal area), leading with the elbows and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with straight arms, fully extending the shoulders while maintaining a stable torso.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding excessive torso lean or momentum; keep movement controlled and driven by the lats.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar performed from a tall seated position, pulling the bar down to the upper chest to target the latissimus dorsi and upper back. Focus is on scapular retraction and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Stand facing a low cable pulley with feet about hip-width apart and attach a straight bar or handle to the low setting.
instructions: Stand facing a low cable pulley with feet about hip-width apart and attach a straight bar or handle to the low setting.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angle), keeping a slight bend in the knees; this is a bent-over position.
Maintain a neutral spine and head in line with the torso, bracing your core and keeping the chest lifted throughout the set.
Reach forward and grab the bar with a comfortable grip (overhand or neutral), allowing the cable to pull your arms fully extended.
Initiate the row by driving your elbows straight back toward your hips, squeezing the shoulder blades together and leading with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at full contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding any excessive torso rotation or momentum.
Slowly extend your arms under control back to the start position until the lats are stretched and the shoulder blades open, inhale on the return.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movements controlled and spine neutral; reduce weight if you feel lower back rounding or excessive swinging.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed with a low cable pulley that targets the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades while the torso is hinged forward. The movement also engages the lats, arms and posterior shoulder while the posterior chain and core stabilize the body.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
instructions: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; maintain a neutral spine and soft knees.
Keep your torso upright and immobile; do not lean or use leg drive throughout the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears by driving the scapulae upward—think of trying to touch your shoulders to your ears.
Hold the top position and squeeze the traps and levator scapulae for 1–2 seconds while keeping the arms straight and grip firm (forearms engaged).
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control until the traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders and avoid excessive neck extension—keep your head neutral and eyes forward.
exercises.description: Standing plate-loaded lever shrugs target the upper trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding the machine handles; the lift is a vertical scapular elevation with a controlled lowering phase.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
Brace your core, keep the elbow straight (but not locked) and shoulders relaxed before initiating the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) the shoulder of the working arm straight up toward the ear, using only the shoulder girdle—do not roll or rotate the shoulder.
Pause and squeeze the traps at the top for 1–2 seconds, focusing on the contraction near the neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulder back to the starting position under control until the arm is fully extended and the trap is relaxed.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch arms; maintain neutral head position and avoid using momentum throughout the set.
exercises.description: A standing single-arm dumbbell shrug that elevates the shoulder to target the upper trapezius and surrounding neck muscles. Performed with one arm at a time to concentrate on trap contraction and improve unilateral strength and symmetry.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Seated High Row (V-bar)
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar. Sit on the bench facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip and sit upright with a tall chest, slight natural lumbar curve, and shoulders relaxed downward.
Start with your arms extended overhead (or up in front) so there is tension on the cable and your shoulder blades are not fully protracted.
Exhale and pull the V-bar down and back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage and slightly behind you, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction).
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) with your torso stable and not leaning back excessively.
Inhale and slowly control the V-bar back to the start position, allowing the arms to extend while maintaining core tension and posture.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep movements smooth, avoid jerking or using momentum, and do not let the lower back round or overextend.
exercises.description: Seated cable high row using a V-bar is a horizontal/upper-angle pulling movement that targets the muscles of the upper back by driving the elbows down and back while squeezing the shoulder blades together. It uses a neutral grip to emphasize middle traps, rhomboids and lats with assistance from the arms.

exercises.name: Lever Front Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads when seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads when seated upright.
Sit upright (not reclined) with a neutral spine, grasp the overhead handles with a pronated (palms facing away) or neutral grip depending on the machine, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Begin with your arms extended overhead and your shoulder blades elevated; brace your core and keep chest up.
Exhale and pull the handles down in a smooth arc toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and back, focusing on squeezing the lats and pinching the shoulder blades together.
Avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum; keep the movement controlled and lead with the elbows rather than pulling with the hands.
Pause briefly at the bottom with the handles near your chest while maintaining scapular retraction and lat tension.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with a controlled motion until your arms are extended overhead and your shoulder blades are slightly apart.
Repeat for the desired reps. Use a weight that allows full, controlled range of motion and maintain steady breathing throughout (exhale pulling down, inhale returning up).
exercises.description: Seated lever front pulldown performed on a machine where you pull handles from overhead down in front of you; it primarily targets the lats while engaging the upper back and arm muscles. Maintain a tall torso and lead the movement with the elbows to maximize back activation.

exercises.name: Lever Bent over Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine or plate-loaded bar to the desired weight and stand with feet hip-width under the bar/platform.
instructions: Set the lever machine or plate-loaded bar to the desired weight and stand with feet hip-width under the bar/platform.
Hinge at the hips with a soft bend in the knees until your torso is approximately 30–45 degrees from vertical (bent-over position); keep the spine neutral and neck in line with the torso.
Grasp the bar with an overhand (or chosen) grip about shoulder-width apart and let your arms hang straight down; engage your core to stabilize the trunk.
Initiate the rep by pulling your elbows back and driving the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a braced torso, then slowly lower the bar back to the start under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or rounding of the lower back throughout the set.
exercises.description: A bent-over lever row performed from a hip-hinged position to drive the loaded bar toward the torso, emphasizing the mid/upper back. It develops pulling strength and upper-back thickness while also engaging the arms and rear shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Wide Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Place a loaded barbell across the back of your hips/upper thighs or behind your body and take a wide, overhand grip (hands wider than shoulder-width).
Keep your chest up, core braced, neck neutral and arms straight (no elbow bend) throughout the movement.
Raise (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible, focusing on contracting the traps—do not roll the shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the traps.
Slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding forward/backward lean.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug performed with a wide, behind-the-back grip to place emphasis on the upper traps; elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears while keeping the arms extended and torso stable.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (straight arm)
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley.
instructions: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley. Select a moderate weight that allows controlled movement while keeping arms mostly straight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart (or a slight stagger for balance). Grasp the handle with both hands (pronated or neutral grip) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.

Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso leans about 10–20 degrees; keep knees soft and maintain a neutral spine. Arms should be extended in front of you with a small, fixed bend in the elbows (not fully locked).

Initiate the rep by driving the shoulders into extension: press the hands downward and back in a controlled arc until the hands reach thigh level and you feel maximal contraction in the lats. Keep the elbows stationary and avoid bending them to pull.
Pause briefly at the bottom while squeezing the lats and maintaining straight-arm tension.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position under control, keeping constant tension on the cable and avoiding letting the shoulders fully relax at the top.
Breathe out during the downward (working) phase and inhale while returning to the start. Perform desired number of reps with steady tempo.
exercises.description: Standing straight-arm cable pushdowns target the lats by driving the arms down from a high cable with minimal elbow flexion. The movement emphasizes shoulder extension and scapular retraction while keeping the torso stable.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a loaded barbell within reach on the floor or on low supports.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a loaded barbell within reach on the floor or on low supports.
Lie face down (prone) on the bench so your chest is supported, feet braced on the floor or bench for stability, and your torso is fixed against the pad.
Reach down and grasp the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip about shoulder-width apart. Allow your arms to hang fully extended.
Before pulling, draw your shoulder blades down and together (scapular retraction) to start the movement from the back rather than the arms.
Pull the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows back and slightly out.
At the top, squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold briefly (about 1 second) while keeping the torso pressed to the bench and neck neutral.
Lower the barbell slowly back to full arm extension under control, maintaining scapular tension and avoiding any bouncing or trunk rotation.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale while lowering. Keep movement strict—do not jerk the weight or lift your chest off the bench.
exercises.description: Prone row performed on an incline bench where the torso is supported and a barbell is pulled toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. It emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the upper back, rear delts and arm muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.

Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet away, feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent; hinge forward at the hips so your torso is angled forward (standing bent-over position) with a neutral spine.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) about shoulder-width apart; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows throughout (do not lock).
Brace your core and depress your shoulder blades slightly (avoid shrugging) so tension is felt in the lats before starting the pull.

Exhale and pull the bar down in a controlled arc by extending the shoulders and pulling with the lats until the bar reaches your upper thighs or mid-thigh level; keep the arms long and the elbows fixed in their slight bend.
Squeeze the lats and hold the bottom position briefly while keeping the ribs down and spine neutral.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, resisting the cable so the lats remain under tension; do not let the lower back round as you return.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and long-arm position on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge from the hips and pull a high cable down with mostly straight arms to load the lats through a long-range extension. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while keeping the torso braced.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and chest pad (if present) so your feet are flat on the platform and the handles are within comfortable reach with your arms extended.
instructions: Adjust the seat and chest pad (if present) so your feet are flat on the platform and the handles are within comfortable reach with your arms extended.
Select an appropriate weight on the lever stack.
Sit upright with your chest against the pad (or seated tall if no pad), knees slightly bent, feet stable, and maintain a neutral spine and head alignment.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a firm, neutral grip; start with your arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows backward and slightly down, retracting your shoulder blades to bring the handles toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Keep elbows close to your sides, avoid excessive torso lean or shrugging the shoulders, and breathe out as you pull.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze your lats and upper back.
Slowly extend your arms to return the handles to the start position under control, allowing the shoulder blades to protract slightly but maintain tension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever rows are a horizontal pulling exercise performed on a lever machine that primarily targets the lats while also engaging the middle and upper back and arm muscles. The movement involves pulling handles toward the torso with a scapular retraction and controlled return.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Palm Rotational Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Keep a neutral spine and neck (eyes toward the floor a few feet in front of you).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand. Slightly bend the knees and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (bent-over position).
Keep a neutral spine and neck (eyes toward the floor a few feet in front of you). Let the dumbbells hang at arm's length beneath your shoulders with a pronated or neutral grip.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction).

Pull the dumbbells upward toward your lower ribs/waist by driving the elbows up and back. As you ascend, rotate your wrists so the palms turn (for example from pronation/neutral toward supination) at the top of the row.
Squeeze the upper back and rear delts at the top for a brief pause, keeping elbows close to the body and avoiding excessive torso rotation or momentum.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position while rotating the palms back to the initial orientation, maintaining control throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps. Maintain the hip hinge, slight knee bend, and neutral spine for the entire set.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row variation where the wrists/palms rotate during the pull to increase scapular retraction and arm involvement. Performed in a hip-hinged position, it emphasizes the upper back while also engaging the lats, rear delts and arms.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Cross Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the rollers while seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the rollers while seated upright.
Select an appropriate weight on the lever stack.
Reach up and grasp the overhead handles with a comfortable grip (neutral or slightly wider than shoulder width), arms fully extended but not locked.
Begin the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then pull the handles down and slightly across your body toward the upper chest or outer chest, leading with the elbows.
Keep a slight lean back in the torso (not rounded) and avoid using momentum—drive the movement with the lats and upper back, squeezing at the bottom for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly return the handles to the start position with control until your arms are nearly extended and shoulders are not shrugged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady breathing and avoiding excessive swinging or lower-back drive.
exercises.description: Seated lever pulldown performed on a machine where you pull an overhead lever/handle down and slightly across the body to drive shoulder extension and adduction, emphasizing the lats. Focus on scapular retraction and controlled tempo to maximize lat engagement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
