exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Back Seated Row
exercises.notes: Set your spine in a neutral (straight) position with chest up and shoulders relaxed—avoid rounding the back or excessive leaning.
instructions: Sit on the bench with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; grasp the close/V-handle with both hands and sit tall.
Set your spine in a neutral (straight) position with chest up and shoulders relaxed—avoid rounding the back or excessive leaning.
Begin the repetition by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction) to initiate the movement.
Continue pulling the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows straight back; keep elbows close to the body.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the end range for 1 second while maintaining an upright seated position (do not hyperextend the lower back).
Return the handle slowly under control by extending the arms and allowing the scapulae to protract until arms are nearly straight.
Breathe out as you pull, inhale as you return; use a controlled tempo and avoid using torso momentum to complete the reps.
exercises.description: Seated cable row performed with a straight, neutral spine; pull the handle toward the torso using shoulder blade retraction to emphasize the lats and upper back. Maintain an upright torso and controlled tempo throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Cable Front Seated Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench facing the machine with your chest pressed against the vertical support pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90°.
Reach forward and grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed.
Keep your torso upright and core braced; maintain contact with the chest pad throughout the movement.
Exhale and pull the handle straight back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the end range when your hands reach your torso and you feel a full contraction across the upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward in a controlled manner until they are nearly straight, resisting the weight on the return.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding trunk rotation, excessive trunk lean, or shrugging the shoulders; keep the motion controlled and driven by the back, not the arms.
exercises.description: A seated cable row variation performed facing the machine with chest supported by a pad, pulling a single handle straight toward the torso to work the mid/upper back. Emphasis is on scapular retraction and elbow drive to target the upper back and lats.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
Sit upright with a slight lean back (about 10–20 degrees), chest up, and scapulae slightly retracted; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Inhale, brace your core, then exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back until the bar reaches your upper chest or collarbone level.
Squeeze your lats and upper back at the bottom of the motion while keeping wrists neutral and avoiding excessive neck or shoulder shrugging.
Slowly control the weight back to the start by allowing the arms to extend fully overhead while maintaining scapular tension (do not fully relax shoulders between reps).
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out on the pull and inhale on the return, and avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a supinated (reverse/underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. Pull the bar from an overhead position down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and shoulders retracted.

exercises.name: Cable Neutral Grip Wide Pulldown
exercises.notes: Reach up and grasp the wide neutral/V-bar handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands placed wide on the bar.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with knees secured under the pads, feet flat on the floor and select an appropriate weight.
Reach up and grasp the wide neutral/V-bar handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands placed wide on the bar.
Start with arms fully extended overhead, chest lifted and a slight controlled lean back (about 10–20°); keep the spine neutral.
Exhale and pull the handle down by driving your elbows down and back toward your sides, aiming to bring the handle to your upper chest/collarbone area.
Squeeze the lats and pinch the shoulder blades together at the bottom of the movement; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Pause briefly, then inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position under control until the arms are nearly straight, maintaining tension in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding excessive torso momentum or pulling the bar behind the neck.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a neutral (palms-facing) wide grip that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the upper back and arm muscles. The handle is pulled down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and focusing on elbow-driven movement.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.

Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet away, feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent; hinge forward at the hips so your torso is angled forward (standing bent-over position) with a neutral spine.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) about shoulder-width apart; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows throughout (do not lock).
Brace your core and depress your shoulder blades slightly (avoid shrugging) so tension is felt in the lats before starting the pull.

Exhale and pull the bar down in a controlled arc by extending the shoulders and pulling with the lats until the bar reaches your upper thighs or mid-thigh level; keep the arms long and the elbows fixed in their slight bend.
Squeeze the lats and hold the bottom position briefly while keeping the ribs down and spine neutral.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, resisting the cable so the lats remain under tension; do not let the lower back round as you return.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and long-arm position on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge from the hips and pull a high cable down with mostly straight arms to load the lats through a long-range extension. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while keeping the torso braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
Keep your arms straight (no shrugging with the elbows) and maintain a neutral spine and head position throughout.
Exhale and lift (shrug) both shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can, focusing on contracting the top of the traps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1–2 seconds, avoiding rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position with control until arms are fully relaxed at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement vertical and controlled; increase weight only if you can maintain form.
exercises.description: Standing exercise using dumbbells to raise the shoulders toward the ears, primarily working the trapezius muscles to develop upper-back and neck strength. Performed with a short vertical shrugging motion while keeping the arms straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

exercises.name: Cable Low Seated Row
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to the low position and select an appropriate weight; sit on the bench or seat with feet braced on the foot platform and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the low position and select an appropriate weight; sit on the bench or seat with feet braced on the foot platform and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handle with both hands using a neutral or overhand grip, then sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted; brace your abs.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward (the starting position).
Exhale and pull the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together — keep elbows close to your body.
Avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum; maintain a stable, upright torso (a small, controlled lean is acceptable but do not rock).
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while maintaining scapular retraction and a tight core.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms to return the handle to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to protract but not round excessively.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping movements smooth and controlled; lower the weight if you find yourself shrugging or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated low cable row is a horizontal-pulling exercise performed from a seated position that primarily develops the lats while also engaging the mid- and upper-back and arm muscles. It emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive to pull the handle toward the torso.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent (seated, upright position).
Grab the handle (V-bar or close grip) with a neutral or overhand grip and extend your arms so the weight stack is lifted and shoulders are protracted; maintain a neutral spine and chest slightly up.
Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
Keep the torso stable—avoid excessive leaning or using momentum; maintain a slight forward trunk angle only if required to reach the handle, then return to a tall, braced posture as you pull.

Squeeze the shoulder blades and lats at the end range for a brief 1–2 second pause, then slowly extend the arms forward under control to the start position while allowing the scapulae to move forward.
Breathe out while pulling and inhale while returning to the start; choose a weight that allows controlled form and full elbow extension between reps.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed sitting with feet braced that primarily targets the lats while working the middle/upper back and arm pulling muscles. It emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive to pull the handle to the torso.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.

Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet away, feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent; hinge forward at the hips so your torso is angled forward (standing bent-over position) with a neutral spine.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) about shoulder-width apart; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows throughout (do not lock).
Brace your core and depress your shoulder blades slightly (avoid shrugging) so tension is felt in the lats before starting the pull.

Exhale and pull the bar down in a controlled arc by extending the shoulders and pulling with the lats until the bar reaches your upper thighs or mid-thigh level; keep the arms long and the elbows fixed in their slight bend.
Squeeze the lats and hold the bottom position briefly while keeping the ribs down and spine neutral.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, resisting the cable so the lats remain under tension; do not let the lower back round as you return.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and long-arm position on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge from the hips and pull a high cable down with mostly straight arms to load the lats through a long-range extension. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while keeping the torso braced.

exercises.name: Cable Close Grip Front Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your feet are flat on the floor and the thigh pads sit snugly over your thighs to prevent you from rising during the pull.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your feet are flat on the floor and the thigh pads sit snugly over your thighs to prevent you from rising during the pull.

Select an appropriate weight and attach a close-grip (V-bar or narrow neutral) handle. Sit upright on the bench facing the machine and grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated close grip.
Start with your arms fully extended overhead, chest up, shoulders down and back, and a slight natural arch in the lower back; lean back only a few degrees (not a full lean).
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down toward your sides and back, squeezing your shoulder blades together and pulling the handle to the upper chest or clavicle area.
Pause for 1 second at the bottom while maintaining tension in the lats and keeping the torso stable (avoid excessive swinging or using momentum).
Slowly extend your arms back to the start under control until elbows are nearly straight, feeling a stretch in the lats, then repeat for the desired reps.
Keep breathing steadily (exhale during the pull, inhale during the return) and avoid shrugging the shoulders or letting the wrists bend excessively to protect the traps and forearms.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown using a close/narrow grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi while reducing wide-grip involvement; performed by pulling the handle to the upper chest with elbows driving down and back. Useful for building lower and mid-lat thickness with increased biceps and grip involvement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (neutral spine) with feet about hip-width apart and knees unlocked (soft bend).
instructions: Stand upright (neutral spine) with feet about hip-width apart and knees unlocked (soft bend).
Grip a barbell with both hands, arms fully extended, using an overhand (pronated) or mixed grip; hold the bar at thigh level in front of your body.
Keep your torso tall, chest up and head neutral — avoid leaning or using lower back to lift.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears by contracting the traps and levator scapulae; do not rotate or roll the shoulders.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction while keeping arms straight and elbows relaxed.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start, controlling the descent through the full range.
Repeat for the desired reps; maintain a steady tempo, avoid using momentum, and keep a firm grip (forearms engaged) throughout.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrugs elevate the shoulders to target the upper trapezius and surrounding scapular elevators; the movement is a vertical scapular elevation with the arms kept straight. Perform controlled shrugs up toward the ears, pause at the top, then lower slowly to the start.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Arm Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight up over your chest with elbows bent roughly 90 degrees; keep the elbows fixed throughout the movement.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the shoulders before beginning the descent.

Slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing shoulder extension (keeping the elbow angle constant) until you feel a stretch in the lats/upper chest area or until the bar reaches a comfortable range of motion.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the lats and serratus anterior, then pull the bar back over the chest by driving the shoulders into flexion using your lats and chest (exhale on the concentric).
Keep the movement smooth and controlled, avoiding excessive lower back arch; maintain the bent-elbow position and avoid turning the elbows outward or using momentum.
Perform the desired number of reps and then safely rack the barbell, or have a spotter assist when lowering the weight off your chest.
exercises.description: A supine barbell pullover performed with the elbows bent that moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, stretching and loading the latissimus region while also recruiting the chest and triceps. Performed with a fixed elbow angle to emphasize shoulder extension and thoracic mobility.

exercises.name: Cable Seated High Row (V-bar)
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar. Sit on the bench facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip and sit upright with a tall chest, slight natural lumbar curve, and shoulders relaxed downward.
Start with your arms extended overhead (or up in front) so there is tension on the cable and your shoulder blades are not fully protracted.
Exhale and pull the V-bar down and back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage and slightly behind you, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction).
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) with your torso stable and not leaning back excessively.
Inhale and slowly control the V-bar back to the start position, allowing the arms to extend while maintaining core tension and posture.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep movements smooth, avoid jerking or using momentum, and do not let the lower back round or overextend.
exercises.description: Seated cable high row using a V-bar is a horizontal/upper-angle pulling movement that targets the muscles of the upper back by driving the elbows down and back while squeezing the shoulder blades together. It uses a neutral grip to emphasize middle traps, rhomboids and lats with assistance from the arms.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Shrug
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
instructions: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.

Pick up a barbell with a double overhand (pronated) grip about shoulder-width to slightly wider, arms fully extended so the bar hangs in front of your thighs; keep your torso neutral and head in a natural position.
Brace your core and maintain a tall, upright posture (no leaning forward or backward) throughout the movement.
Without bending the elbows, lift (shrug) the shoulders straight up toward the ears by elevating the scapulae; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds to maximize contraction of the traps.
Lower the shoulders slowly and under control to the starting position, allowing a full stretch of the traps between reps.
Breathe out as you shrug up and inhale as you lower; perform the desired number of reps while keeping movement strict and controlled.
exercises.description: A seated barbell shrug isolates the trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a barbell; performing it seated reduces leg drive so the traps take most of the load. It emphasizes strong, vertical shoulder elevation (scapular elevation) with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Cable one arm lat pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley. Sit upright with a slight natural arch in the lower back and grasp the handle with one hand using a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your working arm fully extended overhead and your torso upright (a very slight lean back is optional), opposite hand resting on your thigh or the machine for stability.
Exhale and pull the handle straight down and slightly toward the side of your ribcage by driving your elbow down and back; lead the movement with the elbow rather than the hand.
At the bottom of the movement, pause and squeeze the lat and upper-back muscles, keeping the shoulder blade pulled down and back; avoid shrugging the shoulder up.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position with a controlled motion, allowing the lat to stretch but maintaining tension in the cable.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides. Avoid twisting the torso or using momentum — keep the movement controlled and driven by the lat.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pulldown performed from a seated position to isolate each lat muscle while limiting trunk rotation. The exercise pulls a handle from overhead down toward the side of the chest, emphasizing a strong squeeze of the lats.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Pulldown (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads; sit upright with your chest against the chest pad if present.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads; sit upright with your chest against the chest pad if present.
Load the desired plates on the machine and grasp the overhead lever/bar with an overhand (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Start with your arms fully extended overhead, shoulders relaxed, and a slight natural arch in your spine — do not excessively lean back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the lever toward your upper chest; lead with the elbows rather than the hands.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades as you reach the bottom of the motion and pause briefly while squeezing the lats.
Slowly return the lever to the start position under control until your arms are fully extended and the shoulders are not rounded forward.
Keep the torso still and avoid using momentum or excessive backward lean; breathe out while pulling down and inhale while returning to the start.
exercises.description: A seated, plate-loaded pulldown performed on a lever machine that targets the latissimus dorsi by pulling a fixed lever down from overhead toward the upper chest while keeping the torso stable. Emphasize elbow-driven movement and scapular retraction to maximize lat engagement.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Single Arm Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle on a high pulley and position an adjustable bench under it with the pad inclined ~30–45° so your chest will be supported when seated.
instructions: Set a single-handle on a high pulley and position an adjustable bench under it with the pad inclined ~30–45° so your chest will be supported when seated.
Sit on the bench facing the pulley, press your chest firmly against the incline pad and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability (chest-supported/prone on the incline pad).
Reach up with one arm and grasp the handle with a neutral grip (palm facing inward) so the arm is fully extended overhead — this is the start position.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbow down and back toward your hip, leading with the elbow and pulling the handle in an arc so the hand finishes beside your ribcage.
Squeeze the lat and retract the shoulder blade at the bottom of the movement, hold for a brief pause (1 second), then slowly allow the arm to extend back to the start under control.
Keep your chest pressed to the bench, avoid rotating or leaning the torso, and do not use momentum — exhale as you pull, inhale as you return to start.
Perform all repetitions on one side before switching arms to maintain consistent unilateral loading and focus on equal work per side.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pulldown performed while chest-supported on an incline bench to isolate the latissimus dorsi. The high-pulley single-handle setup emphasizes unilateral pulling strength and minimizes torso cheat.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a reverse (palms-up / supinated) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the dumbbells under your chest; brace your core and keep the neck in line with the spine.
Initiate the pull by driving the elbows upward and backward, pulling the dumbbells toward the lower ribcage/upper waist while keeping elbows close to the body.
At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the shoulder blades together without rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control until the arms are extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the weights up and inhale as you lower them; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. The lifter hip-hinges and pulls the dumbbells toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Lever Single Arm Reverse Grip High Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up: load appropriate plate weight and adjust the seat/chest pad so the handle is within easy reach overhead when seated; sit with chest pressed against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set up: load appropriate plate weight and adjust the seat/chest pad so the handle is within easy reach overhead when seated; sit with chest pressed against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grip: reach up with one hand and take a reverse (supinated/palm-up) grip on the handle; keep the opposite hand resting on the machine or your knee for stability.
Starting position: sit upright with a slight natural arch in the low back, shoulder blades relaxed, and the working arm nearly extended (small bend in elbow).

Pull: exhale and pull the handle down and back in a controlled arc toward your upper chest/shoulder, leading with the elbow and driving the elbow toward your torso while squeezing the lats and upper-back (scapular retraction).
Hold and control: pause briefly (about 1 second) at the peak contraction while maintaining a stable torso; avoid rotating or leaning backward.
Return: inhale and slowly allow the handle to return to the start under control, extending the arm but not locking the elbow.
Reps and side switch: perform the desired number of repetitions with one arm, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain steady tempo and avoid using momentum or excessive shoulder shrugging.
exercises.description: A single-arm, supinated (reverse) grip high row on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes pulling the handle down and back from a high position to work the lats and upper-back musculature while stabilizing with the chest pad.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about shoulder-width), hold a dumbbell in one hand with the arm fully extended at your side and palm facing the body; keep a neutral head and spine.
Brace your core, keep the elbow straight (but not locked) and shoulders relaxed before initiating the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) the shoulder of the working arm straight up toward the ear, using only the shoulder girdle—do not roll or rotate the shoulder.
Pause and squeeze the traps at the top for 1–2 seconds, focusing on the contraction near the neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulder back to the starting position under control until the arm is fully extended and the trap is relaxed.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch arms; maintain neutral head position and avoid using momentum throughout the set.
exercises.description: A standing single-arm dumbbell shrug that elevates the shoulder to target the upper trapezius and surrounding neck muscles. Performed with one arm at a time to concentrate on trap contraction and improve unilateral strength and symmetry.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Stand upright in front of the plate-loaded lever machine with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand upright in front of the plate-loaded lever machine with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral (thumbs-forward) grip so the loaded plates hang at arm's length; keep your arms straight and the torso tall with chest open.
Keep your head in a neutral position and core braced to prevent torso movement throughout the set.
Exhale and slowly shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the upper traps; avoid rolling the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds with a strong contraction in the traps.
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down in a controlled manner until arms are fully extended and traps are relaxed.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form and avoiding momentum or torso leaning.
exercises.description: Standing plate-loaded lever shrugs involve vertically elevating the shoulders while holding the machine handles to isolate the trapezius muscles. The movement is a short, controlled shoulder elevation and lowering motion with the arms kept straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable one arm lat pulldown
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley and sit facing the machine on the seat with knees secured under the pads; sit upright with feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Setup: Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley and sit facing the machine on the seat with knees secured under the pads; sit upright with feet flat on the floor.
Grip and position: Reach up and grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip using one hand; keep the torso tall with a slight backward lean from the hips and brace your core.

Start position: Allow the arm to be fully extended overhead while maintaining scapular stability (shoulder blades slightly down and back), and keep the opposite hand resting or lightly stabilizing the thigh.
Pull: Exhale and pull the handle down and back by driving your elbow toward your hip/side of the torso, focusing on squeezing the lats and retracting the shoulder blade.
Peak contraction: Pause briefly at the bottom when your hand reaches about chest/hip level and you feel a strong contraction in the lats; avoid rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulder.
Return: Inhale and slowly control the handle back up to the fully extended start position, resisting the weight to maintain tension throughout the eccentric phase.
Repetitions and switch: Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch to the other arm to maintain balance.
exercises.description: Seated single-arm cable pulldown uses a high pulley and single handle to pull one arm down and back, isolating the lats while encouraging unilateral strength and balance. The movement emphasizes pulling the elbow toward the hip/side of the torso with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Rear Pulldown Behind Neck
exercises.notes: Set the lat pulldown machine with a wide bar attachment and select an appropriate weight; sit on the bench and lock your thighs under the pads with feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set the lat pulldown machine with a wide bar attachment and select an appropriate weight; sit on the bench and lock your thighs under the pads with feet flat on the floor.
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide, overhand grip (palms facing away) with hands wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with a slight lean back at the hips, brace your core, and allow your arms to start fully extended overhead.

Initiate the movement by pulling your elbows down and back, driving the bar behind your head toward the upper trapezius/base of the skull while leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the bottom with the shoulder blades fully retracted, then slowly and under control allow the bar to return to the starting position until the arms are fully extended.
Breathe out while pulling the bar down and inhale while returning; avoid jerking, using momentum, or forcing the bar too far behind the neck to reduce shoulder strain.
Use a moderate weight and stop the exercise if you experience shoulder or neck pain; consider using a front pulldown variation if you have limited shoulder mobility.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip pulldown where the bar is pulled down behind the neck to emphasize the upper portion of the lats and upper back. The movement uses a wide overhand grip and scapular retraction to drive the descent.

exercises.name: Lever T-bar Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up the lever T-bar station: load plates on the bar, position the foot platform, and select the handle attachment.
instructions: Set up the lever T-bar station: load plates on the bar, position the foot platform, and select the handle attachment. Stand on the platform with feet about hip-width apart.

Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees so your torso is angled forward (roughly 30–45 degrees) while keeping a neutral spine and chest up; grip the handles with a double overhand or neutral grip.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades before initiating the lift to maintain a stable torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and pull the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and squeezing the lats and mid-back, keeping elbows relatively close to the body.
Pause briefly at the top with scapulae squeezed and chest slightly lifted, avoiding excessive leaning back or shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and lower the handle under control until the arms are nearly fully extended and you feel a stretch in the lats, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep tempo controlled, avoid using momentum, and maintain the hinged standing position throughout the set.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever T-bar row is a bent-over rowing movement performed from a hinged standing position that primarily targets the lats while engaging the upper back and pulling muscles. It involves pulling a landmine-style bar/handle toward the torso to develop thickness and strength through the mid-back.

exercises.name: Cable High Row with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.

Position an incline bench or chest pad in front of the machine so you can sit facing the pulleys with your chest pressed firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor; adjust pad height so torso is supported and arms can reach the handles overhead.
Sit tall with chest against the pad, neutral neck, core braced and shoulders down away from ears; reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Begin with arms nearly extended overhead, scapulae slightly protracted; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and out, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end range.
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a supported chest and no torso rotation.
Slowly release the handles under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the start while keeping tension on the cables and scapulae engaged.
Repeat for desired reps; avoid using momentum, leaning away from the pad, or shrugging the shoulders upward.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported cable high row performed from dual high pulleys; you pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest while the chest rests against the pad to isolate the upper back. Emphasizes scapular retraction and posterior shoulder activation with reduced lower-back involvement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
Keep your arms straight (no shrugging with the elbows) and maintain a neutral spine and head position throughout.
Exhale and lift (shrug) both shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can, focusing on contracting the top of the traps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1–2 seconds, avoiding rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position with control until arms are fully relaxed at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement vertical and controlled; increase weight only if you can maintain form.
exercises.description: Standing exercise using dumbbells to raise the shoulders toward the ears, primarily working the trapezius muscles to develop upper-back and neck strength. Performed with a short vertical shrugging motion while keeping the arms straight.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent (seated, upright position).
Grab the handle (V-bar or close grip) with a neutral or overhand grip and extend your arms so the weight stack is lifted and shoulders are protracted; maintain a neutral spine and chest slightly up.
Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
Keep the torso stable—avoid excessive leaning or using momentum; maintain a slight forward trunk angle only if required to reach the handle, then return to a tall, braced posture as you pull.

Squeeze the shoulder blades and lats at the end range for a brief 1–2 second pause, then slowly extend the arms forward under control to the start position while allowing the scapulae to move forward.
Breathe out while pulling and inhale while returning to the start; choose a weight that allows controlled form and full elbow extension between reps.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed sitting with feet braced that primarily targets the lats while working the middle/upper back and arm pulling muscles. It emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive to pull the handle to the torso.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Lever Neutral Grip Seated Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the machine to the desired weight and sit facing the machine with your chest supported on the chest pad and feet braced on the foot platform; knees should be bent and stable.
instructions: Load the machine to the desired weight and sit facing the machine with your chest supported on the chest pad and feet braced on the foot platform; knees should be bent and stable.
Reach forward and grasp the neutral (palms-facing) handles with a firm grip, arms extended and shoulders relaxed; start with a tall, braced torso and a tight core.
Exhale and pull the handles straight back by driving your elbows toward your hips, keeping elbows close to your body and squeezing the shoulder blades together at peak contraction.
Hold the contracted position for about 1 second while maintaining a stable chest on the pad and avoiding excessive torso lean or momentum.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms under control, allowing the shoulder blades to protract and return to the starting position without dropping the weight.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining controlled tempo and full range of motion on each rep.
exercises.description: Seated neutral-grip lever row performed on a plate-loaded machine to target the latissimus dorsi while stabilizing the torso against a pad. Pull the handles toward the torso with a neutral (palms-facing) grip, squeeze the shoulder blades, then slowly return to the start.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip so your palms face down and arms are fully extended.
instructions: Sit on the machine seat with knees bent and feet planted on the footrests; brace your chest against the chest pad (if present) and sit upright.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip so your palms face down and arms are fully extended.
Set your spine neutral, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and maintain a tall chest—do not round your back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, pulling the handles toward your lower chest/upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and feel the lats and mid-back engage; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start position in a controlled manner until your arms are nearly straight and the lats are stretched.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping consistent tempo, full range of motion, and a firm grip throughout.
exercises.description: Seated pronated-grip lever row performed on a plate-loaded machine to develop the lats and mid-upper back by pulling a lever toward the torso using an overhand grip. Emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive while maintaining an upright, braced seated position.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

exercises.name: Lever High Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present).
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present). Adjust seat so handles are at or slightly above shoulder height when arms are extended.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended and shoulders relaxed. Take a full breath and brace your core.

Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the handles down and back toward the upper chest/near clavicle, leading with the elbows and keeping them at roughly shoulder height (elbows flared).
Squeeze the shoulder blades and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1 second, keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive trunk lean or momentum.
Slowly extend the arms back to the start position under control (eccentric phase), maintaining tension in the upper back and preventing the weights from slamming.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out during the pull and inhale during the release.

Common form tips: keep the neck neutral, avoid shrugging the shoulders up, don’t use excessive body swing, and adjust seat/handle height so the pull targets the upper back rather than purely the lats.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded high row where you pull overhead/forward handles toward the upper chest to emphasize the upper back (rhomboids, mid traps and rear delts) while also engaging lats and arm muscles. The torso is stabilized against a pad and movement is performed in a controlled pull-and-release.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Set a pulley to low or mid position and attach single handles or a straight bar; choose a moderate weight.
instructions: Set a pulley to low or mid position and attach single handles or a straight bar; choose a moderate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip so your arms hang straight at your sides.
Keep your torso stationary and arms straight (small bend in the elbows is fine); maintain a neutral neck and look forward.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears by contracting the traps — avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Pause at the top for 1 second, focusing on a strong contraction in the upper traps.
Inhale and lower the shoulders slowly and under control back to the start position until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining an upright posture and avoiding momentum to ensure the traps are doing the work.
exercises.description: A standing cable shrug performed with handles at the sides to elevate the shoulders and load the upper trapezius. The movement isolates upward shoulder elevation while keeping the arms nearly straight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
instructions: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and weight evenly distributed on the mid-foot; keep a slight bend in the knees (not locked).
Brace your core and maintain an upright torso; focus on keeping shoulders down and the bar stable on your traps/delts.
Slowly raise both heels by pushing through the balls of your feet until you reach full plantarflexion (stand on your toes); contract the calves at the top and hold for 1–2 seconds.
Lower your heels back to the floor in a controlled manner until the soles are flat, feeling a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the ascent and inhaling on the descent.
If balance is an issue, perform the movement near a wall or use a spotter; avoid bouncing or using excessive forward trunk lean to lift the weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed with a barbell resting on the upper back to load the lower legs; raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles, pause at the top, then lower with control. This exercise primarily isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves) while the upper back and shoulders stabilize the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
