exercises.name: Barbell Wide Squat
exercises.notes: Set a barbell in a squat rack at about upper-chest/shoulder height and load appropriate weight; use safety pins or a spotter if needed.
instructions: Set a barbell in a squat rack at about upper-chest/shoulder height and load appropriate weight; use safety pins or a spotter if needed.
Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper back/traps (not on your neck); grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Step back from the rack and take a wide stance (wider than shoulder-width) with toes pointed slightly outward (~30 degrees).
Brace your core, keep chest up and spine neutral, and ensure knees are tracking in the same direction as your toes.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the hips and knees to lower your body until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor (or lower if mobility allows).
Keep weight distributed through your heels and midfoot; avoid letting the knees cave inward.
Exhale and drive through the heels to extend the hips and knees, returning to the starting standing position while maintaining an upright torso.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and carefully re-rack the bar when finished.
exercises.description: A wide-stance barbell squat performed with the bar across the upper back; the wider stance and outward foot angle place extra emphasis on the inner thighs and quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes and hamstrings. Lower by bending hips and knees until the thighs are about parallel, then drive back up through the heels.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
Position feet about hip-width apart and keep a slight, natural bend in the knees (do not lock the knees).
Engage your abs to stabilize the torso and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet until you are fully on your toes and calves are fully contracted; avoid rocking the torso forward.
Pause for 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the step level until you feel a stretch in the calves (control the descent — do not drop abruptly).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping movements smooth and maintaining balance; if needed, reduce weight or use a rail/wall for light support.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed on a raised step while holding dumbbells at the sides to load the lower leg; the movement raises the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). Emphasis is on controlled concentric raise and slow eccentric lowering below the step for stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
Set a slight bend in the knees (soft knees) and brace your core to stabilize the spine before initiating the movement.

Hinge at the hips by pushing your hips straight back while keeping the spine neutral and chest slightly lifted; allow the dumbbells to slide down the front of your thighs and shins, staying close to the body.
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach mid‑shin (maintain neutral cervical alignment, do not round the lower back).
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward, squeezing the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the knees softly bent and the weights close to the legs.
Maintain controlled tempo (slow on the descent, controlled drive on the ascent), keep the shoulders down away from the ears, and avoid using the lower back to lift.
exercises.description: A standing hip‑hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings while the hips move back and forth; the movement also engages the glutes and core to maintain a neutral spine. Keep a slight knee bend and the weights close to the legs throughout.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Squat Calf Raise on Leg Press Machine
exercises.notes: Set the lever/leg-press machine to a comfortable weight and sit with your back pressed into the seat (seated/squat position).
instructions: Set the lever/leg-press machine to a comfortable weight and sit with your back pressed into the seat (seated/squat position). Grip the side handles for stability.
Place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the footplate, feet about hip-width and toes pointing forward; knees should be bent (approximately 90°) throughout the exercise.
Unlock the machine safety if applicable and allow your heels to lower until you feel a stretch in the calves — this is the start position.
Exhale and contract the calves to raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet, lifting as high as possible without extending or locking the knees; pause 1 second at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back toward the start position under control until you feel a stretch in the calves; avoid bouncing.
Maintain a stable torso and fixed knee angle throughout the set so movement comes primarily from the ankle joint.
After completing the set, lock or re-engage the machine safety before releasing the handles and exiting the seat.
exercises.description: A seated, squat-style calf raise performed on a leg-press/lever machine where the user plantarflexes the ankles against the footplate to target the calf muscles while the hips and knees remain in a flexed (seated/squat) position.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Lever Squat (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load desired plates and set any safety stops on the lever machine.
instructions: Load desired plates and set any safety stops on the lever machine.

Sit back against the angled pad (leaning/reclined position) and place your shoulders under the shoulder pads or hold the upper handles; position feet shoulder-width on the footplate with heels firmly planted.
Unlatch or disengage the safety / lock so the sled can move freely.

Brace your core and keep your back pressed into the pad; inhale and slowly bend the knees to lower the sled until your thighs are about parallel to the footplate (or until a controlled depth where knees track over toes).

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the legs and then exhale as you drive through the heels to extend the knees and hips, pushing the footplate away until legs are nearly straight but not locked out.
Control the descent back to the start position for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral head and spine and steady breathing throughout.
When finished, carefully re-engage the safety / latch before releasing the shoulder pads or standing up.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever squat performed on a reclining/hack-style sled where you push a footplate away to extend the knees and hips. The movement emphasizes knee extension under load with the back supported by the angled pad.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
Set a slight bend in the knees (soft knees) and brace your core to stabilize the spine before initiating the movement.

Hinge at the hips by pushing your hips straight back while keeping the spine neutral and chest slightly lifted; allow the dumbbells to slide down the front of your thighs and shins, staying close to the body.
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach mid‑shin (maintain neutral cervical alignment, do not round the lower back).
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward, squeezing the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the knees softly bent and the weights close to the legs.
Maintain controlled tempo (slow on the descent, controlled drive on the ascent), keep the shoulders down away from the ears, and avoid using the lower back to lift.
exercises.description: A standing hip‑hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings while the hips move back and forth; the movement also engages the glutes and core to maintain a neutral spine. Keep a slight knee bend and the weights close to the legs throughout.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Step onto a raised, stable platform or aerobic step so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can drop freely; stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width and toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a comfortable overhand grip to maintain control; engage your abs and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout (standing position).
Keep knees straight but not locked (a slight soft bend) and maintain weight balanced over the balls of your feet.
Exhale and raise your heels by plantarflexing at the ankle, lifting as high as possible onto your toes and pausing 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent — do not drop quickly or bounce.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and full range of motion. Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack if using heavy weight.
When finished, carefully step down from the platform and re-rack the bar in the squat rack.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed from a raised platform with a barbell across the upper back; rise onto the toes to concentrically contract the calves, then lower the heels below the platform for a full stretch. This is a standing, weight-bearing isolation exercise for the calves with upper-back and core stabilization.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Walking Lunge
exercises.notes: Step 1: Position the barbell across your upper back (high-bar position) and grip it firmly with hands just outside shoulder width; stand tall with feet hip-width apart and core braced (standing).
instructions: Step 1: Position the barbell across your upper back (high-bar position) and grip it firmly with hands just outside shoulder width; stand tall with feet hip-width apart and core braced (standing).

Step 2: Maintain an upright torso and tight abs; take a controlled long step forward with one foot (about 2–3 feet depending on leg length) so your stride allows the front thigh to descend toward parallel.

Step 3: Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground; keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid letting it collapse inward.
Step 4: Push through the front heel to extend the front hip and knee, driving the back leg forward and bringing the rear foot up to pass the front foot (walking motion) into the next step.
Step 5: Continue alternating forward steps for the desired number of reps, keeping an even, controlled tempo and breathing out as you push up.
Step 6: Keep the barbell stable on the upper back, shoulders slightly retracted, and avoid excessive forward lean; if balance becomes difficult, reduce weight or shorten stride.
Step 7: To finish, carefully step back to the rack area if needed or come to a stop, then rerack the barbell with proper form or set it down safely.
exercises.description: A loaded, forward-stepping lunge that emphasizes the front thigh (quadriceps) while also recruiting the glutes and hamstrings for hip extension and balance. The barbell rests across the upper back as you take controlled steps forward and lower into each lunge.

exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform so your heels can hang freely; grip the machine handles for stability.
instructions: Sit on the machine seat with knees bent (approximately 90°) and your lower thighs positioned under the padded lever so the pad rests comfortably on top of the thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform so your heels can hang freely; grip the machine handles for stability.
Load the lever arm with the desired weight plates and ensure the machine is secured before beginning.
Start with your heels lowered below the platform to get a full stretch in the calves.
Exhale and drive through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, raising your heels as high as possible by contracting the calf muscles.
Pause and squeeze the calves briefly at the top (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a neutral spine and firm grip on the handles.
Inhale and lower your heels slowly and with control back to the starting stretched position — avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the planned number of repetitions, maintaining a slow, controlled tempo and keeping the knees stationary under the pad throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated calf raise performed on a plate-loaded lever machine; with knees bent and a padded lever pressing on the thighs, you plantarflex the ankles to raise the heels and lift the load, isolating the calves (mainly the soleus).

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Stiff Legged Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Set a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff-legged, not fully locked) and brace your core so the spine is neutral; chest up and shoulders back.
Initiate the rep by hinging at the hips: push your hips straight back while keeping the bar close to your legs and maintaining a long, flat back.
Lower the bar in a controlled manner until you feel a deep stretch in the hamstrings (bar will be around mid-shin or just below the knees depending on mobility); do not let the lower back round.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes to return to a standing position while keeping the slight knee bend.
Exhale as you reach the top, maintain control on the descent, and repeat for the desired number of reps.
Safety notes: reduce range of motion or weight if your lower back rounds, keep the neck neutral (look a few feet ahead), and use straps or lighter loads if grip (forearms) fails before hamstrings.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge movement performed with a barbell and minimal knee bend that lengthens and loads the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back. Keep a neutral spine and the bar close to the legs throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Landmine Rear Lunge
exercises.notes: Setup: Secure one end of the barbell in a landmine attachment.
instructions: Setup: Secure one end of the barbell in a landmine attachment. Stand facing the bar with feet about hip-width apart and the working leg slightly forward. Grasp the free end of the bar with both hands close to the chest in a goblet-style hold (elbows tucked).
Brace your core and stand tall with a neutral spine and shoulders down and back. Keep weight distributed through the midfoot and heel of the front foot.

Step the non-working leg straight back onto the toes (standing on the ball of the back foot) and lower into a controlled rear lunge by bending both knees until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor or the back knee approaches the ground.

Keep the torso upright (slight forward lean allowed), chest proud, and the front knee tracking over the toes without collapsing inward. Maintain a firm grip on the bar against the chest throughout the movement.
Drive through the front heel and contract the glutes to push yourself back up to the starting standing position while bringing the back foot forward.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch legs. Breathe in while lowering and exhale while driving back up.

Coaching tips: Use a controlled tempo, avoid letting the front knee travel excessively past the toes, and reduce weight if balance or form breaks down. Keep the elbow position stable to avoid undue shoulder strain.
exercises.description: A unilateral loaded lunge performed with a landmine bar held at the chest that emphasizes hip extension to drive the back leg and load the glutes while the front leg controls descent and ascent. It challenges single-leg strength, balance, and core stability.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Ipsilateral Single Leg Stiff Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright on one leg (working leg) with a dumbbell held in the hand on the same side as the standing leg (ipsilateral).
instructions: Stand upright on one leg (working leg) with a dumbbell held in the hand on the same side as the standing leg (ipsilateral). Keep the non-working foot lightly off the floor or resting just behind the heel for balance.
Set a small, controlled bend in the knee of the working leg and brace your core so your spine remains neutral; keep shoulders back and the chest long.

Hinge at the hips by pushing them straight back while maintaining the slight knee bend; allow the dumbbell to lower straight down the front of the working leg toward the mid-shin while the non-working leg extends straight back to counterbalance.
Keep hips square to the floor and avoid rotating the torso; maintain a neutral neck by looking at a point on the floor a few feet in front of you.
Lower until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (torso approx. parallel to the floor or limited by flexibility), then pause briefly.
Drive the hips forward, squeezing the hamstrings and glutes to return to the upright start position while exhaling; keep the movement slow and controlled throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides so the dumbbell is held in the opposite hand on the opposite standing leg.
Breathing and tempo: inhale on the descent, exhale on the ascent; maintain control to protect the lower back and prioritize balance.
exercises.description: A single-leg Romanian (stiff-leg) deadlift performed while holding a dumbbell on the same side as the standing leg; it emphasizes a hip hinge to load the hamstrings and glutes while challenging single-leg balance and core stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back fully supported against the back pad (seated position) and grip the handles for stability.
Position the lower leg pad just above your heels / at the lower calf (behind the ankles) and secure the thigh pad across your front upper thighs to prevent hip movement.
Start with your legs nearly extended (knees close to straight but not locked) and feet pointed forward.
Exhale and curl your lower legs downward/back by flexing the knees, bringing the pad toward the underside of your thighs; contract the hamstrings and glutes at the peak.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, then inhale and slowly extend your knees to return the weight to the start position with controlled tempo—do not let the stack slam or lock the knees.
Keep your hips and torso stable against the pad throughout; avoid lifting the hips or using momentum to move the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and adjust weight if you cannot maintain smooth, controlled reps.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl performed on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to target the hamstrings while the seat/back pad stabilizes the hips. The movement curls the lower leg back toward the underside of the thigh and then returns under control to the start.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell High Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about upper-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about upper-chest height. Step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar position), not on the neck.
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width and retract your shoulder blades to create a stable shelf; lift the bar off the rack and take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack.
Stand with feet approximately shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward. Brace your abs and keep a tall, upright torso.
Initiate the squat by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, keeping weight distributed through the midfoot to heels.
Descend under control until your thighs are approximately parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows), ensuring knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward.
Maintain chest up and a neutral spine throughout the descent; keep the upper back tight to support the bar.
Drive through the heels and midfoot, extend the knees and hips simultaneously to return to a standing position, exhaling as you rise.
Reset your brace at the top, then repeat for the desired number of repetitions. When finished, step forward into the rack and safely re-rack the bar.
exercises.description: A barbell high bar squat places the bar on the upper traps and emphasizes knee flexion to target the quads while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings. The lifter descends to at least parallel with an upright torso, then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Leg Diagonal Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.

Stand upright facing the machine with the non-working foot planted on the floor (or on a small balance pad) and a slight bend in that knee; grasp the machine or hold a handle with one hand for light support and stabilization.
Shift your weight onto the planted leg, brace your abs to keep the torso stable and upright, and allow the working leg to hang straight behind you with toes pointed or slightly flexed.

Without bending the working knee, drive the leg straight back and diagonally across your body (slightly outward and behind), contracting the glute at the top of the movement; avoid arching the lower back or leaning the torso forward.
Pause briefly at full extension while squeezing the glute, then slowly return the leg to the starting position under control without letting the cable pull your torso out of alignment.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-leg diagonal kickbacks use a low-pulley ankle attachment to extend a straight working leg backward and slightly across the midline, emphasizing hip extension and glute contraction while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
instructions: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
Grip the side handles and ensure your lower back and hips are pressed firmly against the backpad; unlock the sled safety/locks if present.
Start position: Bend your knees to bring your thighs toward your chest until knees are about 90 degrees, keeping feet flat on the plate and knees tracking over toes.
Press: Exhale and drive through your heels and mid-foot to extend your knees and hips, pushing the footplate away until legs are nearly straight but do not lock the knees at full extension.
Return: Inhale and slowly control the sled back down by bending the knees, lowering the weight under tension until your knees reach about 90 degrees again.
Repetitions: Repeat for the desired number of reps with controlled tempo; avoid bouncing at the bottom or using momentum to lock the knees.
Safety cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the pad throughout, avoid lifting hips off the pad, and keep knees aligned with feet. Rerack/lock the sled before exiting.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed supine on an inclined sled platform where you push the footplate away using the legs. The movement primarily extends the knees to target the quadriceps while the glutes, hamstrings, and calves assist and stabilize.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
instructions: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
Plant your feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart so that at the top of the movement your knees are roughly bent to 90 degrees; keep chin tucked and gaze forward or slightly up.
Brace your abs and grip the barbell on each side to steady it; maintain a neutral spine (avoid overarching the lower back).
Drive through your heels and contract the glutes to lift your hips upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line (hips fully extended); squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Lower the hips slowly and under control until they are just above the floor or until you feel a stretch in the glutes/hamstrings, maintaining core tension throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement smooth and controlled and avoiding thrusting with the lower back.
If needed, adjust foot distance to emphasize glutes (feet slightly forward) or hamstrings (feet further from bench) while preserving a stable foot contact and neutral spine.
exercises.description: The barbell hip thrust is a supine, bench-supported hip extension exercise that primarily develops the glutes by driving the hips upward against resistance. It also recruits the hamstrings and quads while the core stabilizes the trunk.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
Sit upright with feet under the roller pad, grip the side handles or seat edges for stability, and keep your chest lifted and core engaged.
Start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or the machine's starting position) and inhale to prepare.
Exhale and extend your legs by straightening the knees until they are almost fully extended — do not lock the knees at the top.
Pause briefly (0.5–1 second) at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until knees return to the initial bend.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding momentum or bouncing.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knees against a padded lever. The exercise focuses on controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering to target the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
instructions: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
Set the lower shin pad so it rests just above your ankles when seated; grip the handles or seat edges for stability.
Sit upright with your chest up, core engaged, and feet under the pad (seated position).
Exhale and extend your knees by pressing the pad forward with your lower shins until your legs are nearly straight; avoid hyperextending or locking the knees.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled motion until your knees return to approximately 90°.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping the torso stable and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever-style machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance while seated. The torso stays braced against the backpad and the lower shin pushes the pad forward until the knee is nearly straight.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
Position your shoulders under the padded lever, stand tall and grip the machine handles for stability; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward (or slightly turned in/out to vary emphasis).
Begin with a full stretch: lower your heels down until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves (keep torso upright and spine neutral).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, exhaling and keeping knees straight (a slight knee bend shifts emphasis to the soleus if desired).
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top and squeeze the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the stretched starting position with control—avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and proper alignment throughout.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing calf raise where the shoulders are braced under a padded lever and the heels drop and rise from a platform to load the calf muscles through a full range of motion. Emphasizes plantarflexion to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat

instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.

exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight. Step under the bar and rest it across your upper back/traps with a secure, pronated grip; unrack the bar and take a small step back.

Position your feet in a split stance: one foot forward (working leg) and the other foot back with toes on the floor and heel elevated slightly; keep a distance so the front knee can bend to about 90 degrees at the bottom.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright and chest lifted throughout the movement; gaze forward to help maintain posture.

Slowly lower your body by bending the front knee and pushing the hips back slightly until the front thigh is around parallel to the floor or you reach a comfortable depth; the rear knee should lower toward the floor but not necessarily touch.
Ensure the front knee tracks in line with your toes (avoid collapsing inward) and keep weight primarily through the front heel and midfoot.
Drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip, returning to the starting split stance. Exhale on the drive.
Complete the desired number of reps on the working leg, then carefully step the back foot forward, re‑rack the bar, reset, and repeat with the opposite leg.
exercises.description: A unilateral, loaded lower‑body exercise performed in a staggered (split) stance with a barbell on the upper back that emphasizes the front leg's quadriceps while also working the glutes and hamstrings for hip control and the core for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back fully supported against the back pad (seated position) and grip the handles for stability.
Position the lower leg pad just above your heels / at the lower calf (behind the ankles) and secure the thigh pad across your front upper thighs to prevent hip movement.
Start with your legs nearly extended (knees close to straight but not locked) and feet pointed forward.
Exhale and curl your lower legs downward/back by flexing the knees, bringing the pad toward the underside of your thighs; contract the hamstrings and glutes at the peak.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, then inhale and slowly extend your knees to return the weight to the start position with controlled tempo—do not let the stack slam or lock the knees.
Keep your hips and torso stable against the pad throughout; avoid lifting the hips or using momentum to move the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and adjust weight if you cannot maintain smooth, controlled reps.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl performed on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to target the hamstrings while the seat/back pad stabilizes the hips. The movement curls the lower leg back toward the underside of the thigh and then returns under control to the start.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward or slightly out, barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
Set a neutral spine (no rounding), pull shoulder blades down and back, lift the chest and brace the abs tightly.
Take a deep breath, drive through the heels and mid-foot, extend the hips and knees together to lift the bar, keeping the bar close to the shins and thighs.
Continue driving the hips forward until standing tall with hips fully extended and shoulders stacked over hips (lockout); do not hyperextend the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with glutes engaged and core braced.
To lower, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back while maintaining a neutral spine, let the bar pass the knees, then bend the knees and place the bar back on the floor under control.
Reset breathing and core tension before each repetition and keep consistent form; avoid rounding the spine and jerking the bar.
exercises.description: A heavy compound hip-hinge lift that develops the glutes and the posterior chain by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to a standing position. The movement also requires strong spinal bracing and upper-back stability while challenging grip strength.

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Stand with feet hip-width, unrack the bar and take a small step forward so the bar is balanced; shift your weight onto one foot and lift the other foot slightly off the floor or cross it behind the working leg.
Keep the working knee slightly bent (not fully locked) and maintain an upright torso with core engaged.
Exhale and press through the ball of the working foot to rise up as high as possible onto your toes (heel raise), focusing on contracting the calf.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower slowly back down with control until your heel returns to the floor (or to neutral dorsiflexion), keeping balance throughout.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then carefully re-rack the bar if switching legs, or step back under the bar and repeat for the opposite leg.
Maintain steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep shoulders and hips squared forward to prevent compensations.
exercises.description: A unilateral standing calf raise performed inside a Smith machine with the bar resting on the upper back; raises the body onto the ball of one foot to load the calf muscles. Performed on the floor (not a raised step) for balance and single-leg strength and stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
Grasp the handles at chest level (or hold the pad if no handles) and set a moderate weight; maintain a neutral head position and brace your core.

Exhale and contract your abs to curl your torso forward and down, flexing the spine while keeping the hips stationary and using controlled motion — continue until you feel a full contraction of the abs.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement while keeping tension in the abs; do not pull with your arms or yank with momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting upright position with a controlled motion, maintaining core engagement and avoiding hyperextension of the spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Adjust weight to maintain strict form and avoid using neck or hip drive to complete the movement.
exercises.description: A seated, machine-based crunch performed with a chest pad and lever; the exercise flexes the torso forward against resistance to target the rectus abdominis. The knees and feet are stabilized while the upper torso curls forward, emphasizing spinal flexion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests on the rear delts / just below the spine of the scapula (low-bar position).
instructions: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests on the rear delts / just below the spine of the scapula (low-bar position).
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width, retract the shoulder blades and push the elbows back to create a stable shelf across the upper back; stand to unrack the bar.
Step back from the rack with feet about shoulder-width to slightly wider, toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable, flat-footed stance.
Take a deep breath and brace your abs and upper back tight to create full-body tension before descent.
Begin the descent by sending the hips back and down while allowing the knees to track outward over the toes; keep the chest up and the spine neutral.
Lower until thigh crease is around parallel to the floor or your comfortable depth while maintaining tension and a consistent bar path over mid-foot.
Drive upward by pressing through the heels and mid-foot, extending hips and knees simultaneously while keeping the core braced and the upper back stable.
Exhale/finish the rep at full hip and knee extension, reset your brace as needed, then repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
When finished, step forward under control and rack the bar safely on the supports.
exercises.description: A barbell low-bar squat places the bar lower across the rear delts/upper back and emphasizes a hip-dominant squat pattern while still loading the thighs. The exercise trains knee and hip extension by lowering to squat depth and driving back up through the heels.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
instructions: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
Plant your feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart so that at the top of the movement your knees are roughly bent to 90 degrees; keep chin tucked and gaze forward or slightly up.
Brace your abs and grip the barbell on each side to steady it; maintain a neutral spine (avoid overarching the lower back).
Drive through your heels and contract the glutes to lift your hips upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line (hips fully extended); squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Lower the hips slowly and under control until they are just above the floor or until you feel a stretch in the glutes/hamstrings, maintaining core tension throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement smooth and controlled and avoiding thrusting with the lower back.
If needed, adjust foot distance to emphasize glutes (feet slightly forward) or hamstrings (feet further from bench) while preserving a stable foot contact and neutral spine.
exercises.description: The barbell hip thrust is a supine, bench-supported hip extension exercise that primarily develops the glutes by driving the hips upward against resistance. It also recruits the hamstrings and quads while the core stabilizes the trunk.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

exercises.name: Lever Rotary Calf
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your back against the pad and knees bent at about 90 degrees; adjust the thigh pad so it rests firmly on your thighs.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your back against the pad and knees bent at about 90 degrees; adjust the thigh pad so it rests firmly on your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with heels hanging off the edge; grip the handles at your sides for stability.
Start with your ankles dorsiflexed (heels lowered) to feel a stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, raising your heels as high as comfortably possible while maintaining control.
If the machine allows rotary motion, rotate the footplate slightly inward or outward across sets or reps to emphasize medial or lateral calf fibers while maintaining the same plantarflexion pattern.
Pause briefly at the top for maximal contraction, then inhale and slowly lower your heels back to the starting stretched position with a controlled tempo.
Keep the movement smooth, avoid bouncing, and maintain a stable seated posture (do not lift hips or lock knees).
exercises.description: Seated lever rotary calf is a seated plantarflexion exercise performed on a lever/rotary calf machine that isolates the calf muscles through a full range of motion, often allowing a slight rotary/angle change at the footplate to target different calf fibers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Rack Lunge
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and the barbell racked across the front of your shoulders (front-rack) with elbows high and upper arms roughly parallel to the floor; grip the bar with either a clean grip or cross-arm position.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and the barbell racked across the front of your shoulders (front-rack) with elbows high and upper arms roughly parallel to the floor; grip the bar with either a clean grip or cross-arm position.
Brace your core, keep your chest tall and shoulders back to maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Take a controlled long step forward with one foot (standing position to forward lunge), landing on the heel of the front foot.

Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is approximately parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground (about 90° at the front knee); keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid letting it collapse inward.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining an upright torso and tight core; keep the bar stable on the front shoulders by keeping elbows up.
Drive through the front heel to push yourself back to the starting standing position, fully extending the front hip and knee.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch legs and perform the same number of repetitions on the opposite side.
Safety notes: choose a weight that allows you to keep an upright torso and high elbows; if balance is difficult, reduce the load or perform stationary split squats until comfortable.
exercises.description: A loaded lunge performed from a front-rack barbell position that primarily targets the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The bar is held on the front shoulders with elbows high while stepping forward and lowering into a controlled lunge.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Mixed grip Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower your torso until you can grip the bar; use a mixed grip (one palm facing you, one palm facing away) with hands slightly outside the knees.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the bar over the mid-foot while standing (upright position to start).
Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower your torso until you can grip the bar; use a mixed grip (one palm facing you, one palm facing away) with hands slightly outside the knees.
Set your back: pull the shoulder blades back, lift the chest, and maintain a neutral spine (avoid rounding) while your hips sit between a low and mid position.
Brace your core and take a full breath, keep the bar close to your shins (bar touches or tracks just above the mid-foot).
Drive through the heels and extend the hips and knees simultaneously to lift the bar in a controlled hip-hinge, keeping the bar close to your body and shoulders over or slightly in front of the bar.
Stand tall at the top by fully extending the hips and squeezing the glutes; avoid excessive lumbar extension (no hyperextension of the spine).
Lower the bar under control by hinging at the hips first, then bending the knees when the bar passes them, returning the bar to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine (repeat for desired reps).
If using heavy loads, alternate which hand is supinated between sets to balance grip stress and reduce muscle imbalances.
exercises.description: A conventional barbell deadlift using a mixed (over/under) grip to improve grip security while lifting; it trains the hip hinge to drive the bar from the floor to standing, emphasizing the posterior chain. The mixed grip helps prevent the bar from rolling in heavy lifts.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your knees just off the edge, chest supported, and hook your lower calves/ankles under the padded lever.
Grip the handles or edge of the bench for stability and keep your hips pressed into the bench throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl your heels up toward your glutes by flexing the knees until you reach a full but comfortable contraction.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the hamstrings and glutes.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start with controlled extension of the knees, avoiding letting the hips rise.
Perform repetitions with steady tempo, focusing on full range of motion and control rather than momentum.
exercises.description: Prone (face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by bending the knees to lift a pad toward the glutes. The movement emphasizes knee flexion with a controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
Sit upright with feet under the roller pad, grip the side handles or seat edges for stability, and keep your chest lifted and core engaged.
Start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or the machine's starting position) and inhale to prepare.
Exhale and extend your legs by straightening the knees until they are almost fully extended — do not lock the knees at the top.
Pause briefly (0.5–1 second) at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until knees return to the initial bend.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding momentum or bouncing.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knees against a padded lever. The exercise focuses on controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering to target the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, arms hanging in front of your thighs.
