exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lunge
exercises.notes: Stand tall in a neutral, upright position holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip), feet hip-width apart.
instructions: Stand tall in a neutral, upright position holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip), feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs) and keep your chest up and shoulders back throughout the movement.
Take a controlled step forward about 2–3 feet with one leg so you can lower into a comfortable lunge (adjust distance for your limb length).
Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor and the rear knee is just above the ground; aim for about a 90° angle at the front knee.
Keep the front knee tracking over the toes (do not let it collapse inward) and avoid letting the front knee travel excessively past the toes.
Press through the front heel and drive the hip forward to return to the starting standing position, squeezing the glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch legs (or perform alternating lunges) maintaining steady tempo and controlled movement.
Breathe in as you lower, exhale as you push back up; use a weight that allows good form and full range of motion.
exercises.description: A weighted forward lunge performed standing while holding dumbbells at your sides; it emphasizes the front thigh (quadriceps) while also recruiting the glutes and hamstrings for hip drive and calves/abs for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Glute Press
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine to the desired weight and adjust the chest pad height so your torso is upright and supported when gripping the handles.
instructions: Set the lever machine to the desired weight and adjust the chest pad height so your torso is upright and supported when gripping the handles.
Stand facing the machine with your chest pressed into the pad, both hands holding the handles for support, and feet on the platform or footpads as required by the machine.
Position the working leg so the foot is centered on the footplate with the knee slightly bent (single‑leg variation) or place both feet if using both legs; keep the non‑working leg clear of the path.
Brace your core (abs) and maintain a neutral spine and fixed chest on the pad; avoid leaning forward or arching the lower back.
Drive the working foot backward and slightly upward by extending the hip, contracting the glute until the leg is nearly straight or you reach a controlled end range of hip extension.
Pause briefly at full contraction, squeezing the glute, then slowly return the lever to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it snap back.
Complete the prescribed reps, then switch legs and repeat. Keep movement smooth and controlled throughout; reduce range or weight if you feel lumbar strain.
exercises.description: A chest‑supported, single‑leg hip extension performed on a lever machine that drives the footplate backward to extend the hip and contract the glute. It develops unilateral hip power and isolates the glute while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
instructions: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
Place a barbell across your upper thighs just above the knees; use a pad if needed for comfort and grip the bar with both hands to steady it (seated position).
Position the balls of your feet on the floor (or a small raised platform) while keeping heels hanging or supported so your ankles can move through full range.
Keeping your torso upright and core engaged, exhale and press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.

Pause briefly at the top with the calves fully contracted, then inhale and slowly lower the heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves but maintain control (avoid letting the weight slam down).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; maintain a steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep knees stationary throughout the set.
To stop, carefully roll the barbell forward off your thighs or have a spotter help lift it off before standing.
exercises.description: Seated on a bench with a loaded barbell resting across the thighs, raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the calf muscles. The movement is performed with knees bent, emphasizing the soleus portion of the calves through controlled heel raises and lowers.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Stiff Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, arms hanging in front of your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, arms hanging in front of your thighs.
Soften the knees slightly (small, fixed bend) and keep a neutral spine and head in line with the torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips straight back (hip hinge), lowering the dumbbells down the front of the legs toward mid-shin while maintaining the slight knee bend.
Keep the dumbbells close to the legs, shoulders back and chest open — avoid rounding the lower back; stop when you feel a deep hamstring stretch or when your torso is near parallel to the floor.
Reverse the movement by driving the hips forward, engaging the hamstrings and glutes to stand back up while keeping the arms straight and the weights close to the body.
Squeeze the glutes at the top and maintain tight core/bracing; repeat for prescribed reps while controlling the eccentric (lowering) phase.
Progress by increasing weight gradually and prioritize form over load; avoid excessive knee bend or rounded spine to reduce injury risk.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge movement performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings while keeping the legs relatively straight. Lower the weights by pushing the hips back, then extend the hips to return to standing and squeeze the glutes.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your back flat against the pad, chest up, and grip the side handles for stability; keep the non-working leg relaxed on the floor or slightly bent.
Start with the working knee bent to about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and the foot dorsiflexed (toes up) so the pad contacts above the ankle.
Exhale and extend the working knee by lifting the lower leg until it is almost straight; avoid fully locking the knee at the top.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize quad contraction while maintaining a controlled posture (abs engaged, back against pad).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it drop.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions, then switch sides and perform the same steps for the other leg.
exercises.description: Seated single-leg extension on a lever machine that isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to focus on each quad and correct imbalances.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Straight Legs Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.
Soften the knees slightly (do not lock them) and set a neutral spine by bracing the abs and pulling the shoulder blades back slightly.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips straight back (hip hinge), keeping the dumbbells close to the front of the thighs.
Lower the dumbbells down the front of your legs until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (around mid-shin or just below the knees), maintaining a flat back and neutral neck.
Keep the knees only slightly bent throughout; avoid excessive knee bend or letting the lower back round.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the dumbbells close to the legs.
Maintain a firm grip so the forearms remain engaged, exhale as you stand, and repeat for the desired reps.
Use lighter weights or reduce range of motion if you cannot maintain a neutral spine or hamstring tension throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back; performed with nearly straight legs and a neutral spine. Keep the weights close to the legs and drive the hips back and forward to move the load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly back and down to engage the upper back, and keep a neutral (flat) spine throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, a dumbbell in each hand held at arm's length at your sides (or in front of thighs) with a neutral grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly back and down to engage the upper back, and keep a neutral (flat) spine throughout the movement.
Slightly bend the knees (soft knees) — do not squat — and hinge at the hips by pushing them back while keeping the chest open and eyes forward or slightly down.
Lower the dumbbells in a straight path close to your legs until they reach about mid-shin (or you feel a deep stretch in the hamstrings) while maintaining the neutral spine.
Drive through the heels and contract the glutes and hamstrings to extend the hips and return to a tall standing position; squeeze the glutes at the top without hyperextending the lower back.
Keep the dumbbells close to the body during the entire movement and maintain even breathing (inhale on the descent, exhale as you stand).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining form; reduce range of motion or weight if you cannot keep a neutral spine.
Common faults to avoid: rounding the lower back, letting the knees collapse inward, using the arms to lift (should be hip-driven), and hyperextending the lumbar spine at the top.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge strength exercise using dumbbells to develop the posterior chain; it emphasizes hip extension to drive the body upright while maintaining a neutral spine. Performed from a standing position with a slight knee bend, lowering the weights toward the shins and then driving the hips forward to return to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Press (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load appropriate plates on the machine's weight pegs and ensure the safety latch/lock is engaged before sitting.
instructions: Load appropriate plates on the machine's weight pegs and ensure the safety latch/lock is engaged before sitting.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the backrest, feet placed about shoulder-width apart on the center of the footplate (toes slightly pointed forward), and grip the side handles.
Disengage the safety/lock or release the lever according to the machine's design so the footplate can move freely.
Inhale and slowly bend your knees to lower the footplate until your knees are about 90 degrees (or a comfortable depth) while keeping your lower back pressed into the pad and avoiding hip rise.
Exhale and drive through your heels and mid-foot to extend your legs, straightening the knees until nearly extended but do not lock the knees at the top.
Control the return by slowly bending the knees to repeat the next rep; maintain steady tempo and full contact of your back with the pad throughout.
After the final repetition, re-engage the safety/lock before removing your feet from the footplate and unloading plates if necessary.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever leg press where you push a footplate away using your legs while your back is supported on an angled seat. Primarily develops knee extension strength (quadriceps) with assistance from the glutes, hamstrings and calves.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright in a split stance with one foot forward and the other foot stepped back (rear foot on the toes).
instructions: Stand upright in a split stance with one foot forward and the other foot stepped back (rear foot on the toes). Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, arms straight and palms facing the body.
Brace your core and keep your torso tall and neutral (avoid excessive forward lean). Position feet so that when you lower, the front knee tracks over the front toes without collapsing inward.

Begin the descent by bending both knees, lowering your hips straight down until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor or the front knee reaches about 90 degrees. Allow the rear knee to move toward the floor but not to rest on it.
Keep weight distributed through the front heel and midfoot; maintain even balance and a stable pelvis throughout the movement.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining an upright torso and engaged core.
Drive through the front heel to straighten the front leg and return to the starting upright split stance. Keep the movement controlled; do not lock the knee at the top.
Repeat for desired repetitions, then switch legs and perform the same number on the opposite side.
exercises.description: A single-leg, stationary lunge performed with dumbbells to load the front leg; it emphasizes the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings for hip stability. The movement is done in a split stance with a controlled descent and drive through the front heel to return to standing.

exercises.name: Sled Calf Press On Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up the leg press sled with an appropriate weight and unlock the safety catches.
instructions: Set up the leg press sled with an appropriate weight and unlock the safety catches.
Sit reclined with your back and head firmly against the pad (supine/reclined position) and place the balls of your feet and toes on the lower edge of the footplate so your heels can hang freely.
Keep your knees slightly bent (do not lock) and grip the handles or sides of the seat for stability.
Inhale, then exhale and press through the balls of your feet to lift the sled by plantarflexing your ankles, raising your heels as high as comfortable.
Pause briefly at the top for a full contraction of the calves.
Slowly lower your heels back down below the level of the footplate to achieve a controlled stretch of the calves, keeping the motion smooth and under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully re-engage the safety catches before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Performed on a leg press sled while reclined, this exercise isolates ankle plantarflexion by pressing through the balls of the feet to raise the sled using the calf muscles. It emphasizes a full range of motion from a stretch (heels lowered) to a strong contraction (heels lifted).

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at arm's length in front of your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Keep a slight bend in the knees (soft knees) and set a neutral spine — chest up, shoulders back and down — bracing your core.
Hinge at the hips by pushing your hips back while maintaining the neutral spine; allow the dumbbells to travel close to the front of your legs toward mid-shin level.

Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings or until the dumbbells reach just below the knees or mid-shin (depending on mobility), keeping the weights close to the legs and elbows straight.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive through your heels and thrust your hips forward to return to standing, squeezing the glutes at the top.
Breathe in while lowering and exhale while rising; maintain control and avoid rounding the lower back or letting the shoulders collapse forward.
Keep the dumbbells close to the body throughout, and adjust range of motion if you cannot maintain a neutral spine — reduce depth or use lighter weights.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge weight exercise using dumbbells to train the posterior chain by loading the glutes and hamstrings while the back and upper body stabilize the lift. The movement lowers the weights by bending at the hips with a slight knee bend and returns to standing by driving the hips forward.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
instructions: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
Place a barbell across your upper thighs just above the knees; use a pad if needed for comfort and grip the bar with both hands to steady it (seated position).
Position the balls of your feet on the floor (or a small raised platform) while keeping heels hanging or supported so your ankles can move through full range.
Keeping your torso upright and core engaged, exhale and press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.

Pause briefly at the top with the calves fully contracted, then inhale and slowly lower the heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves but maintain control (avoid letting the weight slam down).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; maintain a steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep knees stationary throughout the set.
To stop, carefully roll the barbell forward off your thighs or have a spotter help lift it off before standing.
exercises.description: Seated on a bench with a loaded barbell resting across the thighs, raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the calf muscles. The movement is performed with knees bent, emphasizing the soleus portion of the calves through controlled heel raises and lowers.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Split Squat
exercises.notes: Set a barbell in a rack at about upper-chest height, step under the bar and place it across your upper back/traps; grip the bar just outside shoulder width.
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack at about upper-chest height, step under the bar and place it across your upper back/traps; grip the bar just outside shoulder width.

Unrack the bar and take a staggered stance with one foot forward (front foot flat) and the other foot stepped back approximately 2–3 feet (rear foot on toes); stand tall with a neutral spine and braced abs.
Position weight mostly over the front foot and ensure the front knee tracks over the toes (avoid extreme forward collapse); keep your torso upright and chin neutral.
Slowly lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is approximately parallel to the floor and the rear knee is close to but not resting on the floor (a low split squat depth).
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the front leg, then press through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip, returning to the start position.
Perform the desired number of reps on one leg, then carefully rack the barbell (or switch legs and repeat) making sure to maintain balance and control throughout each rep.
Maintain even breathing (inhale as you lower, exhale as you drive up), keep the core braced, and avoid excessive forward lean or letting the front knee collapse inward.
exercises.description: A unilateral barbell leg exercise performed from a staggered (split) stance that emphasizes knee extension of the front leg to load the quadriceps while the rear leg provides balance and hip stability. The lifter lowers the hips until the rear knee approaches the floor, then drives back up through the front foot.

exercises.name: Sled Narrow Stance Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set the sled with an appropriate weight and sit on the sled seat in a supine position with your back and head pressed firmly against the back pad.
instructions: Set the sled with an appropriate weight and sit on the sled seat in a supine position with your back and head pressed firmly against the back pad.

Place your feet close together (narrow stance) near the middle of the foot platform about hip-width or slightly narrower, toes pointing forward or slightly outward; grip the side handles for stability.
Unlock the sled safety catch or release the lever so the sled is free to move, keeping knees slightly bent before starting the repetition.
Inhale, then push the platform away by extending your knees and hips, driving through the mid-foot and heels until legs are nearly straight—avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Exhale as you press; pause briefly at the top with knees almost extended but not locked.
Slowly lower the sled back by bending the knees under control until your knees are about 90 degrees or just above parallel to the chest, keeping your lower back and hips pressed against the pad.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining controlled tempo and proper alignment; after the set, re-engage the safety catch before stepping off the sled.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed with a narrow foot stance that emphasizes the quadriceps through a controlled pressing motion while lying supine on the angled sled. The narrow placement shifts emphasis to the quads while still recruiting glutes, hamstrings and calves for stability and drive.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back fully supported against the back pad (seated position) and grip the handles for stability.
Position the lower leg pad just above your heels / at the lower calf (behind the ankles) and secure the thigh pad across your front upper thighs to prevent hip movement.
Start with your legs nearly extended (knees close to straight but not locked) and feet pointed forward.
Exhale and curl your lower legs downward/back by flexing the knees, bringing the pad toward the underside of your thighs; contract the hamstrings and glutes at the peak.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, then inhale and slowly extend your knees to return the weight to the start position with controlled tempo—do not let the stack slam or lock the knees.
Keep your hips and torso stable against the pad throughout; avoid lifting the hips or using momentum to move the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and adjust weight if you cannot maintain smooth, controlled reps.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl performed on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to target the hamstrings while the seat/back pad stabilizes the hips. The movement curls the lower leg back toward the underside of the thigh and then returns under control to the start.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Landmine Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot; stand with feet hip-width apart facing the free end of the bar.
instructions: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot; stand with feet hip-width apart facing the free end of the bar.
Grasp the bar with both hands (overhand or one hand over/one under if preferred) a few inches below the bar sleeve so the load hangs in front of your thighs; keep the bar close to your legs.
Soften the knees with a small, fixed bend—do not squat—then brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly back to create a neutral spine.

Hinge at the hips by sending your hips back while maintaining the small knee bend; lower the bar in a straight path close to your shins until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings (roughly to mid-shin).
Keep the chest long, neck neutral, and spine flat throughout the descent — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing, keeping the bar close to the body.
Exhale as you stand up, maintain tension in the core and forearms for grip, and repeat for the desired reps while avoiding overextension of the lower back at the top.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed with a landmine bar that places primary emphasis on the hamstrings and posterior chain. It reinforces hip-hinge mechanics while challenging the glutes and lower back and requires a secure grip.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Leg Diagonal Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.

Stand upright facing the machine with the non-working foot planted on the floor (or on a small balance pad) and a slight bend in that knee; grasp the machine or hold a handle with one hand for light support and stabilization.
Shift your weight onto the planted leg, brace your abs to keep the torso stable and upright, and allow the working leg to hang straight behind you with toes pointed or slightly flexed.

Without bending the working knee, drive the leg straight back and diagonally across your body (slightly outward and behind), contracting the glute at the top of the movement; avoid arching the lower back or leaning the torso forward.
Pause briefly at full extension while squeezing the glute, then slowly return the leg to the starting position under control without letting the cable pull your torso out of alignment.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-leg diagonal kickbacks use a low-pulley ankle attachment to extend a straight working leg backward and slightly across the midline, emphasizing hip extension and glute contraction while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Side Squat
exercises.notes: Start standing in a wide stance (feet wider than shoulder-width) with toes pointed slightly outward while holding a dumbbell at chest level in a goblet grip.
instructions: Start standing in a wide stance (feet wider than shoulder-width) with toes pointed slightly outward while holding a dumbbell at chest level in a goblet grip.
Shift your weight onto the working leg (the side you will squat to) and keep the opposite leg relatively straight but foot flat and toes pointing outward for balance.

Brace your core, keep chest upright and shoulders back, then sit the hips back and bend the knee of the working leg, lowering into a deep side squat until the thigh is near-parallel to the floor or comfortable depth.
Ensure the working knee tracks over the toes (not caving inward) and keep the non-working leg extended with light tension through the foot and inner thigh.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining an upright torso, then press through the heel of the bent leg to extend the knee and return to the centered starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps on one side, then switch sides and perform an equal number of reps on the other leg. Breathe in while lowering and exhale while rising.
exercises.description: A wide-stance goblet squat variation where you shift your weight to one leg and lower into a lateral (side) squat to load the working thigh and inner thigh. It emphasizes knee-dominant strength on the bent leg while the opposite leg remains extended to stretch and stabilize.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, retract your shoulder blades and brace your core.
Step back from the rack and position your feet wider than shoulder-width with toes turned out about 30–45 degrees (wide/sumo stance).
Stand tall with weight evenly distributed through the midfoot and heels; keep a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, allowing the knees to track outward in line with your toes.
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while maintaining an upright torso and tight core.
Drive up through the heels and the inner edge of the feet, extending the hips and knees simultaneously to return to standing while exhaling.
Keep movements controlled, avoid collapsing the knees inward, and maintain a braced core and neutral spine throughout each rep.
Re-rack the bar carefully when finished by walking it forward into the rack and securing it.
exercises.description: A wide-stance back squat performed with a barbell across the upper back that emphasizes the quadriceps while increasing activation of the inner thighs (adductors) and glutes. Use a wider-than-shoulder stance with toes turned out to target the inner thigh more than a standard squat.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Wood Chopper
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at or slightly above shoulder height for a high-to-low wood chopper (or at/below hip height for the reverse direction).
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at or slightly above shoulder height for a high-to-low wood chopper (or at/below hip height for the reverse direction).
Stand sideways to the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart or in a staggered stance (outside foot slightly back) and knees slightly bent; grab the handle with both hands, arms extended.

Brace your core, keep a neutral spine, and pull the handle diagonally across your body toward the opposite hip by rotating your torso and hips as a single unit; allow your rear foot to pivot as you rotate.
Keep your arms mostly straight (a slight bend in the elbows) so the rotation is driven by the torso, not by pulling with the arms or shoulders.
Exhale as you rotate through the movement to the finish (handle near opposite hip), pause briefly while maintaining tension, then inhale and slowly return the cable to the start under control.
Perform all reps on one side, then switch sides to work the opposite obliques; maintain controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or excessive lumbar flexion/extension.
exercises.description: A standing cable wood chopper is a rotational core exercise where you pull a cable diagonally across your body, driving trunk rotation and anti-rotation through the obliques and overall core while the lower body stabilizes.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift to Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with feet hip-width apart and palms facing the body (neutral grip).

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Diagonal Kickback

exercises.name: Smith Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place two sturdy blocks or a raised platform under the bar so your heels can hang freely off the edge when standing on the platform.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).

workout.title: Leg Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Leg Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat to Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes pointing slightly out; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (rack position) with elbows under the weights and palms facing each other or forward.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes pointing slightly out; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (rack position) with elbows under the weights and palms facing each other or forward.

Brace your core, keep a neutral spine and chest up; inhale and begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering until your thighs are approximately parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobile).
Keep your knees tracking over your toes and weight distributed through your heels and midfoot; avoid letting the knees collapse inward.
Pause briefly at the bottom while keeping tension in the quads and glutes.
Exhale and drive through your heels to stand up explosively, extending the hips and knees until you reach full standing position.

As you complete the stand, press the dumbbells overhead by extending your elbows and driving through the shoulders until arms are fully extended overhead; finish with the weights roughly in line with your ears.
Control the descent: lower the dumbbells back to shoulder/rack position, then hinge the hips and descend into the next squat to begin the next rep.
Maintain a braced core and neutral spine throughout; breathe in on the descent (squat) and exhale as you stand and press. Use a weight that allows controlled movement and safe technique.
exercises.description: A compound standing movement that combines a dumbbell squat followed immediately by an overhead press, training the thighs while also working the shoulders and upper body. It builds lower-body strength and power while challenging core stability and pressing strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift to Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with feet hip-width apart and palms facing the body (neutral grip).
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with feet hip-width apart and palms facing the body (neutral grip).

Brace your abs, keep a slight bend in the knees, and hinge at the hips by pushing them back to lower the dumbbells along the front of your thighs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (torso will lean forward but keep a neutral spine).
Stop the descent when the dumbbells reach about mid-shin or when you can no longer maintain a flat back; shoulders should be packed and shoulder blades slightly retracted.

From this hinged position, initiate the bent-over row by pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs/hips, driving the elbows up and back and squeezing the shoulder blades together (keep torso stable and hips fixed).
Lower the dumbbells under control back to the hanging position at the end of the RDL, maintaining the hip hinge.
To return to standing, drive the hips forward while keeping the core braced and extend the hips until you are upright with the dumbbells at your sides.
Repeat the sequence: hinge (Romanian deadlift) then row, keeping movements smooth, spine neutral, and avoiding excessive rounding of the back or using momentum.
exercises.description: A compound combo that starts with a Romanian deadlift hip hinge to load the hamstrings and glutes, then transitions to a bent-over dumbbell row to work the lats and upper back. Maintains a neutral spine throughout while moving from the hinge to the rowing position.

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Diagonal Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
Stand facing the cable machine and place your hands on the frame or a handle at chest height for support; keep the non-working leg slightly bent and weight-bearing.
Hinge forward slightly at the hips so your torso is angled, brace your abs, and keep your spine neutral (avoid arching or rotating the torso).
Start with the working knee bent approximately 90° and the foot close to the floor; this is the start position.
Exhale and drive the working heel back and up in a diagonal line (backward and slightly outward/upward) by contracting the glute, keeping the knee bent throughout the motion.
Pause and squeeze the glute at the top of the movement while maintaining a neutral pelvis and preventing hip rotation.
Inhale and slowly return the leg back to the start position under control, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch legs. Keep movements smooth and controlled; avoid using momentum or excessive lumbar extension.
exercises.description: A cable-resisted donkey-style kick performed on a diagonal plane to emphasize the glute muscles (particularly the posterior and lateral fibers) while limiting lumbar involvement. The movement drives the heel/back of the foot up and back in a controlled diagonal arc against cable resistance.

exercises.name: Smith Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place two sturdy blocks or a raised platform under the bar so your heels can hang freely off the edge when standing on the platform.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place two sturdy blocks or a raised platform under the bar so your heels can hang freely off the edge when standing on the platform.

Load the bar with an appropriate weight and step under it, placing the bar across your upper back (traps/shoulders) or hold it securely with straight arms at hip level, feet hip-width apart on the platforms with toes pointing forward.
Stand tall with a neutral spine, knees straight but not locked (a slight bend), and shoulders back to stabilize the torso.
Begin the repetition by slowly lowering your heels down below the platform edge until you feel a stretch in the calves (eccentric phase).
Drive through the balls of your feet and raise your heels as high as possible, contracting the calves at the top (concentric phase); pause briefly and squeeze.
Lower your heels under control back to the stretched position; avoid bouncing or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and, when finished, carefully rack the bar and step down from the platforms.
exercises.description: Standing Smith machine calf raise performed on raised platforms to allow a full heel drop and a full plantarflexion at the top; primarily trains the calf muscles while the lower body stabilizers assist. The movement is done with the bar held on the shoulders or hands while the heels move up and down off the edge of the platform.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
Sit upright with feet under the roller pad, grip the side handles or seat edges for stability, and keep your chest lifted and core engaged.
Start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or the machine's starting position) and inhale to prepare.
Exhale and extend your legs by straightening the knees until they are almost fully extended — do not lock the knees at the top.
Pause briefly (0.5–1 second) at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until knees return to the initial bend.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding momentum or bouncing.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knees against a padded lever. The exercise focuses on controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering to target the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
instructions: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
Set the lower shin pad so it rests just above your ankles when seated; grip the handles or seat edges for stability.
Sit upright with your chest up, core engaged, and feet under the pad (seated position).
Exhale and extend your knees by pressing the pad forward with your lower shins until your legs are nearly straight; avoid hyperextending or locking the knees.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled motion until your knees return to approximately 90°.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping the torso stable and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever-style machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance while seated. The torso stays braced against the backpad and the lower shin pushes the pad forward until the knee is nearly straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat with Rack
exercises.notes: Set the rack height so the bar sits at about mid-chest when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the rack height so the bar sits at about mid-chest when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Step under the bar and position it low across the rear shoulders / lower trapezius (just below the top of the posterior delts), hands gripping the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar by straightening the legs, then take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack and plant your feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes turned out about 10–30 degrees.
Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine and upright chest with eyes forward; squeeze the shoulder blades and keep the elbows slightly down and back to create a solid shelf for the bar.
Initiate the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping knees tracking over the toes and weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Lower until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to your comfortable depth while keeping a neutral spine), maintaining the low-bar placement and tight torso throughout.
Drive upward by pressing through the heels/midfoot, extending the hips and knees together while keeping the chest up and core braced.
When standing tall at the top, stabilize the bar, step forward to the rack, and carefully re-rack the bar onto the rack supports.
exercises.description: A compound lower-body squat performed with the barbell set low across the rear deltoids/traps using a rack; it develops thigh strength while recruiting the posterior chain and core for stability. The low-bar position shifts some load onto the hips and upper back compared with a high-bar squat.

exercises.name: Barbell one leg hip thrust
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench and load a barbell with an appropriate weight; optionally use a pad on the bar for comfort.
instructions: Set up a flat bench and load a barbell with an appropriate weight; optionally use a pad on the bar for comfort.
Sit on the floor with your upper back (around the shoulder blade area) against the bench edge and the barbell positioned over your hips.

Roll the barbell onto your hips while leaning back so your shoulder blades remain supported on the bench; bend both knees and plant one foot flat on the floor while extending the other leg straight or slightly bent and held off the floor.
Grip the barbell on either side of your hips to stabilize it and brace your core (tighten your abs) and keep your chin slightly tucked.

Drive through the heel of the planted foot, extend the hip of the working leg, and lift your hips upward until your hips are in line with your torso and knee (forming a straight line from shoulders to knee).
Squeeze the glutes hard at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping the non-working leg off the floor and hips level (avoid rotating or dropping a hip).
Slowly lower your hips under control until your glutes are just above the floor (or until you feel a full stretch), then repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
Keep movements smooth and controlled, maintain core tension to protect the spine, and use a lighter weight if you cannot keep hips level or control the descent.
exercises.description: A single-leg variation of the barbell hip thrust performed with the upper back resting on a bench; the working leg drives the hips upward against the barbell to strongly activate the glutes. The non-working leg is kept off the floor to increase unilateral load and demand on hip extensors and core stabilization.

exercises.name: Smith Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place a stable raised platform or block under your feet so your heels can drop below the platform.
instructions: Set the Smith bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place a stable raised platform or block under your feet so your heels can drop below the platform.

Step under the bar and position it across your upper back/trapezius (or hold it at hip level if demonstrated), then unlock the Smith carriage and stand with feet hip-width apart and toes pointing forward on the platform edge.
Keep knees straight or with a very slight bend, chest up, and core engaged for a stable upright posture.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, fully plantarflexing the ankles and squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly, then inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level to achieve a full stretch in the calves.
Control the movement; avoid bouncing or using trunk momentum. Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the Smith bar when finished.
exercises.description: A standing calf raise performed on a Smith machine that increases resistance for plantarflexion to develop the gastrocnemius and soleus. The lifter rises onto the balls of the feet from a raised surface then lowers the heels below the platform for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Barbell Snatch Deadlift
exercises.notes: Setup: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width (or slightly wider for snatch-style), toes pointed slightly out, barbell on the floor over mid-foot.
instructions: Setup: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width (or slightly wider for snatch-style), toes pointed slightly out, barbell on the floor over mid-foot. Use a wide snatch grip (hands well outside knees) with a pronated grip.

Hinge and brace: Bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar — hips should be lower than a clean deadlift but higher than a full squat; keep chest up, spine neutral, shoulders slightly in front of the bar and eyes looking forward or slightly down.
Grip and tension: Grip the bar tightly with both hands, pull the slack out of the bar by taking a deep breath and bracing your core, engage lats to keep the bar close to your shins.
First pull: Drive through the heels by extending the knees and hips simultaneously, keeping the torso angle constant; focus on pushing the floor away rather than pulling with the arms.
Bar path and extension: Keep the bar close to the body as you extend the knees and hips to full extension, squeezing the glutes at the top and maintaining a tall chest and straight spine.
Lockout and lower: Do not hyperextend the lower back at lockout; control the descent by pushing the hips back, bending the knees, and lowering the bar back to the floor along the same path.
Breathing and reps: Exhale or hold a controlled breath at the top, reset tension between reps by re-bracing the core and lats before initiating the next pull.
Coaching cues and safety: Keep the bar close, avoid rounding the back, ensure knees track over toes, and use lighter weight when learning the wide snatch grip to protect the lower back and shoulders.
exercises.description: The barbell snatch deadlift is a wide-grip deadlift variation that trains the initial pull position for the snatch, emphasizing knee extension and quad drive while also loading the posterior chain and upper back. It uses a very wide (snatch) grip and a slightly lower hip position than a conventional deadlift.

exercises.name: Cable Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Setup: attach a single handle or straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the machine with feet hip‑width apart; grab the handle with both hands (overhand) and step back until there is light tension in the cable.
instructions: Setup: attach a single handle or straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the machine with feet hip‑width apart; grab the handle with both hands (overhand) and step back until there is light tension in the cable.
Start position: stand tall with a slight bend in the knees, shoulders down and back, chest lifted and core braced (standing, not kneeling or prone), arms extended holding the handle in front of you.

Descent (hip hinge): initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (not by bending the knees more), keeping a neutral spine and neutral head position; allow your torso to lower toward parallel to the floor until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings or until your torso is roughly parallel.
Maintain arm position: keep your arms long and relaxed (the cable provides the load) and avoid pulling with the arms or rounding the upper or lower back.
Ascent (return): drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings, returning to the upright start position while keeping the core engaged and avoiding hyperextension of the lower back.
Breathing and tempo: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward; control the descent and avoid bouncing at the bottom.

Form cues and safety: keep a slight bend in the knees throughout, maintain a neutral spine (do not round the back), keep the cable aligned with the midline of the body, and reduce weight if you cannot keep proper hip hinge mechanics.
exercises.description: A hip‑hinge variation of the Romanian deadlift using a low cable to load the posterior chain; emphasizes hamstring lengthening under tension while the cable provides constant forward pull. Performed standing, keeping a neutral spine and slight knee bend while driving the hips back and then forward.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
