exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lunge
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides, and core braced (neutral spine).
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides, and core braced (neutral spine).
Take a controlled step forward with one leg, landing heel-first and keeping the feet roughly hip-width apart for balance.
Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground (but not resting on it).
Keep the torso upright and chest lifted, shoulders back, and ensure the front knee tracks in line with the foot (avoid collapsing inward or letting the knee travel far past the toes).
Drive through the front heel to push yourself back up to the starting standing position, fully extending the front hip and knee.
Repeat for the desired number of reps on that leg, then switch legs; alternatively perform alternating forward steps (walking lunges).
Maintain controlled tempo, steady breathing, and avoid leaning forward or rounding the lower back during the descent and ascent.
exercises.description: A weighted forward lunge performed while holding a dumbbell in each hand; the movement primarily loads the quadriceps while the glutes and hamstrings assist the drive and the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Stand with feet hip-width, unrack the bar and take a small step forward so the bar is balanced; shift your weight onto one foot and lift the other foot slightly off the floor or cross it behind the working leg.
Keep the working knee slightly bent (not fully locked) and maintain an upright torso with core engaged.
Exhale and press through the ball of the working foot to rise up as high as possible onto your toes (heel raise), focusing on contracting the calf.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower slowly back down with control until your heel returns to the floor (or to neutral dorsiflexion), keeping balance throughout.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then carefully re-rack the bar if switching legs, or step back under the bar and repeat for the opposite leg.
Maintain steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep shoulders and hips squared forward to prevent compensations.
exercises.description: A unilateral standing calf raise performed inside a Smith machine with the bar resting on the upper back; raises the body onto the ball of one foot to load the calf muscles. Performed on the floor (not a raised step) for balance and single-leg strength and stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
Set a slight bend in the knees (soft knees) and brace your core to stabilize the spine before initiating the movement.

Hinge at the hips by pushing your hips straight back while keeping the spine neutral and chest slightly lifted; allow the dumbbells to slide down the front of your thighs and shins, staying close to the body.
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach mid‑shin (maintain neutral cervical alignment, do not round the lower back).
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward, squeezing the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the knees softly bent and the weights close to the legs.
Maintain controlled tempo (slow on the descent, controlled drive on the ascent), keep the shoulders down away from the ears, and avoid using the lower back to lift.
exercises.description: A standing hip‑hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings while the hips move back and forth; the movement also engages the glutes and core to maintain a neutral spine. Keep a slight knee bend and the weights close to the legs throughout.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Step onto a raised, stable platform or aerobic step so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can drop freely; stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width and toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a comfortable overhand grip to maintain control; engage your abs and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout (standing position).
Keep knees straight but not locked (a slight soft bend) and maintain weight balanced over the balls of your feet.
Exhale and raise your heels by plantarflexing at the ankle, lifting as high as possible onto your toes and pausing 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent — do not drop quickly or bounce.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and full range of motion. Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack if using heavy weight.
When finished, carefully step down from the platform and re-rack the bar in the squat rack.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed from a raised platform with a barbell across the upper back; rise onto the toes to concentrically contract the calves, then lower the heels below the platform for a full stretch. This is a standing, weight-bearing isolation exercise for the calves with upper-back and core stabilization.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out, holding a single dumbbell vertically at chest level (both hands cupping one end) — this is the goblet position.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out, holding a single dumbbell vertically at chest level (both hands cupping one end) — this is the goblet position.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine and chest lifted; elbows should point down between your knees as you move.
Initiate the movement by sending hips back and bending the knees, keeping weight distributed through the heels and mid-foot; push knees out in line with toes to avoid collapse.
Descend until your thighs are approximately parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while maintaining an upright torso and the dumbbell close to your chest.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then push through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to standing, keeping the core engaged and the dumbbell steady.
Repeat for the desired reps; inhale on the way down and exhale as you drive up. Avoid letting the heels rise or knees cave inward.
exercises.description: A goblet squat performed standing while holding a dumbbell at the chest; it emphasizes knee-driven squat movement to load the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.
instructions: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.
Find balance and keep the knee of the working leg straight or with a slight bend (do not lock); engage your abs to stabilize the torso and keep weight centered over the midfoot.
Slowly raise your heel by plantarflexing the ankle (push through the ball of the foot) until you reach maximum height on the toes; avoid leaning forward or using momentum.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calf, maintaining an upright posture and stable core.
Lower the heel slowly and with control until the sole is flat on the floor (or slightly below the step if performed on an elevated surface) to complete one repetition.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch legs; use a nearby wall or rail for light support if balance is a limiting factor but avoid pushing off with the support.
exercises.description: A single-leg calf raise performed standing while holding dumbbell(s) to add resistance; it isolates the calf (gastrocnemius/soleus) on the working leg while the opposite leg is off the ground. Balance and core stability are required to perform controlled raises and lowers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Squat
exercises.notes: Step 1: Set a flat bench behind you and load the barbell in a squat rack at about mid-chest height; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on the neck).
instructions: Step 1: Set a flat bench behind you and load the barbell in a squat rack at about mid-chest height; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on the neck).
Step 2: Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, lift the chest, brace your abs and unrack the bar by standing up, then step back 1–2 foot lengths until the bench is directly behind you.
Step 3: Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core throughout.
Step 4: Begin the descent by pushing your hips back slightly and bending the knees, keeping weight through the midfoot/heels and knees tracking over your toes.
Step 5: Lower under control until your glutes lightly touch the bench (brief contact only) — do not fully sit or relax on the bench.
Step 6: Drive through the heels and extend the hips and knees to return to standing, exhaling as you rise and keeping the chest up.
Step 7: Step forward to re-rack the bar safely when finished; use a spotter for heavy loads and avoid bouncing off the bench or rounding the lower back.
exercises.description: A bench (box) squat variation performed with a barbell across the upper back where you sit back to a bench to control depth, emphasizing the quadriceps while also engaging the posterior chain and core for stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip‑width apart and toes pointing forward.
Set a slight bend in the knees (soft knees) and brace your core to stabilize the spine before initiating the movement.

Hinge at the hips by pushing your hips straight back while keeping the spine neutral and chest slightly lifted; allow the dumbbells to slide down the front of your thighs and shins, staying close to the body.
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach mid‑shin (maintain neutral cervical alignment, do not round the lower back).
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward, squeezing the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the knees softly bent and the weights close to the legs.
Maintain controlled tempo (slow on the descent, controlled drive on the ascent), keep the shoulders down away from the ears, and avoid using the lower back to lift.
exercises.description: A standing hip‑hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings while the hips move back and forth; the movement also engages the glutes and core to maintain a neutral spine. Keep a slight knee bend and the weights close to the legs throughout.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred), feet hip-width apart.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred), feet hip-width apart.

Shift your weight onto one foot and lift the opposite foot slightly off the floor so you are balancing on a single leg (standing position, single-leg). Keep the supporting knee straight but not locked and torso tall.
Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine; if needed lightly touch a wall or hold a sturdy object for balance (wall-assisted) without leaning.

Slowly raise your heel by plantarflexing the ankle of the supporting leg until you are on the ball of your foot/toes, squeezing the calf at the top. Keep shoulders relaxed and dumbbells at your sides.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top while maintaining balance and control.
Lower your heel slowly back to the starting position until the sole is flat on the floor (or slightly below the step edge if using a raised surface for increased range of motion).
Complete the desired number of repetitions on that leg, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A single-leg standing calf raise with dumbbells isolates the calf (plantarflexors) on one side while challenging balance and stability. It builds strength and size in the calves while the core, grip, and leg stabilizers assist.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat with Rack
exercises.notes: Set the rack height so the bar sits at about mid-chest when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the rack height so the bar sits at about mid-chest when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Step under the bar and position it low across the rear shoulders / lower trapezius (just below the top of the posterior delts), hands gripping the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar by straightening the legs, then take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack and plant your feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes turned out about 10–30 degrees.
Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine and upright chest with eyes forward; squeeze the shoulder blades and keep the elbows slightly down and back to create a solid shelf for the bar.
Initiate the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping knees tracking over the toes and weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Lower until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to your comfortable depth while keeping a neutral spine), maintaining the low-bar placement and tight torso throughout.
Drive upward by pressing through the heels/midfoot, extending the hips and knees together while keeping the chest up and core braced.
When standing tall at the top, stabilize the bar, step forward to the rack, and carefully re-rack the bar onto the rack supports.
exercises.description: A compound lower-body squat performed with the barbell set low across the rear deltoids/traps using a rack; it develops thigh strength while recruiting the posterior chain and core for stability. The low-bar position shifts some load onto the hips and upper back compared with a high-bar squat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; feet about hip-width apart.
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; feet about hip-width apart.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip, arms relaxed; optionally lightly touch a wall or railing for balance (wall-assisted if needed).
Keep the torso upright, chest lifted and gaze forward; maintain a slight bend in the knees (do not lock them).
Inhale, then exhale and contract your calves to raise your heels as high as possible until you are standing on your toes; keep weight evenly distributed across the forefoot.
Pause at the top for 1–2 seconds, focusing on a full contraction of the calves while maintaining core tension.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels below the level of the step until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, controlling both the concentric (raise) and eccentric (lower) phases and breathing rhythmically.
exercises.description: A weighted standing calf raise performed on the edge of a step to load the calf muscles through a full plantarflexion and stretch. Dumbbells add resistance and grip demand while the core and glutes stabilize the body.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Clean
exercises.notes: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position).
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back while maintaining a flat back, allowing a slight bend in the knees so the dumbbells hang at mid-shin (hip-hinge position).

Explosively extend the hips and knees (triple extension) by driving through the feet and forcefully straightening the body; simultaneously shrug the shoulders and pull the dumbbells upward close to the body.

As the dumbbells reach chest height, quickly rotate the elbows forward and under the weights so you 'catch' them against the front of your shoulders in a racked position (partial squat catch is optional).

Absorb the load by slightly bending the knees into a shallow squat if needed, then stand fully upright to complete the rep with the dumbbells resting at the shoulders (upright standing racked position).

Lower the dumbbells back to the hanging position in a controlled manner by reversing the motion: push the hips back, bend the knees, and let the weights return to mid-shin before starting the next rep.
Keep the core engaged, chest up, wrists neutral, and maintain the dumbbells close to the body throughout to protect the lower back and maximize efficiency.
exercises.description: A dynamic compound lift where dumbbells are explosively pulled from a hip-hinge to a racked position at the shoulders, using a quick leg drive and shrug. Trains power production through the hips and legs while also recruiting upper-back and shoulder muscles to receive the load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright in a split stance with one foot forward and the other foot stepped back (rear foot on the toes).
instructions: Stand upright in a split stance with one foot forward and the other foot stepped back (rear foot on the toes). Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, arms straight and palms facing the body.
Brace your core and keep your torso tall and neutral (avoid excessive forward lean). Position feet so that when you lower, the front knee tracks over the front toes without collapsing inward.

Begin the descent by bending both knees, lowering your hips straight down until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor or the front knee reaches about 90 degrees. Allow the rear knee to move toward the floor but not to rest on it.
Keep weight distributed through the front heel and midfoot; maintain even balance and a stable pelvis throughout the movement.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining an upright torso and engaged core.
Drive through the front heel to straighten the front leg and return to the starting upright split stance. Keep the movement controlled; do not lock the knee at the top.
Repeat for desired repetitions, then switch legs and perform the same number on the opposite side.
exercises.description: A single-leg, stationary lunge performed with dumbbells to load the front leg; it emphasizes the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings for hip stability. The movement is done in a split stance with a controlled descent and drive through the front heel to return to standing.

exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; feet about hip-width apart.
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; feet about hip-width apart.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip, arms relaxed; optionally lightly touch a wall or railing for balance (wall-assisted if needed).
Keep the torso upright, chest lifted and gaze forward; maintain a slight bend in the knees (do not lock them).
Inhale, then exhale and contract your calves to raise your heels as high as possible until you are standing on your toes; keep weight evenly distributed across the forefoot.
Pause at the top for 1–2 seconds, focusing on a full contraction of the calves while maintaining core tension.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels below the level of the step until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, controlling both the concentric (raise) and eccentric (lower) phases and breathing rhythmically.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Wide Stance Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up the sled leg press: load appropriate weight, sit supine with your back fully supported on the 45-degree sled back pad, and grasp the side handles for stability.
instructions: Set up the sled leg press: load appropriate weight, sit supine with your back fully supported on the 45-degree sled back pad, and grasp the side handles for stability.

Place your feet high enough on the footplate with a wide stance (feet wider than shoulder-width) and toes slightly pointed outward to bias the glutes and adductors while keeping the knees tracking over the toes.
Release the safety latch or pins so the sled can move freely, keeping control with your hands on the handles.

Inhale and slowly lower the sled by bending at the knees and hips, lowering until your thighs are roughly parallel to the footplate or until you feel a comfortable stretch in the quads and glutes (avoid letting the lower back lift off the pad).
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the legs and ensuring knees remain aligned with the feet (do not let knees collapse inward).
Exhale and press through your heels and midfoot to extend the knees and hips, driving the sled back up to near full extension without locking out the knees.
Control the return motion and repeat for the desired number of reps, then carefully re-engage the safety latch before exiting the sled.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed with a wide foot placement to emphasize the quadriceps while engaging the glutes and inner thighs. The exerciser pushes the sled away by extending the knees and hips from a reclined (back-supported) position.

exercises.name: Smith Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set up the Smith machine: position safety stops at or just below mid-shin and load the desired weight; stand with feet roughly hip-width apart under the bar so the bar is over the mid-foot.
instructions: Set up the Smith machine: position safety stops at or just below mid-shin and load the desired weight; stand with feet roughly hip-width apart under the bar so the bar is over the mid-foot.

Stand facing the bar (upright standing position), bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar, keeping your chest up and spine neutral; grip the bar just outside the legs with a double overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull shoulders down and back (retract traps slightly) and ensure the bar is close to your shins with shoulders slightly in front of the bar before initiating the lift.
Drive through the heels by extending the hips and knees together, pushing the floor away while keeping a strong hip hinge; keep the bar close to the body along the fixed vertical path.
Continue to extend until hips are fully locked out with hips forward and chest upright; avoid hyperextending the spine at the top.
To lower, hinge at the hips first and push them back while maintaining a neutral spine, then bend the knees once the bar passes the knees and lower it under control to the starting position.
Breathe: inhale and brace before each rep, exhale at or after the top of the lift; use controlled tempo and avoid rounding the back.
Use the Smith machine safety stops for sets to limit range if needed and reduce weight if you cannot maintain neutral spine or bar path.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift variation performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the hip hinge to train the glutes and posterior chain while the fixed bar path adds stability and control. It develops hip extension strength and reinforces safe bar path for beginners or heavier loads.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Sled Calf Press On Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up the leg press sled with an appropriate weight and unlock the safety catches.
instructions: Set up the leg press sled with an appropriate weight and unlock the safety catches.
Sit reclined with your back and head firmly against the pad (supine/reclined position) and place the balls of your feet and toes on the lower edge of the footplate so your heels can hang freely.
Keep your knees slightly bent (do not lock) and grip the handles or sides of the seat for stability.
Inhale, then exhale and press through the balls of your feet to lift the sled by plantarflexing your ankles, raising your heels as high as comfortable.
Pause briefly at the top for a full contraction of the calves.
Slowly lower your heels back down below the level of the footplate to achieve a controlled stretch of the calves, keeping the motion smooth and under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully re-engage the safety catches before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Performed on a leg press sled while reclined, this exercise isolates ankle plantarflexion by pressing through the balls of the feet to raise the sled using the calf muscles. It emphasizes a full range of motion from a stretch (heels lowered) to a strong contraction (heels lifted).

exercises.name: Lever Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with hips and pelvis firmly against the pad, one foot hooked under the padded lever and the other leg extended and relaxed.
Grasp the handles or edge of the bench for stability and brace your core to prevent your hips from lifting.
Start with the working leg nearly straight (slight natural bend in the knee) and exhale as you flex the knee to curl the heel toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a full hamstring contraction without excessive hip extension.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position, fully controlling the eccentric phase without letting the weight slam down.
Keep the non-working leg relaxed and hips pressed into the bench; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips to cheat the movement.
Complete the desired reps, then switch legs and repeat for the other side.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) single-leg curl performed on a lever/lying leg curl machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee to bring the heel toward the glutes. Movement emphasizes a controlled concentric curl and a slow eccentric return while keeping the hips pinned to the bench.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.
instructions: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.
Find balance and keep the knee of the working leg straight or with a slight bend (do not lock); engage your abs to stabilize the torso and keep weight centered over the midfoot.
Slowly raise your heel by plantarflexing the ankle (push through the ball of the foot) until you reach maximum height on the toes; avoid leaning forward or using momentum.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calf, maintaining an upright posture and stable core.
Lower the heel slowly and with control until the sole is flat on the floor (or slightly below the step if performed on an elevated surface) to complete one repetition.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch legs; use a nearby wall or rail for light support if balance is a limiting factor but avoid pushing off with the support.
exercises.description: A single-leg calf raise performed standing while holding dumbbell(s) to add resistance; it isolates the calf (gastrocnemius/soleus) on the working leg while the opposite leg is off the ground. Balance and core stability are required to perform controlled raises and lowers.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
instructions: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
Grip the side handles and ensure your lower back and hips are pressed firmly against the backpad; unlock the sled safety/locks if present.
Start position: Bend your knees to bring your thighs toward your chest until knees are about 90 degrees, keeping feet flat on the plate and knees tracking over toes.
Press: Exhale and drive through your heels and mid-foot to extend your knees and hips, pushing the footplate away until legs are nearly straight but do not lock the knees at full extension.
Return: Inhale and slowly control the sled back down by bending the knees, lowering the weight under tension until your knees reach about 90 degrees again.
Repetitions: Repeat for the desired number of reps with controlled tempo; avoid bouncing at the bottom or using momentum to lock the knees.
Safety cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the pad throughout, avoid lifting hips off the pad, and keep knees aligned with feet. Rerack/lock the sled before exiting.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed supine on an inclined sled platform where you push the footplate away using the legs. The movement primarily extends the knees to target the quadriceps while the glutes, hamstrings, and calves assist and stabilize.

exercises.name: Lever Hip Thrust (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the machine to an appropriate weight and sit with your upper back firmly against the backrest (semi-reclined).
instructions: Set the machine to an appropriate weight and sit with your upper back firmly against the backrest (semi-reclined).
Position the padded roller directly over your hips and place your feet flat on the foot platform about hip-width apart, knees bent.
Begin with your hips lowered so your thighs are angled toward the platform (starting position). Grip the handles for stability.
Drive through your heels and contract your glutes to extend your hips upward until your hips are in line with your shoulders and knees (full hip extension).
Squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping your chin tucked and spine neutral; avoid hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the lever in a controlled manner back to the starting position, allowing the hips to descend fully while maintaining tension in the glutes.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the concentric (hip raise) and in on the eccentric (lowering) phase.
exercises.description: Plate-loaded lever hip thrust performed from a seated/reclined position to drive the hips upward against resistance, primarily targeting the glute muscles. Feet are planted on a foot platform while a padded lever rests on the hips and the upper back is supported by the backrest.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Angled Leg Press
exercises.notes: Disengage the safety/locks while keeping control of the sled with your hands on the handles.
instructions: Position yourself supine on the angled leg press sled with your back and head fully supported against the pad; grip the side handles for stability.

Place your feet about shoulder-width on the platform (mid-foot placement); toes slightly pointed up so heels remain contact points. Adjust feet higher on the platform to emphasize glutes/hamstrings or lower to emphasize quads.
Disengage the safety/locks while keeping control of the sled with your hands on the handles.
Start with knees bent to roughly 90 degrees (or a comfortable depth), feet flat on the platform and core braced to keep the lower back pressed into the pad.
Exhale and press the platform away by extending the knees, driving through the heels and mid-foot until legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the legs, then inhale and slowly lower the platform by bending the knees under control back toward the starting position.
Keep knees tracking over your toes and prevent them from caving inward; maintain a neutral spine and avoid lifting your hips off the pad.
After completing the set, re-engage the safety/locks before releasing your grip and exiting the sled.
exercises.description: A supine, angled sled leg press that primarily targets the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and calves. Performed by pressing the platform away with the feet while the back remains supported on the angled seat.

exercises.name: Landmine Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot and loading the other end with the desired weight; grasp the bar with both hands close to the plates or use a V-handle attachment.
instructions: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot and loading the other end with the desired weight; grasp the bar with both hands close to the plates or use a V-handle attachment.
Stand facing the bar with a wide (sumo) stance: feet wider than shoulder-width, toes turned slightly outward and weight distributed through the heels and midfoot.
Hold the bar close to your body at chest level with elbows down and a neutral spine; brace your core (abs) and keep chest tall and head neutral.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, letting them track outward over your toes; descend until your thighs are about parallel to the floor or a comfortable depth with no lumbar rounding.
Keep the bar close, maintain an upright torso, and feel tension in the glutes and adductors throughout the descent.
Drive back up by pressing through the heels and pushing your knees out, extending the hips and knees to stand while maintaining core bracing and chest position.
Control the bar on the way down for each rep; exhale as you drive up and inhale as you descend.
Maintain knee alignment (tracking toes), neutral spine, and avoid collapsing the knees inward or letting the torso fold forward.
exercises.description: A wide-stance squat performed with a landmine bar held in front of the body; the sumo stance shifts emphasis to the glutes and inner thighs while the landmine setup helps maintain an upright torso. It builds hip extension strength and targets adductors and quads as you drive up from the bottom position.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Step onto a raised, stable platform or aerobic step so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can drop freely; stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width and toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a comfortable overhand grip to maintain control; engage your abs and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout (standing position).
Keep knees straight but not locked (a slight soft bend) and maintain weight balanced over the balls of your feet.
Exhale and raise your heels by plantarflexing at the ankle, lifting as high as possible onto your toes and pausing 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent — do not drop quickly or bounce.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and full range of motion. Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack if using heavy weight.
When finished, carefully step down from the platform and re-rack the bar in the squat rack.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed from a raised platform with a barbell across the upper back; rise onto the toes to concentrically contract the calves, then lower the heels below the platform for a full stretch. This is a standing, weight-bearing isolation exercise for the calves with upper-back and core stabilization.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

exercises.name: Lever Seated One Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.

Sit upright with your back pressed firmly against the back pad, chest up, and grasp the handles or seat grips for stability; position the non-working foot flat on the floor or on the machine footrest.
Start with the working leg extended (knee nearly straight) and the ankle neutral; this is the starting position.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knee, driving the heel toward the underside of your glute while keeping the thigh and hips stationary against the seat.
Pause briefly at the top of the contraction and squeeze the hamstrings; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips.
Inhale and slowly return the lower leg to the starting extended position under control, resisting the weight on the way down.
Complete all reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg. Maintain a controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Seated single‑leg curl performed on a lever machine to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to correct imbalances and increase muscle activation.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean grip Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set up the barbell in a front-rack position using a clean grip: hands just outside shoulders, fingers under the bar, elbows lifted so upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor; stand with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
instructions: Set up the barbell in a front-rack position using a clean grip: hands just outside shoulders, fingers under the bar, elbows lifted so upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor; stand with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core and keep chest up and back straight (neutral spine); eyes forward and shoulders high to support the bar on the front deltoids/upper chest.
Inhale, initiate the movement by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously, pushing the knees outward to track over the toes and keeping the elbows up so the bar does not roll forward.
Descend under control until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to a comfortable depth while maintaining an upright torso), keeping weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining core tension and a proud chest, then drive the feet into the ground (focus on heels) to extend the knees and hips and return to standing.
Exhale as you stand up, fully extending hips and knees while keeping elbows high and the bar stable on your shoulders; reset breathing and posture before the next rep.

Keep repetitions controlled and avoid letting the torso collapse forward, the elbows drop, or the knees cave inward; if wrist discomfort occurs with the clean grip, adjust hand position or use cross-arm/front-rack variation.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat performed with a clean grip (bar rests on the front shoulders, elbows high) that emphasizes knee extension to target the quadriceps while also engaging the posterior chain and core for stability. Maintain an upright torso and high elbows throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Stiff Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, dumbbells held in front of thighs with a neutral grip; keep a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff‑leg position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, dumbbells held in front of thighs with a neutral grip; keep a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff‑leg position).
Brace your core and retract the shoulders slightly to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them straight back while keeping the dumbbells close to your legs; allow the torso to come forward until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (usually to mid‑shin).
Keep the back flat (avoid rounding) and maintain the slight knee bend; eyes should be looking a few feet ahead to help keep the spine neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom when you feel a firm hamstring stretch, then drive the hips forward by contracting the hamstrings and glutes to return to standing.
Squeeze the glutes at the top and avoid leaning back or hyperextending the spine.
Breathe in while lowering, exhale as you drive the hips forward; maintain controlled tempo and a firm grip (forearms engaged) throughout.
exercises.description: A hinge-style deadlift performed with minimal knee bend to load the posterior chain, primarily targeting the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back. Dumbbells are lowered along the legs by driving the hips back and then the hips are thrust forward to return to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
instructions: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
Position your feet much wider than shoulder-width with toes pointed out at ~30–45 degrees (sumo stance).
Brace your core, keep chest tall and a neutral spine, and allow a slight forward torso lean while maintaining an upright upper body.

Begin the movement by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, lowering yourself into a squat until your thighs are roughly parallel to the platform or a comfortable depth where knees track over toes.
Keep your knees tracking in line with your toes and distribute weight through your heels and mid-foot; avoid collapsing the knees inward.
Drive forcefully through the heels and hips to return to standing, fully extending the hips at the top while resisting the lever’s load throughout the motion.
Control the descent on each rep, breathe in on the way down and exhale as you drive up, and perform repetitions with a steady tempo.
exercises.description: A wide-stance (sumo) squat performed with a lever belt/harness attached to a machine; emphasizes hip drive and inner-thigh engagement while reducing load on the spine. Use a wide, turned-out foot position and sit back between the heels, then drive up through the hips.

exercises.name: Barbell Lunge
exercises.notes: Position a loaded barbell in a rack at upper‑chest height, step under it and place it across your upper back/traps, hands slightly wider than shoulder width with a firm overhand grip; unrack the bar and take one or two steps back to a stable, shoulder‑width starting stance (standing).
instructions: Position a loaded barbell in a rack at upper‑chest height, step under it and place it across your upper back/traps, hands slightly wider than shoulder width with a firm overhand grip; unrack the bar and take one or two steps back to a stable, shoulder‑width starting stance (standing).
Brace your abs and keep a neutral spine and chest up; feet should be hip to shoulder width for balance and toes pointing forward.
Take a controlled long step forward with one foot (forward lunge), planting the front foot flat while keeping weight centered over the midfoot to heel.

Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor and the rear knee is close to the ground (approximately 90° at the front knee); keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid excessive forward collapse of the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip and return to the starting standing position.
Repeat for the desired reps, alternating legs each repetition or complete all reps on one side before switching; breathe in while lowering and exhale while driving up.
Maintain controlled tempo, core bracing, and even foot placement each rep; use a power rack or spotter for safety when loading heavy.
exercises.description: A weighted forward lunge performed with a barbell resting on the upper back that emphasizes single‑leg knee extension to work the front thigh while also engaging the hips and core for stability. Each repetition involves stepping forward, lowering until the front thigh is near parallel to the floor, then driving back to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your knees are roughly 90 degrees and your hips are supported while seated (seated position).
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your knees are roughly 90 degrees and your hips are supported while seated (seated position).
Select an appropriate weight and ensure the machine's safety catch or lever is engaged before sitting.
Place the balls of both feet shoulder-width on the footplate with toes pointing forward; heels should hang slightly to allow a full range of motion.
Grip the side handles or seat handles to stabilize the torso and unlock the safety if applicable.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet, driving the footplate away by extending your ankles into full plantarflexion (raise your heels as high as possible).
Pause briefly at the top for a strong contraction in the calves, then inhale and slowly lower your heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves (controlled eccentric phase).
Repeat for desired reps. After finishing, lock the safety catch before removing your feet from the footplate.
exercises.description: Seated calf press performed on a lever machine targets the calf muscles by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while seated with knees bent. It isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus complex for focused calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Weighted Chains Bench Squat
exercises.notes: Set a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; place a barbell with chains across your upper traps (barbell resting on the shoulders, not the neck).

exercises.name: Barbell Weighted Chains Bench Squat
exercises.notes: Set a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; place a barbell with chains across your upper traps (barbell resting on the shoulders, not the neck).
instructions: Set a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; place a barbell with chains across your upper traps (barbell resting on the shoulders, not the neck).
Stand under the bar in a rack, grip the bar just outside shoulder width, take a deep breath and brace your abs and spine before unracking.
Step the bar out of the rack and take 1–2 controlled steps back until you are standing with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes angled slightly outwards; keep the bench directly behind you.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, keeping your chest up and spine neutral; keep knees tracking over your toes and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Lower in a controlled manner until your glutes lightly contact the bench (do not fully sit or relax on the bench; maintain tension), ensuring thighs reach about parallel to the floor.

Pause briefly on the bench (no bouncing), then drive through your heels and mid-foot, extend hips and knees to stand up explosively while exhaling; allow the chains to rise and add resistance as you near lockout.
At the top, fully extend hips and knees while maintaining a braced core and neutral spine; reset your breath as needed between reps.
After completing the set, carefully walk the bar forward and re-rack it in the squat rack.

Coaching notes: keep the bar on the upper traps (not high on the neck), avoid letting knees collapse inward, control the descent to protect knees and lower back, and adjust bench height or foot position to maintain proper depth and comfort.
exercises.description: A barbell back squat performed to a bench with chains added for accommodating resistance; this bench-squat variation emphasizes the quadriceps while using the bench as a depth cue. Chains increase load as you rise, encouraging stronger lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower into a squat-like setup (upright torso, hips lower than a conventional deadlift) and grip the bar with hands inside your knees using a double overhand or mixed grip.
instructions: Stand with feet well outside shoulder width (sumo stance), toes pointed slightly outward; position the bar over the mid-foot while standing upright.

Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower into a squat-like setup (upright torso, hips lower than a conventional deadlift) and grip the bar with hands inside your knees using a double overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine (no rounded back); pull your shoulder blades down and back so the lats are engaged and chest is up.
Drive your knees out so they track over your toes (toes and knees aligned) to engage the adductors and maintain the wide stance.
Initiate the lift by pushing through the heels and mid-foot, driving the hips forward and straightening the legs simultaneously—keep the bar close to the shins.
Stand tall at lockout by fully extending the hips and knees, squeezing the glutes and keeping the shoulders back (avoid hyperextending the lower back).
Lower the bar under control by hinging at the hips first, then bending the knees to return the bar to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine and tight core.
Reset your position between reps as needed (re-brace core, realign knees over toes, and ensure bar remains over mid-foot) before initiating the next repetition.
exercises.description: The barbell sumo deadlift is a wide-stance deadlift variation that emphasizes hip extension and inner-thigh drive to lift the bar from the floor. It uses a more upright torso and wider foot placement to shift load onto the glutes and adductors while still recruiting the posterior chain and legs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Step-up
exercises.notes: Stand sideways beside a bench with feet roughly hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (neutral grip).
instructions: Stand sideways beside a bench with feet roughly hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (neutral grip).
Position the foot closest to the bench flat on the center of the bench so your knee tracks over your toes; the other foot remains on the floor.
Brace your abs, keep your chest upright and core tight, then drive through the heel of the elevated foot to extend the knee and hip and lift your body up onto the bench.
Stand tall on the bench, squeezing the quads and glutes at the top; avoid rotating or leaning the torso away from the bench.
Control the descent: step the trailing foot back down to the floor first or lower the lead foot down with control so the same side returns to the start position.
Repeat for the prescribed reps on one side, then switch to the other leg and perform the same number of reps.
exercises.description: A single‑leg step-up performed sideways onto a bench while holding dumbbells, emphasizing knee and hip extension of the stepping leg to raise the body laterally. It develops unilateral leg strength, balance and hip stability with emphasis on the thighs and glutes.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with hips and pelvis firmly against the pad, one foot hooked under the padded lever and the other leg extended and relaxed.
Grasp the handles or edge of the bench for stability and brace your core to prevent your hips from lifting.
Start with the working leg nearly straight (slight natural bend in the knee) and exhale as you flex the knee to curl the heel toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a full hamstring contraction without excessive hip extension.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position, fully controlling the eccentric phase without letting the weight slam down.
Keep the non-working leg relaxed and hips pressed into the bench; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips to cheat the movement.
Complete the desired reps, then switch legs and repeat for the other side.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) single-leg curl performed on a lever/lying leg curl machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee to bring the heel toward the glutes. Movement emphasizes a controlled concentric curl and a slow eccentric return while keeping the hips pinned to the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
