exercises.name: Barbell Hang Clean
exercises.notes: Hinge slightly at the hips and bend the knees to set a strong athletic posture: neutral spine, chest up, eyes forward and core braced.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell held at thigh/upper-thigh level (hang position) using a slightly wider-than-shoulder grip, wrists straight and elbows pointed down.
Hinge slightly at the hips and bend the knees to set a strong athletic posture: neutral spine, chest up, eyes forward and core braced.
Initiate the move by forcefully extending the hips and knees (triple extension) and pushing through the midfoot and forefoot to generate upward momentum on the bar.
As the bar rises, shrug the shoulders aggressively and pull the elbows high and out to accelerate the bar close to the body; keep the bar path vertical and close.

Explosively pull yourself under the bar by quickly bending the knees and rotating the elbows under so the bar is received on the front of the shoulders (front-rack) with the elbows high and upper arms roughly parallel to the floor.

Catch the bar in a partial squat or quarter squat (depending on load and intent) with an upright torso, controlled breath, and firm grip; ensure wrist and elbow position supports the bar on the shoulders.
Stand up fully from the catch by extending hips and knees to finish the repetition with hips locked out and shoulders stacked over hips.
Lower the bar in a controlled manner back to the hang position (upper-thigh), reset posture and breathing, then repeat for the desired reps.
Use lighter loads to practice timing and technique before increasing weight; keep the spine neutral throughout and avoid pulling with the arms only.
exercises.description: The barbell hang clean is a power, compound lift performed from a standing hip/upper-thigh position that uses an explosive hip and knee extension to drive the bar upward, followed by pulling yourself under the bar and catching it in a front-rack position. It trains lower-body power, posterior chain drive, upper-back shrugging and a quick catch in the front of the shoulders.

exercises.name: Lever Alternate Leg Press (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the machine appropriately and lock the sled/lever into the start position; grasp the side handles for stability.
instructions: Sit on the lever leg press with your back fully supported and feet placed hip-width on the footplate; adjust the seat so knees start at about a 90-degree angle.
Load the machine appropriately and lock the sled/lever into the start position; grasp the side handles for stability.
Place one foot flat and centered on the footplate (working leg) and keep the other foot off the plate or lightly rested without pushing.
Unlock the sled and press through the heel and mid-foot of the working leg, extending the knee until the leg is almost straight — do not fully lock the knee.
Pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the sled by bending the knee until you return to the starting ~90-degree knee angle.
Keep your lower back pressed into the pad, avoid rotating the hips, and maintain a steady tempo; exhale on the concentric (press) and inhale on the eccentric (return).
Complete the prescribed reps on one leg, re-lock the sled if needed, then switch and perform the same number of reps with the opposite leg.
exercises.description: Seated single-leg (alternate) press on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes knee extension to target the quadriceps while the glutes and hamstrings assist. Performed one leg at a time with the back supported against the seat.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Squat Calf Raise on Leg Press Machine
exercises.notes: Set the lever/leg-press machine to a comfortable weight and sit with your back pressed into the seat (seated/squat position).
instructions: Set the lever/leg-press machine to a comfortable weight and sit with your back pressed into the seat (seated/squat position). Grip the side handles for stability.
Place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the footplate, feet about hip-width and toes pointing forward; knees should be bent (approximately 90°) throughout the exercise.
Unlock the machine safety if applicable and allow your heels to lower until you feel a stretch in the calves — this is the start position.
Exhale and contract the calves to raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet, lifting as high as possible without extending or locking the knees; pause 1 second at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back toward the start position under control until you feel a stretch in the calves; avoid bouncing.
Maintain a stable torso and fixed knee angle throughout the set so movement comes primarily from the ankle joint.
After completing the set, lock or re-engage the machine safety before releasing the handles and exiting the seat.
exercises.description: A seated, squat-style calf raise performed on a leg-press/lever machine where the user plantarflexes the ankles against the footplate to target the calf muscles while the hips and knees remain in a flexed (seated/squat) position.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart with knees bent so shins are near vertical; grip the barbell at the sides to steady it.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back (shoulder blades) against a flat bench and roll the barbell over your legs so it rests in the hip crease; use padding on the bar for comfort.
Plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart with knees bent so shins are near vertical; grip the barbell at the sides to steady it.
Brace your core, press through your heels and drive your hips upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line; actively squeeze the glutes at the top.
Hold the top briefly while maintaining a neutral spine and avoiding lumbar hyperextension (keep ribs down and chin slightly tucked).
Lower your hips under control until they are just above the floor (or until a full comfortable range) and immediately begin the next rep.
Adjust foot placement as needed (closer to mid-foot emphasizes glutes, higher placement can increase hamstring involvement) and use a spotter when using heavy loads.
exercises.description: A bench-supported hip extension where a loaded barbell is driven by the hips to target glute strength and hypertrophy while the upper back rests on a bench. The movement emphasizes powerful horizontal hip extension with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a front-rack position on a squat rack at about collarbone height; approach the bar and step under it so it rests on the anterior deltoids and across the clavicles in a cross-arm or clean grip.
instructions: Set the barbell in a front-rack position on a squat rack at about collarbone height; approach the bar and step under it so it rests on the anterior deltoids and across the clavicles in a cross-arm or clean grip.
Establish your grip (cross-arm shown in video) and lift the chest: elbows high and pointed forward to create a stable shelf for the bar; keep the upper back tight and shoulders engaged.
Unrack the bar by standing up and taking 1-2 steps back; position feet shoulder-width apart with toes turned slightly outward and weight distributed on the midfoot/heel.
Brace your core and inhale deeply to create intra-abdominal pressure; keep the chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
Initiate the squat by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously, sitting back slightly but maintaining an upright torso so the bar stays balanced over the mid-foot.

Descend until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to a comfortable depth while maintaining an upright torso and neutral spine). Ensure knees track in line with the toes and do not cave inward.
Drive the movement upward by pressing through the heels and midfoot, extending the knees and hips while keeping the chest lifted and elbows high; exhale as you stand to full extension.
Reset your breath and posture between reps if needed; maintain controlled tempo and avoid letting the torso collapse forward or the heels rise.
After the set, carefully step forward and re‑rack the bar on the squat rack ensuring control and proper placement before fully releasing.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat variation that emphasizes the quadriceps by keeping the torso more upright and shifting load to the front of the legs; the bar is held in a front-rack or cross-arm position. It trains lower-body strength while also demanding core and upper-back stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Box Squat
exercises.notes: Set a sturdy box or bench behind you at about parallel (hip crease roughly level with or just above the top of the knee) and load a barbell on your upper back (high- or low-bar based on preference).
instructions: Set a sturdy box or bench behind you at about parallel (hip crease roughly level with or just above the top of the knee) and load a barbell on your upper back (high- or low-bar based on preference).
Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward; grip the bar comfortably and brace your core, pulling your shoulder blades together to create a stable upper back.
Unrack the bar and take one or two small steps back to clear the rack, keeping chest up and eyes forward.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees simultaneously — sit back onto the box in a controlled manner, keeping weight through your heels and knees tracking over your toes.
Lightly contact the box without fully relaxing or bouncing; maintain core tension and a firm upper-back position while paused (brief 1–2 second hold).
Drive your feet into the floor and extend hips and knees to stand up explosively while exhaling, maintaining a neutral spine and upright chest.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Rack the bar safely or have a spotter present; use safety pins if available and choose a box height that allows safe, controlled depth.
exercises.description: A loaded squat variation where the lifter sits back onto a box before standing, emphasizing the thighs while engaging the posterior chain and core for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Snatch
exercises.notes: Set the shoulders slightly in front of the bar, brace the abs, take a full breath and hold tension before initiating the lift.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip‑width, barbell over mid‑foot; take a wide snatch grip and hinge at the hips with knees bent so the bar is near the shins (upright torso slightly forward), back flat and chest up.
Set the shoulders slightly in front of the bar, brace the abs, take a full breath and hold tension before initiating the lift.
Begin the pull by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees explosively (triple extension) to generate upward momentum on the bar; keep the bar close to the body.
As the hips extend, aggressively shrug the shoulders and pull the elbows up and out, keeping the bar close and the torso tall—arms remain largely straight until full extension.
At the top of the upward drive, rapidly pull yourself under the bar by rotating the wrists and pulling the elbows down so the bar can be received overhead with straight arms.
Catch the bar locked out overhead while dropping into a deep overhead squat (full squat position) with hips below parallel if mobility allows; ensure knees track toes and core is braced.
Stand up from the deep overhead squat to full hip and knee extension while maintaining a locked overhead position and stable balance.
Lower the bar under control to the back, front, or to the floor (depending on gym rules and skill level) before repeating or rest between attempts.
exercises.description: The barbell snatch is an explosive full‑body Olympic lift that moves the barbell from the floor to an overhead locked position in one continuous motion, finishing in a deep overhead squat. It emphasizes powerful hip extension and coordination to generate upward momentum and then rapidly drop under the bar to catch it overhead.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Squat Calf Raise on Leg Press Machine
exercises.notes: Sit back in the leg press seat with your back firmly against the pad and both hands holding the side handles for stability (seated, slightly reclined position).
instructions: Sit back in the leg press seat with your back firmly against the pad and both hands holding the side handles for stability (seated, slightly reclined position).
Place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the footplate so your heels can move freely; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward.
Unlock or release the lever/safety according to the machine's instructions so the plate can move freely (machine-specific release), keeping control of the sled at all times.
Start with knees bent (seated squat position) and heels dropped to create a full stretch in the calves.
Drive through the balls of your feet, lifting your heels as high as possible by plantarflexing the ankles; squeeze the calves at the top of the movement.
Lower your heels slowly back to the starting stretched position under control — do not let the weight slam down.
Keep your knees slightly bent throughout to minimize knee extension and focus tension on the calves; avoid locking the knees.
Complete the desired reps, then return the sled to the safety catch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever-style leg press, the exercise isolates the calves by raising the heels against the footplate while the knees remain bent in a squat-like position. It emphasizes concentric calf contraction with a controlled eccentric stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Straight Legs Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.
Soften the knees slightly (do not lock them) and set a neutral spine by bracing the abs and pulling the shoulder blades back slightly.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips straight back (hip hinge), keeping the dumbbells close to the front of the thighs.
Lower the dumbbells down the front of your legs until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (around mid-shin or just below the knees), maintaining a flat back and neutral neck.
Keep the knees only slightly bent throughout; avoid excessive knee bend or letting the lower back round.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the dumbbells close to the legs.
Maintain a firm grip so the forearms remain engaged, exhale as you stand, and repeat for the desired reps.
Use lighter weights or reduce range of motion if you cannot maintain a neutral spine or hamstring tension throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back; performed with nearly straight legs and a neutral spine. Keep the weights close to the legs and drive the hips back and forward to move the load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft knees).
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft knees).
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep a neutral spine and chin tuck throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge), keeping the dumbbells close to your legs as you lower them down the front of your thighs.
Lower until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach just below mid-shin, maintaining the slight knee bend and neutral spine — avoid rounding the back.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing, keeping the dumbbells close to the body.
Squeeze the glutes at the top without overextending the lower back and maintain controlled tempo; repeat for desired reps.
Coaching tips: keep weight in the heels, avoid bending excessively at the knees (this becomes a squat), and maintain a firm grip to engage the forearms.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the posterior chain, primarily the hamstrings, while maintaining a neutral spine. The movement lowers the weights by pushing the hips back and returns by driving the hips forward.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Single Leg Curl (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up the machine by loading plates and adjusting the lower leg/roller pad so it sits just above the back of your ankle when standing.
instructions: Set up the machine by loading plates and adjusting the lower leg/roller pad so it sits just above the back of your ankle when standing.

Stand facing the machine, place your chest/abdomen against the support pad if provided, and grasp the handles or frame for stability; support your weight on the non-working foot while the working foot is behind the roller.
Start with the working leg almost fully extended (knee nearly straight) and maintain an upright torso with a braced core and hips squared to the machine.
Exhale and flex the knee of the working leg, curling your heel toward your buttocks in a smooth, controlled motion until you reach peak contraction of the hamstrings.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the hamstrings, avoiding excessive hip extension or swinging of the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position, controlling the resistance until the working leg is nearly straight without hyperextending the knee.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one leg, then switch to the other leg; keep the non-working foot planted or lightly supported to maintain balance throughout.
Keep movement tempo controlled, avoid using momentum, and adjust load so you can maintain proper form for all reps.
exercises.description: A standing, single-leg hamstring curl performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance while supporting the body on the machine pad.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Rack Pull
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a rack or on pins at roughly knee to mid-thigh height depending on the desired range of motion.
instructions: Set the bar in a rack or on pins at roughly knee to mid-thigh height depending on the desired range of motion.
Stand facing the bar with feet about hip-width apart and toes pointing forward, bar over the mid-foot.
Bend at the hips with a slight bend in the knees (standing, hip-hinge position) and grasp the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip just outside the knees.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly back and down to create a strong upper-back position, and maintain a neutral spine (no rounding) throughout.
Initiate the lift by driving the hips forward and extending through the glutes while keeping the bar close to the legs; straighten the knees as needed to complete the movement.
At the top, fully extend the hips and squeeze the glutes while standing tall (avoid excessive leaning back), then lower the bar under control back to the rack pins.
Repeat for the desired reps, resetting your hip hinge and core brace between reps. Use appropriate load and rack height to keep technique strict.
exercises.description: A partial deadlift performed from an elevated bar in a rack that emphasizes hip extension and glute activation while reducing range of motion. The lift trains the posterior chain and upper-back lockout through a shortened pulling range.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set the seat so your back and head are fully supported against the pad and your hips and knees start at about 90° with feet placed hip-width on the platform.
instructions: Set the seat so your back and head are fully supported against the pad and your hips and knees start at about 90° with feet placed hip-width on the platform.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the pad, grip the side handles for stability, and ensure the machine safety latch or pin is engaged before beginning.
Unlock or release the safety according to the machine (or have a spotter remove the lock) so the platform can move freely.
Exhale and press through your heels and mid-foot to extend your legs, driving the footplate away until legs are nearly straight — do not hyperextend or lock the knees.

Pause briefly at the top with tension in the quads, then inhale and slowly bend the knees to return the platform toward your chest under control until your knees are about 90° (or a comfortable range).
Keep your lower back and hips pressed against the pad throughout; avoid letting the hips come off the seat or the knees collapse inward.
Repeat for the desired reps, then re-engage the safety/lock before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated leg press performed on a lever machine that drives a footplate away from the body to extend the knees and hips; primarily trains the quadriceps with secondary work by the glutes, hamstrings and calves. The exerciser pushes the platform while seated with the back supported.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
Sit with your back fully supported against the pad and both feet placed hip-width on the lower edge of the foot platform (balls of feet and toes on the platform, heels free to move).
Grip the handles or seat sides for stability and ensure the safety latch or stops are set before starting.
Unlatch the machine (if applicable) and begin from a neutral/dorsiflexed position with a slight stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet by plantarflexing the ankles to raise the platform until your calves are fully contracted; do not use the knees or hips to drive the movement.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, then inhale and slowly lower the platform under control until you feel a stretch in the calves (avoid dropping quickly or bouncing).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then re-engage the safety latch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever calf press machine, this exercise uses controlled ankle plantarflexion to raise the foot platform and load the calf muscles. It emphasizes the gastrocnemius/soleus complex while the torso and knees remain supported.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your knees just off the edge, chest supported, and hook your lower calves/ankles under the padded lever.
Grip the handles or edge of the bench for stability and keep your hips pressed into the bench throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl your heels up toward your glutes by flexing the knees until you reach a full but comfortable contraction.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the hamstrings and glutes.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start with controlled extension of the knees, avoiding letting the hips rise.
Perform repetitions with steady tempo, focusing on full range of motion and control rather than momentum.
exercises.description: Prone (face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by bending the knees to lift a pad toward the glutes. The movement emphasizes knee flexion with a controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
instructions: Set up: Sit and then lie supine on the sled bench which is angled at 45 degrees; place your feet shoulder-width on the middle of the footplate with toes pointing slightly outward.
Grip the side handles and ensure your lower back and hips are pressed firmly against the backpad; unlock the sled safety/locks if present.
Start position: Bend your knees to bring your thighs toward your chest until knees are about 90 degrees, keeping feet flat on the plate and knees tracking over toes.
Press: Exhale and drive through your heels and mid-foot to extend your knees and hips, pushing the footplate away until legs are nearly straight but do not lock the knees at full extension.
Return: Inhale and slowly control the sled back down by bending the knees, lowering the weight under tension until your knees reach about 90 degrees again.
Repetitions: Repeat for the desired number of reps with controlled tempo; avoid bouncing at the bottom or using momentum to lock the knees.
Safety cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the pad throughout, avoid lifting hips off the pad, and keep knees aligned with feet. Rerack/lock the sled before exiting.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed supine on an inclined sled platform where you push the footplate away using the legs. The movement primarily extends the knees to target the quadriceps while the glutes, hamstrings, and calves assist and stabilize.

exercises.name: Barbell Narrow Stance Squat
exercises.notes: Setup: Rack the barbell at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Setup: Rack the barbell at about mid-chest height. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back on the posterior delts (not on the neck). Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack and stance: Unrack the bar and take one or two small steps back. Stand with a narrow stance — feet close together or hip-width at most, toes pointing forward or slightly outward.
Brace core and posture: Keep a neutral spine, chest up, shoulders back, and brace your abs. Eyes forward to maintain alignment.

Descent: Inhale and begin the squat by bending at the hips and knees, sending your hips back slightly while keeping the knees tracking over the toes. Lower until your thighs are about parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows).
Control and alignment: Avoid letting the knees collapse inward; press them slightly outward in line with your toes. Keep weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels, not the toes.
Ascent: Exhale and drive upward through the heels, extending the knees and hips to return to the standing position while maintaining a tight core and neutral spine.
Finish and rack: Reset posture at the top, then carefully step forward and rack the bar when the set is complete.
Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins when loading heavy. Keep repetitions controlled and prioritize form over depth or load to protect knees and lower back.
exercises.description: A barbell squat performed with a narrow foot stance that places greater emphasis on the quadriceps while still engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core. The lifter holds a barbell across the upper back and lowers hips down and back before returning to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Stand facing the machine and place your chest/forearms or hands on the support pad/handles for stability; plant the non-working foot flat on the floor and keep it slightly bent for balance.
Place the working foot against the lever pad behind you so the pad contacts the lower shin/ankle area, with the working knee either slightly bent or in a comfortable neutral angle.
Brace your core, keep your torso upright and hips square, and avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Exhale and drive the working leg straight back by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is in line with or slightly behind the torso.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining a tight core, then inhale and slowly return the lever to the starting position under control without letting the weight stack slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing hip extension where one leg pushes a rear lever backward to extend the hip, emphasizing the glute muscles with assistance from the hamstrings. Performed while braced against a pad/handle to isolate hip extension through a controlled rearward kick.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at mid-back height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at mid-back height. Step under the bar and place it across the rear delts/upper trapezius area just below the top of the shoulders (low-bar position), not on the neck.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, retract the shoulder blades and squeeze the upper back to create a stable shelf; elbows should be pointed slightly down and back to support the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening the legs and take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack. Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core (tighten abs and breathe into the belly) and maintain a neutral spine; keep chest up and eyes forward. The torso will lean forward more than a high-bar squat but should remain rigid.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously; keep the knees tracking over the toes and push them outward to avoid caving in.
Lower until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to your comfortable depth while maintaining form), keeping weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Drive upward by pushing through the heels and extending the hips and knees simultaneously while maintaining a braced core and tight upper back; exhale as you rise.
Return to a fully upright standing position with hips locked out, then repeat for the desired reps. Control the bar on each rep and reset your breath and brace between reps if needed.
When finished, walk the bar forward and carefully re-rack it on the squat rack. Use a spotter or safety pins if lifting heavy or training near failure.
exercises.description: A loaded barbell squat with the bar positioned low on the rear shoulders (across the posterior deltoids), emphasizing the thighs and posterior chain. The movement consists of a controlled hip-back-and-down descent to roughly parallel followed by an explosive drive back to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Dimel Deadlift
exercises.notes: Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar with both hands just outside the legs using an overhand or mixed grip.
Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips back (hip hinge) and lower the barbell down the front of your legs toward mid-shin while keeping the bar close to your body.
Keep the neck neutral, shoulders retracted, and do not let the lower back round as you descend; feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward explosively while pulling the bar up along the thighs to a near-lockout (stand tall), squeezing the glutes at the top.
Control the descent and repeat for the desired reps, breathing in on the lower and exhaling as you drive the hips forward.
Use lighter weight and higher reps if performing Dimel-style sets, and stop or reduce range if you feel lower-back rounding or pain.
exercises.description: A barbell hip-hinge exercise performed from a standing position that emphasizes eccentric and concentric work of the hamstrings by lowering the bar toward the shins and driving the hips forward to stand. The movement trains the posterior chain with a short range of motion and controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Rear Kick
exercises.notes: Set the lever or ankle attachment to the appropriate height and select a manageable resistance; stand facing the machine and grip the front handles for stability.
instructions: Set the lever or ankle attachment to the appropriate height and select a manageable resistance; stand facing the machine and grip the front handles for stability.

Position the working leg so the cuff or pad is secured at the back of the thigh or ankle (depending on machine); stand upright on the support leg with a slight bend in the knee and feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs engaged), keep the torso upright with a small forward lean if needed for balance, and maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive the working leg straight back by extending at the hip (avoid excessive lumbar arching), squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement; keep the knee slightly bent and the foot neutral.
Pause briefly at full hip extension, then return the leg under control to the starting position without letting the weight stack slam.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, then switch sides. Breathe out on the kickback (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
exercises.description: A standing hip extension performed on a lever/cable machine where one leg drives backward against resistance to isolate and strengthen the glute muscles. The exercise is executed while standing and holding the machine for balance, focusing on controlled hip extension and glute contraction.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
Sit with your back fully supported against the pad and both feet placed hip-width on the lower edge of the foot platform (balls of feet and toes on the platform, heels free to move).
Grip the handles or seat sides for stability and ensure the safety latch or stops are set before starting.
Unlatch the machine (if applicable) and begin from a neutral/dorsiflexed position with a slight stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet by plantarflexing the ankles to raise the platform until your calves are fully contracted; do not use the knees or hips to drive the movement.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, then inhale and slowly lower the platform under control until you feel a stretch in the calves (avoid dropping quickly or bouncing).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then re-engage the safety latch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever calf press machine, this exercise uses controlled ankle plantarflexion to raise the foot platform and load the calf muscles. It emphasizes the gastrocnemius/soleus complex while the torso and knees remain supported.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Raise Crunch (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your lower back is supported and the roller pad sits just above your ankles when knees are bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your lower back is supported and the roller pad sits just above your ankles when knees are bent.
Load the desired plate weight and sit with your back against the back pad, knees bent and feet secured under the roller pad; grasp the machine handles near your head to stabilize the upper body.
Start with torso upright and knees pulled slightly toward the torso (neutral tension) and inhale.
Exhale and simultaneously curl your spine forward and lift your knees slightly toward your chest by contracting your abs, performing a controlled crunch + leg raise against the plate resistance.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the contraction in your abs and avoiding excessive neck strain or momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, extending the spine and lowering the knees under control until tension is maintained.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, keeping movements slow and controlled and avoiding excessive lumbar rounding or swinging.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded seated crunch that combines a leg raise with a spinal crunch to emphasize the rectus abdominis. Performed seated with feet hooked under a roller pad while curling the torso and hips forward against resistance.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Side Bend
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; hold one dumbbell in the hand on the working side with the arm extended down by your thigh.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; hold one dumbbell in the hand on the working side with the arm extended down by your thigh.
Place the non-working hand lightly behind your head or across your chest to avoid pulling on the neck; keep your chest up and shoulders back (standing position).
Keep your hips and pelvis fixed — do not shift them laterally — and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Slowly bend your torso directly to the side of the dumbbell (lateral flexion) by contracting the obliques; lead with the ribcage so the movement comes from the torso, not the hips.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the range without rotating or leaning forward/backward, then contract the abs to return the torso to the starting upright position in a controlled manner.
Breathe in as you lower the torso and exhale as you return upright; perform the desired number of reps then switch sides.
Use a weight that allows smooth, controlled repetitions and avoid jerking or using momentum to pull the torso — stop if you feel low-back pain.
exercises.description: Standing lateral trunk flexion performed while holding a dumbbell at one side to load the obliques. The movement bends the torso toward the weight and then returns upright to work the abs (obliques) with grip and spinal stabilization assistance.

exercises.name: Cable Twist
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a single handle to a cable pulley positioned at about chest height.
instructions: Setup: Attach a single handle to a cable pulley positioned at about chest height. Stand upright facing the pulley with feet shoulder-width apart or in a slightly staggered stance for balance.
Grip & posture: Grasp the handle with both hands out in front of you, arms extended but not locked, shoulders down and back, knees slightly bent, and spine neutral.
Start position: Take a small step away from the machine so the cable is taut and the weight stack is lifted slightly; keep hips facing forward (minimize hip rotation).
Execute rotation: Exhale and rotate your torso away from the machine by driving the ribcage and shoulders across your hips, keeping your arms extended and the handle at roughly chest height.
Control the return: Inhale and slowly return to the start position under control, resisting the pull of the cable. Do not let the cable snap you back.
Repetitions & tempo: Perform the desired number of controlled repetitions on one side, then switch sides by repositioning so you rotate in the opposite direction.
Form tips: Keep the movement driven by the core (abs) not by the arms; maintain a neutral spine and avoid excessive lumbar extension or leaning. Adjust foot stance for stability as needed.
exercises.description: A standing cable rotation where you hold a single handle with both hands and rotate the torso away from the machine, using resisted trunk rotation to train the abs and improve core rotational strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Leg Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Leg Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
Stand with the balls of your feet on a raised platform, plate, or step so your heels can drop below the platform; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward.
Unrack the bar and step clear of the rack, keeping your torso tall, chest up and core braced.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, keeping knees straight but not locked and avoiding forward lean.
Pause and squeeze the calves at the top for a second.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent—do not bounce.
Repeat for the desired reps, then carefully step back into the rack to re-rack the bar.
Common cues: keep a neutral spine, maintain steady tempo, breathe out on the lift and in on the lower, and use a spotter or safety pins if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raises are a weighted heel-raise exercise performed with a barbell across the upper back to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). The movement raises the heels up onto the balls of the feet and then lowers back down for a full calf contraction and stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Step-up
exercises.notes: Start standing sideways next to a bench or sturdy platform with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip; stand close enough so the outer edge of your foot can step onto the bench.
instructions: Start standing sideways next to a bench or sturdy platform with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip; stand close enough so the outer edge of your foot can step onto the bench.
Brace your core (neutral spine and engaged abs), keep chest up and shoulders back, and gaze forward throughout the movement.
Place the foot closest to the bench onto the center of the bench with the entire foot contacting the surface, keeping the stepping knee tracking over the toes.

Drive through the heel of the foot on the bench and extend the hip and knee of that leg to lift your body up until the stepping leg is straight and you are standing tall on the bench (you may bring the trailing foot up to meet the lead foot or keep it hovering depending on your variation).
Pause briefly at the top with hips fully extended and weight primarily on the stepping leg; maintain upright posture and avoid leaning away from the bench.
Slowly lower your trailing foot back down to the floor in a controlled manner, then step the lead foot down to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps on one side, then switch sides so the other leg performs the step-ups. Focus on using the quads and glutes to lift, not momentum.
exercises.description: A lateral step-up performed while holding dumbbells at your sides; you step sideways onto a bench or platform to work the thighs and hip musculature. The exercise emphasizes single-leg pushing, targeting the quads while the glutes, hamstrings and stabilizers assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Dimel Deadlift
exercises.notes: Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar with both hands just outside the legs using an overhand or mixed grip.
Slightly bend the knees (soft knee) and brace your core, maintaining a neutral spine and chest up throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips back (hip hinge) and lower the barbell down the front of your legs toward mid-shin while keeping the bar close to your body.
Keep the neck neutral, shoulders retracted, and do not let the lower back round as you descend; feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward explosively while pulling the bar up along the thighs to a near-lockout (stand tall), squeezing the glutes at the top.
Control the descent and repeat for the desired reps, breathing in on the lower and exhaling as you drive the hips forward.
Use lighter weight and higher reps if performing Dimel-style sets, and stop or reduce range if you feel lower-back rounding or pain.
exercises.description: A barbell hip-hinge exercise performed from a standing position that emphasizes eccentric and concentric work of the hamstrings by lowering the bar toward the shins and driving the hips forward to stand. The movement trains the posterior chain with a short range of motion and controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Barbell Pause Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set position by hinging at the hips with a slight knee bend so your shins are near vertical, chest lifted, shoulder blades over the bar, and back neutral (no rounding).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell positioned over the mid-foot; approach the bar and grip it just outside the knees (double overhand or mixed grip).
Set position by hinging at the hips with a slight knee bend so your shins are near vertical, chest lifted, shoulder blades over the bar, and back neutral (no rounding).
Take a deep breath and brace your core, pull the slack out of the bar by tensioning the lats and upper back before initiating the lift.
Drive through the heels and extend the hips and knees together to lift the bar, keeping the bar close to the body and maintaining a neutral spine.
When the bar reaches the hip/knee region (the sticking point), intentionally pause and hold the top of the hinge for 1–2 seconds while keeping hips tight and chest up.
After the pause, actively push the hips back and bend the knees to lower the bar under control to the floor, maintaining a neutral spine throughout the descent.
Reset your position and breathing between reps if needed; avoid using a rounded back or excessive knee collapse during the lift and pause.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift variation where the lifter pauses at the hip/knee region to remove momentum and increase time under tension, emphasizing hip extension. The pause forces greater glute and posterior chain activation while demanding stable posture.

exercises.name: Lever Deadlift (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load plates on the machine and release any safety latch so the lever can move through its full range.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width under the lever platform, toes pointing forward or slightly out; approach the machine so the handles are reachable when you hinge.
Load plates on the machine and release any safety latch so the lever can move through its full range.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees, push hips back, keep chest lifted and eyes forward so the torso is braced (neutral spine).
Reach down and grasp the machine handles with a firm, even grip; keep shoulders over or slightly in front of the handles.
Brace your core and take a full breath, then drive through the heels and extend the hips and knees simultaneously to stand the lever up.
At the top, lock the hips by squeezing the glutes and avoid excessive lumbar extension — stand tall but neutral.
Lower the weight by pushing the hips back first, then bending the knees to return the lever to the start position while maintaining a neutral spine and the bar close to the legs.
Control the descent to maintain tension in the hamstrings; inhale on the way down and exhale as you drive up.
Repeat for the desired reps, and re-secure the safety latch before unloading plates or stepping away from the machine.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever deadlift is a hip‑hinge dominant lift performed on a lever machine that trains posterior chain strength by extending the hips and knees from a lowered position. It emphasizes hamstrings and glute activation while requiring spinal stabilization and grip strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Good Morning
exercises.notes: Unrack the bar and step back, bracing your core: take a deep breath and maintain a neutral spine and a slight bend in the knees (soft knees).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes pointing forward, barbell placed securely across the upper back/traps (not on the neck), hands gripping the bar just outside the shoulders.
Unrack the bar and step back, bracing your core: take a deep breath and maintain a neutral spine and a slight bend in the knees (soft knees).

Initiate the movement by pushing the hips straight back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the torso to hinge forward until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings or the torso is near parallel to the floor.
Keep the neck neutral (eyes forward or slightly down), shoulders retracted, and avoid rounding the lower back throughout the hinge.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then drive the hips forward to return to the upright starting position by contracting the hamstrings and glutes while exhaling.
Perform the desired number of reps with controlled tempo; use lighter weight if you cannot maintain a neutral spine or proper hip hinge mechanics.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed with a barbell across the upper back that emphasizes the hamstrings while recruiting the glutes and spinal erectors to control torso position. It strengthens the posterior chain and challenges core stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Brace your core, set a neutral spine (no rounding) and slightly bend the knees — this is a standing starting position.

exercises.name: Barbell Staggered Stance Deadlift
