exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline bench).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip; bring the dumbbells to shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°) and wrists stacked over elbows.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep the elbows under the wrists (avoid excessive flaring).
Exhale and press both dumbbells up and slightly together until your arms are almost extended — do not lock the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to shoulder level, keeping the same neutral grip.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining steady tempo, stable bench contact, and neutral wrist alignment.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, dumbbells are pressed upward with a neutral (hammer) grip to emphasize the upper chest. The movement uses a controlled press and descent to work the pectorals with assistance from the delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Assisted Parallel Close Grip Pull-up
exercises.notes: Set the assistance weight on the machine to provide the desired amount of help (more weight = easier). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the assistance weight on the machine to provide the desired amount of help (more weight = easier).

Kneel on the machine’s knee pad so your feet are off the ground and your hips are neutral; grasp the parallel (neutral) handles with a close grip (hands roughly shoulder-width or slightly narrower), palms facing each other.
Start with your arms fully extended, chest lifted, shoulders pulled slightly down and back (scapular depression/retraction) and core braced.
Exhale and pull by driving your elbows down toward your ribs, squeezing your lats and upper back, until your chin clears the handles or your chest approaches the bar.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight shoulder position (avoid shrugging), then inhale and slowly lower yourself under control back to full arm extension.
Maintain a steady tempo, avoid swinging or kipping, and adjust assistance as you get stronger to progress the exercise.
exercises.description: A machine-assisted neutral (parallel) close-grip pull-up that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while reducing load with a counterweight pad to make the vertical pulling motion easier. The close, neutral grip increases biceps and forearm involvement and promotes a more upright torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and chest pad (if present) so your feet are flat on the platform and the handles are within comfortable reach with your arms extended. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat and chest pad (if present) so your feet are flat on the platform and the handles are within comfortable reach with your arms extended.
Select an appropriate weight on the lever stack.
Sit upright with your chest against the pad (or seated tall if no pad), knees slightly bent, feet stable, and maintain a neutral spine and head alignment.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a firm, neutral grip; start with your arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows backward and slightly down, retracting your shoulder blades to bring the handles toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Keep elbows close to your sides, avoid excessive torso lean or shrugging the shoulders, and breathe out as you pull.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze your lats and upper back.
Slowly extend your arms to return the handles to the start position under control, allowing the shoulder blades to protract slightly but maintain tension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever rows are a horizontal pulling exercise performed on a lever machine that primarily targets the lats while also engaging the middle and upper back and arm muscles. The movement involves pulling handles toward the torso with a scapular retraction and controlled return.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Back Seated Row
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight, reach forward and grasp the handle with a neutral or double-handed grip, arms extended and spine straight (no rounded back). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the bench or seat facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Select an appropriate weight, reach forward and grasp the handle with a neutral or double-handed grip, arms extended and spine straight (no rounded back).
Engage your core, maintain an upright torso (straight back), and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction).
Drive your elbows straight back toward your torso while keeping them close to your sides until the handle touches your abdomen or lower chest.
Pause and squeeze your lats/upper back at the end range for 1–2 seconds while avoiding excessive torso lean or trunk rotation.
Slowly extend your arms and allow the cable to return to the start position under control, keeping a straight back throughout the eccentric phase.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the pull and breathing in on the return.
exercises.description: A seated cable row performed with an upright, straight back to pull a handle toward the torso, emphasizing scapular retraction and elbow drive to work the mid- and lower-back muscles. Focus is on controlled pulling and resisting the cable on the return.

exercises.name: Cable Half Kneeling Face Pull
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to upper‑chest/head height and attach a rope or double handle. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to upper‑chest/head height and attach a rope or double handle. Choose a light‑to‑moderate load to maintain strict form.
Assume a half‑kneeling position: one knee on the floor and the opposite foot planted forward so torso is upright and hips square to the pulley. Keep a tall spine and neutral neck.
Reach forward and grasp the rope/handles with a neutral or thumbs‑up grip. Start with arms extended and tension on the cable.
Brace your core and pull the handles toward your face by driving the elbows out to the sides while retracting the shoulder blades. Lead the movement with the elbows, not the hands.
At the end of the pull, externally rotate the shoulders so the hands/rope move toward the sides of your face or slightly behind the ears and squeeze the rear delts and upper back for 1 second.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, extending the arms back to the start while maintaining an upright torso and not collapsing the chest.
Perform all reps on one kneeling side, then switch legs and repeat to maintain unilateral balance. Breathe out while pulling, inhale while returning.
Keep cues in mind: avoid shrugging the shoulders, do not lean back to drive the weight, and use a weight that allows full external rotation and a controlled tempo.
exercises.description: A unilateral, half‑kneeling variation of the cable face pull that emphasizes the rear delts and upper back while adding core and hip stability demands. Pull the cable toward the face with elbows high and externally rotate the hands at the end of the pull to maximize rear delt activation.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Chest Press
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach single hand grips; select an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach single hand grips; select an appropriate weight.

Stand in the middle of the station facing away from the stacks with one foot slightly forward (staggered stance) for stability, arms bent and handles held at chest level, elbows roughly at chest height.
Brace your core (engage abs), keep a neutral spine and shoulders slightly retracted to set the starting position; wrists remain neutral.
Step forward or press your feet into the floor to create tension in the cables so the handles cannot move without effort.
Exhale and press both handles forward in a controlled motion, extending the arms until nearly straight but without locking the elbows; keep the movement horizontal and drive from the chest.
At full extension, pause briefly and squeeze the pectorals (and serratus anterior) while keeping the torso stable and not overextending the lower back.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with control, allowing the elbows to bend to chest level while maintaining core tension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping consistent tempo and avoiding excessive leaning or rotating of the torso.
exercises.description: A standing cable press performed from a dual‑pulley station that emphasizes horizontal pushing to work the chest. The movement also requires shoulder and triceps involvement while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Palms In Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing each other (palms‑in), elbows pointed slightly forward and wrists neutral.
Keep the torso upright and ribs down; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until the arms are nearly extended but elbows are not forcibly locked.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged and scapulae stable (avoid shrugging excessively).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at shoulder height with control.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping the core engaged and avoiding arching the lower back.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell shoulder press performed with a neutral (palms‑facing‑in) grip; primarily targets the delts while engaging the triceps and upper chest for pressing and the traps for scapular support. Performed standing to require core stability and upright posture.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Hammer Grip Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at about shoulder/ear level when seated and select an appropriate weight on the lever machine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at about shoulder/ear level when seated and select an appropriate weight on the lever machine.
Sit upright with your back fully against the pad, feet flat on the floor, chest lifted and core braced.
Grasp the handles with a hammer (neutral) grip (palms facing each other), elbows bent and positioned slightly forward of the body.
Unlatch or release the safety (if applicable) and begin with the handles at shoulder height.
Exhale and press the handles upward and slightly back in a controlled arc until your arms are nearly extended; avoid aggressively locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top without shrugging the shoulders; keep the neck long and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly back to the starting position at shoulder height, maintaining control throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, re-rack or engage the safety when finished.
exercises.description: Seated lever machine shoulder press performed with a hammer (neutral) grip, pressing the handles overhead to primarily work the delts while the triceps and pectorals assist; the machine provides a guided path for controlled movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Neutral grip Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
Stand between the stacks in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) and take one handle in each hand with palms facing each other.
Step forward slightly until you feel tension in the cables, lean your torso forward a few degrees and keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and brace your core to prevent trunk rotation or excessive arching.
Exhale and bring your hands together in a controlled horizontal arc in front of your chest, squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Hold the contracted position for 1–2 seconds, then inhale and slowly reverse the motion, letting the cables return your arms to the starting wide position while maintaining the elbow bend.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement slow and controlled and avoiding locking the elbows or using momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable fly using a neutral (palms-facing) grip that isolates the chest by bringing the hands together in a horizontal arc. Performed from a staggered stance with a slight elbow bend to emphasize the pectoral squeeze while the cables provide continuous tension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
Sit upright with a slight lean back (about 10–20 degrees), chest up, and scapulae slightly retracted; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Inhale, brace your core, then exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back until the bar reaches your upper chest or collarbone level.
Squeeze your lats and upper back at the bottom of the motion while keeping wrists neutral and avoiding excessive neck or shoulder shrugging.
Slowly control the weight back to the start by allowing the arms to extend fully overhead while maintaining scapular tension (do not fully relax shoulders between reps).
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out on the pull and inhale on the return, and avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a supinated (reverse/underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. Pull the bar from an overhead position down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and shoulders retracted.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Seated Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and core braced to maintain a neutral spine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward and elbows bent roughly 90 degrees (elbows slightly in front of the torso).
Inhale, then press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until your arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid fully locking the elbows).
Exhale at the top and keep shoulders down and away from the ears; maintain a straight wrist alignment (dumbbells stacked over elbows).
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control, keeping tension in the delts and triceps.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Avoid excessive low-back arching—tuck the ribs and keep core engaged throughout the movement.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press performed from a bench: dumbbells are pressed overhead from shoulder height to extend the arms, primarily training the shoulder (delts) with assisting work from the triceps and chest. Performed seated to reduce lower-body involvement and improve shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Lean forward from the hips and reach to grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders rounded forward (scapulae protracted). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent; select an appropriate weight.
Lean forward from the hips and reach to grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders rounded forward (scapulae protracted).
Keep a neutral spine and a slight chest-up posture; initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades (pull shoulders down and back) before bending the elbows.

Pull the handle toward your torso (near the lower chest/upper abdomen) driving the elbows back and keeping them close to your sides; squeeze the shoulder blades together at the end of the concentric phase.
Pause briefly at full contraction, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding excessive torso swing or leaning back.
Slowly extend the arms and allow the shoulder blades to protract as you return to the starting position with control, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining neutral spine, controlled tempo, and full scapular retraction on each rep.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed from a seated position that primarily targets the latissimus dorsi while working the mid/upper back and arms. It develops scapular retraction and elbow flexion strength through a controlled pull and release.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Low Fly
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and a slight hinge at the hips (standing, slight forward lean). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and a slight hinge at the hips (standing, slight forward lean).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at or just above thigh level (low position). Keep the spine neutral and shoulders down.
Maintain a small, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement; do not lock the joints.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells in a wide arc up and inward, leading with the elbows so the hands meet or come close in front of the upper chest/shoulder level (low-to-high arc).
Pause briefly at the top and actively squeeze the pectorals while keeping the shoulders stable (avoid shrugging).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back along the same arc to the starting low position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive back extension.
exercises.description: A standing low-to-high dumbbell fly that moves the weights in an upward arc from near the hips to in front of the chest, emphasizing the pectorals. Keep a slight bend in the elbows and squeeze the chest at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Fly
exercises.notes: Sit near the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit near the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart.

Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (near-parallel to the floor) while keeping a neutral spine and neck; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip and arms hanging down.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and brace your core to prevent swinging.

Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, leading with your elbows and focusing on contracting the rear delts; lift until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or in line with your shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the shoulder blades, then slowly lower the weights back to the start under control while inhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a neutral spine and avoiding momentum or torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell fly that emphasizes the rear deltoids by horizontally abducting the arms from a forward-leaning seated position. Performed with a slight elbow bend while squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Lever Chest Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit roughly at mid-chest level when you grasp them; select an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit roughly at mid-chest level when you grasp them; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back and head firmly against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, wrists aligned with forearms and shoulders relaxed away from ears.
Begin with elbows slightly below shoulder height and forearms pointed forward (starting position).
Exhale and press the handles forward and slightly together by extending your arms until nearly straight; avoid locking the elbows at full extension.
Pause briefly at the end of the press while maintaining control and keeping the chest engaged.
Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position, controlling the weight and stopping when elbows reach roughly the same angle as the start.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping the torso stable and avoiding arching the lower back throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated lever chest press performed on a machine to push handles forward using the chest muscles; the movement also involves the front delts and triceps. Use a controlled pressing motion from mid-chest to near full arm extension without locking the elbows.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Row
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, keeping your shoulders down and away from your ears. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your chest firmly against the pad, feet flat on the footrests and knees slightly bent; adopt a tall, neutral spine.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, keeping your shoulders down and away from your ears.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction) and driving your elbows straight back.
Continue pulling until the handles approach your lower ribs/abdomen while squeezing the lats and upper back; avoid shrugging or excessive elbow flare.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start while allowing the shoulder blades to protract, controlling the weight on the eccentric phase.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping motion smooth and avoiding torso rocking, momentum, or rounding of the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated lever row on a chest-supported machine that pulls a handle toward the torso, primarily targeting the lats and middle back while also engaging the arms and rear shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Assisted Close grip Underhand Chin-up
exercises.notes: Adjust the assisted machine weight so you have the desired level of assistance and step onto the knee pad; kneel with both knees on the pad and feet behind you. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the assisted machine weight so you have the desired level of assistance and step onto the knee pad; kneel with both knees on the pad and feet behind you.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an underhand (palms facing you) close grip, hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower; maintain a neutral head and upright torso.
Start from a dead hang with arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed, bracing your core to prevent excessive swinging.
Drive your elbows down and back, retracting your shoulder blades and pulling your chest toward the bar until your chin clears the bar or your chest meets the bar.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the lats and biceps, then lower yourself slowly and controlled back to full arm extension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping motion smooth and avoiding kipping or swinging; adjust assistance as needed between sets.
exercises.description: A close-grip underhand (supinated) chin-up performed on an assisted pull-up machine that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement; the machine pad provides counterweight assistance to reduce the effective load. The movement pulls the torso up until the chin clears the bar and then lowers under control to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand (pronated) grip, arms extended down in front of you and weights close to your thighs.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the dumbbells close to the body and leading with the elbows.
Raise the dumbbells until your elbows reach roughly chest/shoulder height or just below chin level, keeping wrists relatively neutral and palms facing your body or slightly inward.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged but avoid excessive shrugging or leaning back.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms without locking the elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining an upright standing position, steady breathing, and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell upright row where you pull the weights vertically toward your upper chest, emphasizing the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with assistance from the trapezius, biceps, and forearms. Perform with a controlled motion and elbows leading to keep tension on the delts.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent (or seated on a bench); keep a tall spine and neutral head position.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing toward you) and extend your arms so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by pulling the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and keeping them at about shoulder height.
As you pull, externally rotate your shoulders so the ends of the rope separate and your hands move either side of your face; squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end range.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive backward lean or shrugging.
Slowly return the rope to the start position with control, maintaining tension in the cable and scapular control throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and maintain controlled tempo to protect the shoulder joint.
exercises.description: A seated cable face pull using a rope attachment that emphasizes the rear delts and upper posterior shoulder musculature by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the hands and squeezing the shoulder blades.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Lever Crossovers
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so the handles are at mid-chest level when you sit upright. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are at mid-chest level when you sit upright.
Sit with your back pressed firmly against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees at about 90°.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or palms-down grip and start with your arms opened out to the sides at shoulder height, elbows slightly bent.
Brace your core and keep your torso stable; avoid leaning forward or arching the lower back.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a slow, controlled arc in front of your chest, allowing the hands to cross slightly if the machine path allows.
Pause briefly at full contraction and focus on squeezing the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position, maintaining the slight bend in the elbows and control throughout the eccentric phase.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping tension on the chest and avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: Seated lever crossovers are a machine chest fly variation that brings the arms together in front of the body to isolate and load the pectorals while minimizing torso movement. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and a controlled arc to squeeze the chest at peak contraction.

exercises.name: Lever Assisted Standing Chin-up
exercises.notes: Set the machine assistance level (lighter assistance = harder) so you can perform controlled repetitions with good form. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the machine assistance level (lighter assistance = harder) so you can perform controlled repetitions with good form.
Stand on the platform facing the machine and grasp the handles with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width apart; arms should be fully extended at the start.
Keep feet planted on the platform, chest upright and core braced; avoid excessive swinging or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by pulling your elbows down and back, driving your chest toward the handles and squeezing the lats and upper back at the top of the motion.
Pause briefly with chin or upper chest close to the handles while maintaining scapular retraction and elbow flexion.
Slowly lower yourself back to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until they are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and adjust assistance as needed to maintain strict form.
exercises.description: A lever-assisted, standing chin-up uses a counterbalance to reduce the effective bodyweight while performing an underhand pull, allowing you to train the vertical pulling pattern. It emphasizes the lats with strong involvement from the biceps and upper-back musculature for scapular retraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Rear Delt Row (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set the cable to a high pulley and attach a rope. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the cable to a high pulley and attach a rope. Stand facing the machine about 2–4 feet away and grab the rope with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip in both hands.
Adopt a staggered or shoulder-width stance with a slight knee bend and a small hip hinge so your torso is upright to slightly inclined forward; brace your core.
Step back until you feel constant tension on the cable, arms extended in front of you at about eye to forehead level.
Initiate the movement by externally rotating your shoulders and driving your elbows straight out to the sides (away from the body) while pulling the rope toward your upper chest/face.
At the end range, spread the rope ends apart slightly and squeeze the rear delts and upper back for 1–2 seconds; keep the elbows high and avoid shrugging.
Slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to extend forward until tension is maintained, but do not let the weight stack slam.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric). Use a weight that allows strict form and full rear-delt activation.
Avoid leaning back or using torso momentum; if you cannot maintain elbows-high position, reduce the load or shorten the range of motion.
exercises.description: A standing cable row performed with a rope and a high pulley that emphasizes the posterior delts by pulling the rope toward the face/chest with elbows flared, hitting the rear shoulder fibers and upper back musculature.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Pullover
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell placed vertically across your palms (hold one head of the dumbbell with both hands) or hold a single dumbbell by the handle with both hands cupped around one end. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell placed vertically across your palms (hold one head of the dumbbell with both hands) or hold a single dumbbell by the handle with both hands cupped around one end.

Lie back so your upper back and shoulders are supported on the bench (supine on incline), feet flat on the floor for stability and hips down; keep a slight natural arch in the low back but avoid excessive lumbar extension.
Start with the dumbbell held directly over your chest with arms extended but elbows slightly bent and hands steady; brace your core.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head until you feel a comfortable stretch across the lats/pectorals (do not force range or let shoulders shrug upward).
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining a slight bend in the elbows and tension in the shoulders/triceps.
Exhale and pull the dumbbell back up in the same arc using your lats and chest, returning to the start position over the chest; keep movement smooth and avoid using momentum.
Keep the elbows slightly bent throughout, avoid locking out at the top, and maintain shoulder blades down and back to reduce neck/shoulder strain.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot control the descent or if you feel pain in the shoulders or neck.
exercises.description: A supine incline bench pullover performed with a single dumbbell; the weight is moved in an arcing motion from over the chest to behind the head to stretch and contract the lats and chest. Performed slowly to emphasize the stretch and pulling action of the back and front torso muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Underhand Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set the seat height and thigh pads so your knees are secured under the pad and select an appropriate weight; attach a straight bar to the high pulley. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the seat height and thigh pads so your knees are secured under the pad and select an appropriate weight; attach a straight bar to the high pulley.
Sit upright on the bench with feet flat, torso tall and slightly braced, then reach up and grasp the bar with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart.
Begin with arms fully extended and scapulae slightly protracted (shoulders relaxed up), inhale, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back.
Pull the bar to the upper chest/clavicle area while keeping the torso stable and leaning back only slightly; focus on contracting the lats and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then slowly return the bar under control to the start position with arms nearly straight and a controlled stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding torso momentum or excessive backward lean; keep wrists neutral and core engaged throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable underhand pulldown (reverse-grip pulldown) is a vertical pulling exercise that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while using a supinated grip to increase biceps involvement. Performed seated at a high pulley, the bar is pulled down to the upper chest with elbows driven down and back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees bent; keep your back neutral and shoulder blades gently retracted. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees bent; keep your back neutral and shoulder blades gently retracted.
Pick up a pair of dumbbells and hold them at chest level with a neutral (palms facing each other / hammer) grip and elbows bent about 45 degrees from your torso.
Brace your core, keep your wrists neutral, and press the dumbbells upward in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended but not locked out.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining the neutral grip and stable shoulder position.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the starting position at chest level, keeping elbows from flaring excessively.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on steady, controlled motion and exhaling during the press and inhaling during the lowering phase.
Maintain scapular stability throughout; avoid overarching the lower back or letting the shoulders roll forward.
exercises.description: Supine dumbbell press using a neutral (hammer) grip to press weights from chest to full arm extension; emphasizes the chest while reducing shoulder rotation. Performed on a flat bench with feet planted and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Front to Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall (neutral spine) with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing your body (standing position). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand tall (neutral spine) with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing your body (standing position).
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and maintain the same elbow angle throughout the movement; do not lock the arms.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells straight forward to shoulder height (front raise) with controlled motion — lead with the elbows rather than the hands.
Without pausing, continue the motion in an arc outward so the arms move from front-to-side until the dumbbells reach shoulder height to form a T (lateral raise position).
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down (do not shrug) and shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the weights back along the same path, first moving from lateral back to front and then down to the starting position at your sides, maintaining control.
Repeat for desired reps, avoiding torso lean, momentum, or swinging; keep the core engaged and neck relaxed throughout.
exercises.description: A standing combination raise that begins as a front dumbbell raise and transitions into a lateral raise, stressing the shoulder delts through the anterior-to-lateral plane; performed slowly to improve shoulder strength and stability.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Lever One Arm Incline Chest Press (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine and add plates on the lever arm. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the lever machine and add plates on the lever arm. Adjust the seat height so the handle is roughly level with the upper chest/clavicular region when seated.

Sit with your back firmly against the incline pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the single handle with a firm grip using the working arm. Keep the non-working hand resting lightly on your thigh or the machine for balance.

Sit tall and brace your core; retract the shoulder blade of the working side slightly to create a stable chest position. Maintain a slight natural arch in the lower back but avoid leaning or twisting the torso.
Start with the elbow bent so the upper arm is at about shoulder height and the forearm is vertical. This is the start position.
Exhale and press the handle upward and slightly inward in a controlled arc until the arm is near full extension. Do not hyperextend or forcefully lock the elbow at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with control, then slowly inhale and lower the lever back along the same path until the upper arm returns to the start position and you feel a stretch in the chest.
Perform the desired number of reps for one arm while maintaining a stable torso and preventing rotation; then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.
Use a lighter weight when first learning unilateral control, focus on smooth tempo and full range of motion, and avoid bouncing or letting the shoulder collapse at the bottom of the movement.
exercises.description: A single-arm, plate-loaded incline press performed seated with back support that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest while allowing unilateral loading to correct imbalances and increase core stabilization.

exercises.name: Lever Assisted Standing Chin-up
exercises.notes: Set the machine assistance level (lighter assistance = harder) so you can perform controlled repetitions with good form. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the machine assistance level (lighter assistance = harder) so you can perform controlled repetitions with good form.
Stand on the platform facing the machine and grasp the handles with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width apart; arms should be fully extended at the start.
Keep feet planted on the platform, chest upright and core braced; avoid excessive swinging or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by pulling your elbows down and back, driving your chest toward the handles and squeezing the lats and upper back at the top of the motion.
Pause briefly with chin or upper chest close to the handles while maintaining scapular retraction and elbow flexion.
Slowly lower yourself back to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until they are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and adjust assistance as needed to maintain strict form.
exercises.description: A lever-assisted, standing chin-up uses a counterbalance to reduce the effective bodyweight while performing an underhand pull, allowing you to train the vertical pulling pattern. It emphasizes the lats with strong involvement from the biceps and upper-back musculature for scapular retraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl to Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward).

Initiate a controlled dumbbell curl by rotating the palms to face upward and bending the elbows to bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level; keep elbows close to your sides and avoid swinging the torso.

At the top of the curl with dumbbells near shoulder height, rotate palms so they face forward and press the weights overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the shoulders until arms are fully extended.
Pause briefly at full extension with the ribs down and core engaged; avoid overarching the lower back.
Lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled motion to shoulder height, then reverse the curl movement to return the weights to your sides in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the press and inhaling as you lower; maintain a steady tempo and avoid using momentum from the hips or legs.
exercises.description: A standing combination exercise that starts with a dumbbell curl and transitions into an overhead press, targeting the shoulder muscles while also working the arms. Perform the curl to bring weights to shoulder level, then press the dumbbells overhead and control the descent back to the start.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip and take a hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge forward at the hips and place your forehead or chest gently against the bench pad for support so your torso is close to horizontal (bench-assisted bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep a neutral spine and the head supported on the bench.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc — lead with the elbows — until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and you feel the rear delts contract.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position.
Keep the movement strict (no torso rotation or momentum) and maintain head/chest support on the bench throughout the set.
Choose a weight that allows controlled form for the full range of motion and perform recommended reps.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell raise that targets the rear (posterior) delts by moving the arms out to the sides while the torso is stabilized. The head/chest is supported on an incline bench to reduce momentum and isolate the rear deltoids.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent (or seated on a bench); keep a tall spine and neutral head position.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing toward you) and extend your arms so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by pulling the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and keeping them at about shoulder height.
As you pull, externally rotate your shoulders so the ends of the rope separate and your hands move either side of your face; squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end range.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive backward lean or shrugging.
Slowly return the rope to the start position with control, maintaining tension in the cable and scapular control throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and maintain controlled tempo to protect the shoulder joint.
exercises.description: A seated cable face pull using a rope attachment that emphasizes the rear delts and upper posterior shoulder musculature by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the hands and squeezing the shoulder blades.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
