exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a reverse (palms-up / supinated) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the dumbbells under your chest; brace your core and keep the neck in line with the spine.
Initiate the pull by driving the elbows upward and backward, pulling the dumbbells toward the lower ribcage/upper waist while keeping elbows close to the body.
At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the shoulder blades together without rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control until the arms are extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the weights up and inhale as you lower them; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. The lifter hip-hinges and pulls the dumbbells toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or pronated grip.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with weights beneath you; brace your core to prevent rounding of the lower back.
Pull the dumbbells toward your lower ribcage or waist by driving your elbows up and back, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight upper back, then lower the weights slowly under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps while keeping the torso still—avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Breathe out as you row the weights up and inhale as you lower them; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row is a standing, hip-hinged pulling exercise that targets the muscles of the upper back while also engaging the lats and arms. It develops horizontal pulling strength and scapular retraction while the spine and forearms stabilize the position.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Wide Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and place a loaded barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
Grip the bar with a wide overhand grip (wider than shoulder-width) and let your arms hang fully extended.
Brace your core, inhale, then pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows out and back, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders retracted, then exhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner until arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or spinal rounding; keep knees slightly bent and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed with a wide overhand grip that emphasizes the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. The torso stays hinged forward while the hips and spine stabilize the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Scapula Row
exercises.notes: Set up with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (kneeling on bench), the opposite foot planted on the floor for stability; grip a dumbbell in the free hand with the arm hanging straight toward the floor.
instructions: Set up with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (kneeling on bench), the opposite foot planted on the floor for stability; grip a dumbbell in the free hand with the arm hanging straight toward the floor.
Hinge forward at the hips so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a neutral spine and bracing your core; maintain a slight bend in the supporting knee.

Begin the movement by retracting and depressing the scapula of the working side (pull the shoulder blade back and down) while keeping the elbow nearly straight or allowing only a small bend; do not initiate the movement by bending the elbow.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second, feeling the contraction across the upper back and between the shoulder blades.
Slowly allow the scapula to protract and return the arm to the fully extended starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides so the previously supporting hand/knee becomes the working side. Maintain neutral spine and avoid torso rotation throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell row that emphasizes scapular retraction to target the upper back and mid-trapezius/rhomboid area. Performed from a kneeling/bent-over position with a single-arm dumbbell, focusing on pulling the shoulder blade back and down.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with shoulders and head supported, feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the hips.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with shoulders and head supported, feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the hips.
Grasp one dumbbell by the ends with both hands (or hold a single DB handle with hands cupped), arms extended above chest with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a neutral spine and braced core.

Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head, keeping the same slight elbow bend and focusing on stretching the lats and chest; stop when you feel a comfortable stretch or when the dumbbell is roughly level with your forehead/bench line.
Pause briefly at the bottom without letting the lower back over-arch; keep hips down and core engaged to prevent excessive lumbar extension.
Exhale and pull the dumbbell back in the same arcing path until it is over your chest, initiating the movement by contracting the lats and upper back rather than forcing it with the arms.
Maintain steady, controlled tempo for each rep, avoid jerking or using momentum, and keep elbow angle constant throughout the movement.
Adjust range of motion or weight if you cannot maintain scapular control or if you feel pain in the shoulders; perform 8–15 controlled reps depending on your goal.
exercises.description: Supine dumbbell pullover performed on a flat bench: the weight is moved in an arc from over the chest to behind the head and back, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the lats while also engaging the chest and triceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.

Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.

Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.

exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Lever High Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present).
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present). Adjust seat so handles are at or slightly above shoulder height when arms are extended.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended and shoulders relaxed. Take a full breath and brace your core.

Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the handles down and back toward the upper chest/near clavicle, leading with the elbows and keeping them at roughly shoulder height (elbows flared).
Squeeze the shoulder blades and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1 second, keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive trunk lean or momentum.
Slowly extend the arms back to the start position under control (eccentric phase), maintaining tension in the upper back and preventing the weights from slamming.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out during the pull and inhale during the release.

Common form tips: keep the neck neutral, avoid shrugging the shoulders up, don’t use excessive body swing, and adjust seat/handle height so the pull targets the upper back rather than purely the lats.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded high row where you pull overhead/forward handles toward the upper chest to emphasize the upper back (rhomboids, mid traps and rear delts) while also engaging lats and arm muscles. The torso is stabilized against a pad and movement is performed in a controlled pull-and-release.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-hand handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-hand handles.
Stand centered between the towers in an upright position with feet shoulder-width apart or one foot slightly staggered for stability.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip so the cables run low behind your legs; step forward slightly to create tension in the cables and keep a tall chest.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout the exercise (soft elbows) and a small forward lean from the hips; do not round the shoulders.
Exhale and pull both handles upward and inward in a controlled wide arc until your hands meet or nearly meet at chest level, squeezing the pectorals at the end of the motion.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, keeping the shoulders down and scapulae stable (use serratus anterior to stabilize the shoulder blades).
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement, allowing the cables to return your arms to the start position with control — do not let the weights slam.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining posture, a consistent elbow angle, and steady tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly performed with the pulleys set low; you pull each handle upward and inward in a wide arc to bring the hands together in front of the chest, emphasizing horizontal adduction to work the pectorals. Maintain a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo throughout the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).

exercises.name: Lever Supinated Grip Seated Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the desired plates and set the chest pad/seat so your chest rests against the pad with arms fully extended when you reach the handles; sit upright with feet braced on the platform or footrests.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Lever Supinated Grip Seated Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the desired plates and set the chest pad/seat so your chest rests against the pad with arms fully extended when you reach the handles; sit upright with feet braced on the platform or footrests.
instructions: Load the desired plates and set the chest pad/seat so your chest rests against the pad with arms fully extended when you reach the handles; sit upright with feet braced on the platform or footrests.
Grasp the handles with a supinated (palms-up/underhand) grip, wrists neutral and shoulder blades relaxed forward at the start.
Brace your core, keep your chest on the pad and maintain a neutral spine (no excessive arching or leaning forward).
Exhale and pull the handles toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows straight back—lead with the elbows rather than the hands.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and feel the contraction in your lats and upper back at the end range; keep the wrists and forearms steady.
Pause for a brief second at peak contraction while maintaining an upright, chest-supported position.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms back to the starting position with controlled movement until your elbows are almost straight, keeping tension on the muscles.
Repeat for the prescribed reps; avoid using torso momentum or excessive shoulder elevation—focus on controlled scapular retraction and full range.
exercises.description: Seated row performed on a plate-loaded lever machine using a supinated (underhand) grip; the movement pulls the handles toward the torso to drive horizontal shoulder extension and scapular retraction. It emphasizes the lats while also loading the arms and upper back musculature.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bench Press on Floor
exercises.notes: Set a barbell on a rack at a height you can safely unrack while lying on the floor; have a spotter if available.
instructions: Set a barbell on a rack at a height you can safely unrack while lying on the floor; have a spotter if available.
Lie supine (on your back) on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor to stabilize your hips and lower back.
Reach up and grasp the bar with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip, hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower; wrists should be neutral and stacked over the forearms.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving it directly over your chest with elbows locked and shoulders relaxed.

Inhale, then slowly lower the bar until your elbows touch or come just above the floor (upper arms at or slightly below parallel to the floor), keeping your elbows tucked slightly toward your torso to protect the shoulders.
Pause briefly with control, then exhale and press the bar upward until your arms are fully extended without locking the shoulders aggressively.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining a steady, controlled tempo; keep your back flat on the floor (avoid excessive arching) and keep the wrists straight.
When finished, carefully rack the bar with the help of a spotter or by guiding it back onto the rack supports.
exercises.description: A floor-based bench press performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while limiting range of motion. Lying on the floor, you lower the bar to the chest/upper torso and press it back up under control.

exercises.name: Cable Rope Seated Row
exercises.notes: Grasp the rope attachment with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and sit upright with a neutral spine and chest up; start with arms fully extended.
instructions: Sit on a seated row bench or platform with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the rope attachment with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and sit upright with a neutral spine and chest up; start with arms fully extended.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction) and drive your elbows straight back toward your torso.
Pull the rope toward your upper abdomen or lower chest, keeping elbows close to the body and leading with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at peak contraction while squeezing the lats and upper back, maintaining an upright torso (avoid excessive trunk lean or momentum).
Slowly extend the arms back to the starting position with control, returning the shoulders to a slightly protracted position while keeping the spine neutral.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, ensuring wrists remain neutral and movements are smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated cable rope row performed from a bench using a rope attachment to pull the handle toward the torso, emphasizing scapular retraction and elbow drive to work the lats and upper back. Keeps a neutral spine and controlled tempo to isolate the pulling muscles.

exercises.name: Lever High Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present).
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with feet flat on the floor and chest/torso stabilized against the pad (or chest pad if present). Adjust seat so handles are at or slightly above shoulder height when arms are extended.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended and shoulders relaxed. Take a full breath and brace your core.

Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the handles down and back toward the upper chest/near clavicle, leading with the elbows and keeping them at roughly shoulder height (elbows flared).
Squeeze the shoulder blades and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1 second, keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive trunk lean or momentum.
Slowly extend the arms back to the start position under control (eccentric phase), maintaining tension in the upper back and preventing the weights from slamming.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out during the pull and inhale during the release.

Common form tips: keep the neck neutral, avoid shrugging the shoulders up, don’t use excessive body swing, and adjust seat/handle height so the pull targets the upper back rather than purely the lats.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded high row where you pull overhead/forward handles toward the upper chest to emphasize the upper back (rhomboids, mid traps and rear delts) while also engaging lats and arm muscles. The torso is stabilized against a pad and movement is performed in a controlled pull-and-release.

instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Barbell Feet Flat Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
instructions: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
Lie supine on the flat bench with your eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and planted for stability, and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (pinch them together and press them into the bench) to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a full grip slightly wider than shoulder width, wrists stacked over forearms and elbows under the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar into the start position directly above your shoulders.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to mid-chest / nipple line, keeping elbows at about a 45° angle to the torso and maintaining scapular retraction.
Briefly pause with the bar lightly contacting the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward along a slightly arced path back to full arm extension.
Lock out the elbows at the top without overextending, keep the feet planted and core braced throughout the set.
After the final rep, carefully rack the bar onto the supports or have your spotter assist; use a spotter for heavy loads and maintain strict control on every rep.
exercises.description: Barbell feet-flat flat bench press is a supine pressing exercise that primarily works the pectorals by pressing a loaded barbell from the chest to full arm extension. Keeping the feet flat provides a stable base while the triceps and delts assist in elbow extension and shoulder stabilization.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Cable High Row with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.

Position an incline bench or chest pad in front of the machine so you can sit facing the pulleys with your chest pressed firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor; adjust pad height so torso is supported and arms can reach the handles overhead.
Sit tall with chest against the pad, neutral neck, core braced and shoulders down away from ears; reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Begin with arms nearly extended overhead, scapulae slightly protracted; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and out, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end range.
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a supported chest and no torso rotation.
Slowly release the handles under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the start while keeping tension on the cables and scapulae engaged.
Repeat for desired reps; avoid using momentum, leaning away from the pad, or shrugging the shoulders upward.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported cable high row performed from dual high pulleys; you pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest while the chest rests against the pad to isolate the upper back. Emphasizes scapular retraction and posterior shoulder activation with reduced lower-back involvement.

exercises.name: Cable Split Stance Single Arm Row
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to about chest height and attach a single handle; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to about chest height and attach a single handle; select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine and take a split stance (one foot forward, one foot back) so you are stable; keep knees soft and weight evenly distributed between both legs.
Reach forward and grab the handle with a neutral or overhand grip on the working side; torso should be upright with a slight hip hinge, neutral spine, and eyes forward.
Start with the working arm extended and the scapula slightly protracted; brace your core (engage abs) to prevent torso rotation.
Exhale and pull the handle back by driving the elbow toward your ribcage while actively retracting the shoulder blade—lead with the elbow rather than the hand.
Squeeze the lats and upper-back (scapular) muscles at the end of the concentric phase; keep the shoulder down and avoid shrugging.
Inhale and slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control, allowing the scapula to protract but maintaining a stable torso.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat with the opposite arm and reversed split stance to maintain balance.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable rowing variation performed from a split stance that emphasizes unilateral lat activation while challenging core stability and upper-back musculature. The split stance increases balance and prevents torso rotation so the lats and scapular retractors do the work.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.

Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Let the dumbbells hang straight down from your shoulders with your arms fully extended; keep your head aligned with your spine.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows back and up, pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs or hip area while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement, maintaining tightness through the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders excessively.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled motion until your arms are extended.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping the torso fixed (avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation) and continue to breathe: exhale while pulling, inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed with dumbbells where you hinge at the hips and pull the weights toward your torso, emphasizing the lats and upper back. The movement also engages the arms and posterior chain to stabilize the hinged position.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (straight arm)
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley.
instructions: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley. Select a moderate weight that allows controlled movement while keeping arms mostly straight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart (or a slight stagger for balance). Grasp the handle with both hands (pronated or neutral grip) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.

Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso leans about 10–20 degrees; keep knees soft and maintain a neutral spine. Arms should be extended in front of you with a small, fixed bend in the elbows (not fully locked).

Initiate the rep by driving the shoulders into extension: press the hands downward and back in a controlled arc until the hands reach thigh level and you feel maximal contraction in the lats. Keep the elbows stationary and avoid bending them to pull.
Pause briefly at the bottom while squeezing the lats and maintaining straight-arm tension.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position under control, keeping constant tension on the cable and avoiding letting the shoulders fully relax at the top.
Breathe out during the downward (working) phase and inhale while returning to the start. Perform desired number of reps with steady tempo.
exercises.description: Standing straight-arm cable pushdowns target the lats by driving the arms down from a high cable with minimal elbow flexion. The movement emphasizes shoulder extension and scapular retraction while keeping the torso stable.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm High Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley.
instructions: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley. Adjust a bench or chest pad so your chest is firmly supported and you can sit upright without leaning forward or rotating.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest pressed into the support, and grab the handle with a neutral or overhand grip using one arm; start with the arm extended and shoulder slightly protracted.
Brace your core, keep your torso stationary against the chest support, and retract your shoulder blade slightly before initiating the pull.
Pull the handle down and back in a smooth arc by driving your elbow up and back toward your ribcage, focusing on squeezing the upper back and rear delt at the end range.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while maintaining a neutral wrist and no torso rotation or shrugging, then slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain steady breathing (exhale during the pull, inhale during the return) and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm high cable row performed with chest support to isolate the upper back while minimizing torso movement. The movement pulls a high-set handle down and back with the elbow driving toward the ribcage to emphasize the upper back and rear delts.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squeeze Bench Press
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit at the end of a flat bench and bring two dumbbells together so their heads touch; lie back so you are supine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
instructions: Step 1: Sit at the end of a flat bench and bring two dumbbells together so their heads touch; lie back so you are supine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Step 2: Hold the dumbbells together at mid-chest with palms facing each other (neutral grip) and actively squeeze the dumbbells together to create tension in the chest and serratus anterior.
Step 3: Brace your core and keep a slight natural arch in the lower back (do not over-arch); keep the dumbbells touching and elbows tucked to about a 30–45° angle from the torso.
Step 4: Exhale and press the dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended while maintaining the inward squeeze; avoid fully locking the elbows.

Step 5: Pause briefly at the top while continuing to squeeze the dumbbells together, then inhale and slowly lower them back to the starting position at the mid-chest, maintaining contact between the dumbbells.
Step 6: Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on constant squeeze and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if form breaks or shoulders begin to compensate.
exercises.description: A supine dumbbell press variation where two dumbbells are held together and squeezed throughout the movement to increase chest activation and inner-pec engagement. Press the paired dumbbells up from the chest while maintaining constant inward pressure.

exercises.name: Cable Close Grip Front Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench and secure your thighs under the pads so your torso cannot rise during the movement (seated position).
Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
Start with arms extended overhead, chest up and a slight lean backward from the hips (about 10–20°) to put tension on the lats (slight recline).
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows down and back, leading with the elbows rather than the hands, and pull the bar to the upper chest/upper sternum.
At the bottom of the movement, squeeze your lats and upper back for a brief 1–2 second pause while keeping the torso stable and not using momentum.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start in a controlled manner until your lats are under tension again (full but not locked extension).
Repeat for the desired number of reps; keep core engaged and avoid excessive swinging or pulling with the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a close grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi while minimizing shoulder abduction; a narrow grip increases biceps and upper-back involvement. Performed seated with thigh pads to stabilize the body while pulling the handle to the upper chest.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (straight arm)
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley.
instructions: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley. Select a moderate weight that allows controlled movement while keeping arms mostly straight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart (or a slight stagger for balance). Grasp the handle with both hands (pronated or neutral grip) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.

Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso leans about 10–20 degrees; keep knees soft and maintain a neutral spine. Arms should be extended in front of you with a small, fixed bend in the elbows (not fully locked).

Initiate the rep by driving the shoulders into extension: press the hands downward and back in a controlled arc until the hands reach thigh level and you feel maximal contraction in the lats. Keep the elbows stationary and avoid bending them to pull.
Pause briefly at the bottom while squeezing the lats and maintaining straight-arm tension.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position under control, keeping constant tension on the cable and avoiding letting the shoulders fully relax at the top.
Breathe out during the downward (working) phase and inhale while returning to the start. Perform desired number of reps with steady tempo.
exercises.description: Standing straight-arm cable pushdowns target the lats by driving the arms down from a high cable with minimal elbow flexion. The movement emphasizes shoulder extension and scapular retraction while keeping the torso stable.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
