workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Lats Focused Row
exercises.notes: Sit tall with an upright torso, chest lifted, shoulders down and back, and a slight natural arch in the low back; eyes forward.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; grip a neutral (parallel) handle with both hands.
Sit tall with an upright torso, chest lifted, shoulders down and back, and a slight natural arch in the low back; eyes forward.
Start with the arms fully extended and the cable under light tension; inhale and brace your core.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides and focusing on squeezing the lats rather than pulling with the arms.
Pull until your hands reach your abdomen or the handle lightly contacts your torso while you actively retract the shoulder blades and pause 1 second to maximize lat contraction.
Exhale during the concentric (pull) phase; avoid leaning the torso back or using momentum—the movement should come from the shoulders/scapula and lats.
Slowly return the handle to the start position with control by extending the arms and allowing the shoulder blades to protract, maintaining an upright seated posture.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping a controlled tempo (e.g., 2 seconds pull, 1 second squeeze, 3 seconds return) and adjusting weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Seated cable row variation that emphasizes a lat-dominant pulling pattern by keeping the torso upright and driving the elbows back close to the body to maximize lat activation. Use a neutral handle and slow, controlled reps with full scapular retraction and extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Palm Rotational Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Keep a neutral spine and neck (eyes toward the floor a few feet in front of you).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand. Slightly bend the knees and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (bent-over position).
Keep a neutral spine and neck (eyes toward the floor a few feet in front of you). Let the dumbbells hang at arm's length beneath your shoulders with a pronated or neutral grip.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction).

Pull the dumbbells upward toward your lower ribs/waist by driving the elbows up and back. As you ascend, rotate your wrists so the palms turn (for example from pronation/neutral toward supination) at the top of the row.
Squeeze the upper back and rear delts at the top for a brief pause, keeping elbows close to the body and avoiding excessive torso rotation or momentum.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position while rotating the palms back to the initial orientation, maintaining control throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps. Maintain the hip hinge, slight knee bend, and neutral spine for the entire set.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row variation where the wrists/palms rotate during the pull to increase scapular retraction and arm involvement. Performed in a hip-hinged position, it emphasizes the upper back while also engaging the lats, rear delts and arms.

exercises.name: Lever Incline Hammer Chest Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is fully supported on the incline pad and the handles start roughly at upper-chest / clavicle level when grasped.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is fully supported on the incline pad and the handles start roughly at upper-chest / clavicle level when grasped.
Select an appropriate weight and sit with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and your lower back gently pressed into the bench (avoid excessive arching).
Reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral (palms facing each other) 'hammer' grip; pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the chest.
Begin with elbows bent and positioned at about a 30–45° angle from the torso (not flared fully out), wrists neutral and aligned with forearms.
Exhale and press the handles upward and slightly together in a controlled motion until your arms are nearly extended — avoid locking the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top and consciously squeeze the upper pectorals while maintaining scapular stability.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly back to the start position, keeping control of the weight and maintaining the same elbow angle and shoulder position.
Complete desired reps and return the lever to the safety/rest position before releasing the handles; avoid using momentum or excessive shoulder elevation during the set.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline lever press machine using a neutral (hammer) grip, this exercise drives the handles upward to target the upper chest. It emphasizes the upper pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps for pressing and stabilization.

exercises.name: Lever T-bar Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Stand on the machine foot platforms with feet hip-width apart and straddle the bar, grasping the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
instructions: Stand on the machine foot platforms with feet hip-width apart and straddle the bar, grasping the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is inclined forward (maintain a neutral spine and avoid rounding), and brace your core.
Begin with your arms extended and shoulders protracted so the weight is under control.
Exhale and pull the handles toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining scapular retraction and keeping the torso stable (avoid excessive torso lean or momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start under control until arms are nearly extended, keeping tension in the back throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movements smooth and avoiding shrugging the shoulders or rounding the lower back.
Adjust foot position or torso angle as needed to maintain a comfortable, neutral spine and full range of motion.
exercises.description: A bent-over plate-loaded T-bar row where you stand on the foot platform and hinge at the hips to pull the handle toward your torso, primarily working the lats and mid-back. The movement emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive while maintaining a neutral spine.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your upper back supported and feet flat on the floor for stability.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your upper back supported and feet flat on the floor for stability.
Hold one dumbbell with both hands (palms cupped against the underside of the top plate) and extend your arms straight over your chest with a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core and keep your ribs down to maintain a neutral spine; do not hyperextend the lower back.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing your shoulders to extend, keeping the elbows slightly bent and fixed at that angle.
Lower until you feel a stretch in the lats/upper chest or until your range allows without shoulder discomfort.
Exhale and pull the dumbbell back in the same arc to the starting position over your chest by contracting the lats and driving the elbows down toward your hips.
Pause briefly at the top, maintain control on every rep, and avoid jerking the weight or flaring the ribs upward.
Perform repetitions with a controlled tempo; reduce weight if you cannot maintain a stable core and neutral spine.
exercises.description: Supine dumbbell pullover performed on a flat bench involves lowering a single dumbbell in an arc from over the chest behind the head to stretch and load the latissimus and then pulling it back over the chest. It emphasizes lat lengthening and shoulder extension while also engaging the chest, triceps and serratus anterior for control.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.

Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet away, feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent; hinge forward at the hips so your torso is angled forward (standing bent-over position) with a neutral spine.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) about shoulder-width apart; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows throughout (do not lock).
Brace your core and depress your shoulder blades slightly (avoid shrugging) so tension is felt in the lats before starting the pull.

Exhale and pull the bar down in a controlled arc by extending the shoulders and pulling with the lats until the bar reaches your upper thighs or mid-thigh level; keep the arms long and the elbows fixed in their slight bend.
Squeeze the lats and hold the bottom position briefly while keeping the ribs down and spine neutral.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, resisting the cable so the lats remain under tension; do not let the lower back round as you return.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and long-arm position on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge from the hips and pull a high cable down with mostly straight arms to load the lats through a long-range extension. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while keeping the torso braced.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the pulley to the highest position and attach the pro-lat bar; select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench with knees secured under the pads and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with a neutral spine and chest slightly lifted; lean back only a few degrees (small recline) — do not arch the lower back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/clavicle while leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Hold and squeeze the lats briefly at the bottom of the movement without leaning excessively backward.
Slowly and under control allow the bar to return to the start position until the arms are nearly fully extended and the shoulder blades re-expand.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining controlled tempo and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar targets the latissimus dorsi with a wide overhand grip, pulling the bar down toward the upper chest. The movement also engages the biceps, rear delts and upper back muscles to stabilize and complete the pull.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying One Arm Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand at chest level with a firm grip (palm facing forward or neutral); place your free hand on your chest or the bench for balance.
Retract your shoulder blade slightly on the working side and brace your core to prevent your torso from rotating.
Exhale and press the dumbbell straight up until the arm is nearly extended, keeping a soft bend in the elbow (do not fully lock out).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell under control back to the chest level, keeping the elbow at about a 45-degree angle relative to the torso.
Maintain steady posture and avoid twisting; reset your scapula and core between reps if needed.
Complete the desired reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat with equal control and setup.
exercises.description: A single-arm dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench to train the pectorals unilaterally while the triceps and delts assist; the opposite hand rests for stability. Emphasizes controlled pressing and core stabilization to prevent torso rotation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).
instructions: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).
Stand facing across from the pulley in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) with a slight forward lean at the hips; engage your core and keep a neutral spine.
Reach up and grab the handle with the working hand using a neutral grip; allow the arm to start out to the side with a small bend in the elbow.
Depress and stabilize your shoulder (avoid shrugging) and pull the handle down and across your body in a smooth arc toward the opposite mid-chest, rotating the torso slightly as needed.
At the end of the movement, actively squeeze the pectorals and serratus anterior while maintaining the elbow bend; pause briefly at peak contraction.
Slowly and under control, return the handle to the starting position allowing the chest to stretch but keeping tension on the cable; do not let the weight stack slam.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Keep breathing steadily (exhale as you pull across, inhale as you return).
Maintain a slight elbow bend throughout, avoid shoulder hyperextension, and do not use excessive torso rotation or momentum to drive the movement.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable crossover isolates one side of the chest by pulling a cable across the body from a high pulley, emphasizing the pectoral contraction and allowing a strong end-range squeeze. It is performed standing with a slight forward lean and a controlled return to the start position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Low Fly
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in one hand at your side (low position) with a neutral grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in one hand at your side (low position) with a neutral grip.
Hinge very slightly at the hips so your torso tilts forward a few degrees to allow a clear arc for the arm; keep the spine neutral and core braced.
Keep a soft bend in the elbow throughout the movement and the shoulder blades stable (no excessive shrugging).
Initiate the movement from the shoulder/pecs and sweep the dumbbell upward and across your body in a wide arc toward chest/shoulder height, leading with the elbow, not the wrist.
At the top of the arc, pause briefly and squeeze the pectorals while keeping the rest of the body still.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, lowering the dumbbell back to the low starting position until the pecs are mildly stretched.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, then switch sides; avoid twisting the torso, using momentum, or fully locking the elbow.
exercises.description: A single-arm standing dumbbell fly performed from a low starting position, moving the arm in an upward arc to bring the weight across the body and emphasize the pectoral fibers. Controlled eccentric lowering and a slight elbow bend keep tension on the chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

exercises.notes: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).

exercises.name: Lever Seated Row

exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Pin Bench Press Conventional grip
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).
instructions: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).

Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes roughly under the bar, feet flat on the floor, and a slight natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress the shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a conventional shoulder-width (slightly wider than shoulder) overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Begin with the bar resting on the pins (arms either slightly bent or fully extended depending on your setup). Brace your core and take a full breath in before initiating the press.
Press the bar upward from the pins in a controlled, explosive concentric motion until the arms are fully extended and elbows locked out while exhaling.
Lower the bar under control along the same path until it gently contacts the pins at the set height (do not bounce the bar off the pins), pause as needed on the pins to reset tension.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining scapular retraction, a stable foot drive, and controlled tempo on the descent.
exercises.description: A bench press variation performed inside a rack where the bar starts and finishes on safety pins; it emphasizes the press from a dead stop to overload the pectorals through the concentric phase. Performed with a conventional (shoulder‑width) grip to target the mid-chest while the triceps and anterior delts assist.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Row
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, keeping your shoulders down and away from your ears.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your chest firmly against the pad, feet flat on the footrests and knees slightly bent; adopt a tall, neutral spine.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, keeping your shoulders down and away from your ears.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction) and driving your elbows straight back.
Continue pulling until the handles approach your lower ribs/abdomen while squeezing the lats and upper back; avoid shrugging or excessive elbow flare.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start while allowing the shoulder blades to protract, controlling the weight on the eccentric phase.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping motion smooth and avoiding torso rocking, momentum, or rounding of the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated lever row on a chest-supported machine that pulls a handle toward the torso, primarily targeting the lats and middle back while also engaging the arms and rear shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

exercises.name: Cable Half Kneeling Single Arm Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a low to mid pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a low to mid pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Assume a half‑kneeling position beside a bench: the foot of the working side is planted flat on the floor in front, the opposite knee rests on the floor (or pad), and the non‑working hand rests lightly on the bench for support.
Reach forward with the working arm and grasp the handle with a neutral grip, arm extended and shoulder relaxed; maintain an upright torso and neutral spine with core engaged.
Initiate the movement by retracting the scapula and driving the elbow back and down, pulling the handle toward your hip/ribcage — lead with the elbow rather than the hand.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the end of the concentric phase for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive rotation.
Slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control to fully lengthen the lats; maintain core tension throughout.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you return; perform the desired reps then switch sides to repeat on the opposite leg/arm.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable row performed from a half‑kneeling position that emphasizes unilateral lat activation while limiting torso rotation. The working arm pulls the handle back toward the ribcage while the non‑working arm/braced knee provide stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Cable Lying Fly
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a light–moderate weight you can control through the full range.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a light–moderate weight you can control through the full range.
Lie supine (face up) on a flat bench placed between the two pulley towers with feet flat on the floor for stability.
Reach down and grab one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-facing position; start with arms out to the sides at about shoulder height and a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (squeeze the shoulder blades together and down) to stabilize the shoulders before beginning each rep.

Exhale and bring both hands up and together in a wide arc over your chest, maintaining the slight elbow bend and focusing on squeezing the pectorals at the top; hands should meet or nearly meet above the sternum.

Inhale and slowly lower the handles back out to the starting position in a controlled manner until you feel a gentle stretch across the chest — do not let the elbows drop excessively behind the line of the shoulders.
Keep the torso stable and avoid arching the lower back; maintain control of the cables throughout the entire set and adjust weight if you cannot control the return.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then carefully release the handles and sit up before changing weight or leaving the bench.
exercises.description: A supine cable fly performed on a flat bench using low-set pulleys to isolate the chest by bringing the hands together in a wide arc. The movement emphasizes horizontal shoulder adduction while keeping a slight bend in the elbows to protect the joints.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying One Arm Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees bent to stabilize the lower body.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand at chest level with a firm grip (palm facing forward or neutral); place your free hand on your chest or the bench for balance.
Retract your shoulder blade slightly on the working side and brace your core to prevent your torso from rotating.
Exhale and press the dumbbell straight up until the arm is nearly extended, keeping a soft bend in the elbow (do not fully lock out).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell under control back to the chest level, keeping the elbow at about a 45-degree angle relative to the torso.
Maintain steady posture and avoid twisting; reset your scapula and core between reps if needed.
Complete the desired reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat with equal control and setup.
exercises.description: A single-arm dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench to train the pectorals unilaterally while the triceps and delts assist; the opposite hand rests for stability. Emphasizes controlled pressing and core stabilization to prevent torso rotation.

exercises.name: Cable Seated One Arm Alternate Row
exercises.notes: Step 1: Set a single handle on a low pulley and sit on a bench facing the machine with feet flat and slightly braced on the floor or foot platform.
instructions: Step 1: Set a single handle on a low pulley and sit on a bench facing the machine with feet flat and slightly braced on the floor or foot platform.

Step 2: Sit upright with a neutral spine, chest slightly up, shoulders relaxed, and core braced; reach forward to grab the handle with one hand while the opposite hand can rest on your thigh for stability.
Step 3: Start with the working arm fully extended and the shoulder gently protracted (forward) so there is mild tension in the cable.
Step 4: Pull the handle back by driving the elbow straight toward your hip, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blade toward the spine (scapular retraction) and keeping the elbow close to your body.
Step 5: Maintain a stable torso — avoid rotating or leaning excessively; use the arm and back to move the weight, not body momentum.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the end of the concentric phase with a full squeeze of the lats and upper back, then slowly return the handle to the starting position under control.
Step 7: Breathe out while pulling and inhale while returning. Complete the desired reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat, alternating sides for each set.
exercises.description: A seated unilateral cable row that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi through a controlled elbow-driven pull while promoting scapular retraction; performed alternating one arm at a time to address side-to-side imbalances.

exercises.name: Lever One Arm Lateral High Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat and chest/back pad so the handle is reachable with your arm extended overhead while seated; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat and chest/back pad so the handle is reachable with your arm extended overhead while seated; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your chest braced against the pad, feet flat on the floor, core engaged and torso stable to prevent twisting.
Reach up and grasp the single high handle with one hand (neutral or pronated grip) while keeping the shoulder down and away from the ear.
Initiate the movement by driving the elbow down and back in a lateral arc, leading with the elbow so the hand moves toward the side of your ribs and the shoulder blade retracts and rotates downward.
Squeeze the upper back (scapulae) at the end range for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive shoulder elevation or rotation.
Slowly return the handle to the start position with control, fully extending the arm while maintaining tension in the working muscles.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat. Maintain steady breathing (exhale while pulling, inhale while returning).
exercises.description: A single-arm high pulley/lever row performed from a seated position that pulls the handle laterally and upward to emphasize the upper back and rear shoulder musculature. It focuses on scapular retraction and elbow-led pulling to target the upper back and lats while the arm and grip work the biceps and forearms.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Larsen Press
exercises.notes: Set a barbell in a rack at a height you can safely unrack while lying on a flat bench; load appropriate weight and use a spotter or safety pins.
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack at a height you can safely unrack while lying on a flat bench; load appropriate weight and use a spotter or safety pins.
Lie supine on the bench with your eyes roughly under the bar, feet planted flat on the floor for stability, and shoulder blades squeezed together.
Grip the bar with a close/narrow grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower) with thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Unrack the bar and hold it with arms extended over the sternum, wrists stacked over elbows and bar positioned vertically above your chest.
Lower the bar under control to the upper-to-mid chest (sternal/clavicular area) while keeping elbows tucked closer to your body to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to lightly rest on the chest (brief pause) — this is the Larsen touch-and-rest — then drive the bar upward by pressing through the chest and triceps until arms are nearly extended.
Keep a tight torso and steady breathing: inhale on the descent, exhale as you press up. Repeat for desired reps.
Re‑rack the bar under control at the end of the set, making sure the bar is fully seated on the rack supports.
exercises.description: A close‑grip variation of the bench press where the bar is briefly rested on the chest (Larsen style) between reps; emphasizes the inner chest while also heavily recruiting the triceps and front delts. Performed lying supine on a flat bench using a narrow hand position.

exercises.name: Cable Close Grip Front Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench and secure your thighs under the pads so your torso cannot rise during the movement (seated position).
Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
Start with arms extended overhead, chest up and a slight lean backward from the hips (about 10–20°) to put tension on the lats (slight recline).
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows down and back, leading with the elbows rather than the hands, and pull the bar to the upper chest/upper sternum.
At the bottom of the movement, squeeze your lats and upper back for a brief 1–2 second pause while keeping the torso stable and not using momentum.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start in a controlled manner until your lats are under tension again (full but not locked extension).
Repeat for the desired number of reps; keep core engaged and avoid excessive swinging or pulling with the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a close grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi while minimizing shoulder abduction; a narrow grip increases biceps and upper-back involvement. Performed seated with thigh pads to stabilize the body while pulling the handle to the upper chest.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or as far as your mobility allows); keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown (pro lat bar)
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.

exercises.name: Cable Low Seated Row with V bar
exercises.notes: Sit tall with a slight forward lean from the hips, chest lifted, core engaged and shoulders relaxed; allow the arms to fully extend so the weight is supported by the arms and scapulae are slightly protracted.

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pronated to Neutral Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or as far as your mobility allows); keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.
instructions: Stand with feet hip‑width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms facing your thighs/ground) grip.

Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or as far as your mobility allows); keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.
Let the dumbbells hang straight down from your shoulders with arms extended and core braced.
Initiate the row by pulling your elbows back and up, driving them toward your hips/ribcage and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
As you pull, rotate each wrist from a pronated position toward a neutral (palms facing each other) position so the top of the movement finishes with a neutral grip.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining scapular squeeze, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting pronated hang while controlling wrist rotation back to pronation.
Breathe out as you pull, inhale as you lower; keep the torso stable (avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation).
Keep sets controlled and maintain a flat back throughout; reduce range or weight if you cannot keep spinal neutrality.
exercises.description: A two‑arm bent‑over dumbbell row where you start with a pronated (palms down) grip and rotate to a neutral grip as you pull; it trains the large pulling muscles of the back while engaging the arms and rear delts. Performed from a hip hinge position with a flat spine and a controlled pull and lower.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown (pro lat bar)
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Sit on the pulldown bench with knees secured under the thigh pads and feet flat on the floor (seated, knees braced).
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Begin tall with a neutral spine and a slight lean back from the hips (not from the lower back), chest lifted and eyes forward.
Inhale, then exhale and initiate the movement by pulling the shoulder blades down and together (scapular retraction) to engage the lats.
Pull the bar down in a controlled arc toward the upper chest (sternal area), leading with the elbows and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with straight arms, fully extending the shoulders while maintaining a stable torso.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding excessive torso lean or momentum; keep movement controlled and driven by the lats.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar performed from a tall seated position, pulling the bar down to the upper chest to target the latissimus dorsi and upper back. Focus is on scapular retraction and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Cable Low Seated Row with V bar
exercises.notes: Sit tall with a slight forward lean from the hips, chest lifted, core engaged and shoulders relaxed; allow the arms to fully extend so the weight is supported by the arms and scapulae are slightly protracted.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the low cable pulley with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent; grasp the V‑bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip.

Sit tall with a slight forward lean from the hips, chest lifted, core engaged and shoulders relaxed; allow the arms to fully extend so the weight is supported by the arms and scapulae are slightly protracted.
Initiate the movement by pulling the elbows straight back toward your torso (not flaring wide), driving the elbows past your sides and pulling the V‑bar to your lower chest/upper abdomen.
At the end range, squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold the contraction for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable and upright.
Slowly extend the arms forward in a controlled manner, allowing the scapulae to re‑protract and return to the start position without rounding the lower back.
Breathe out while pulling, inhale while returning; avoid using momentum or leaning excessively backward—keep the motion controlled and driven by the back and arms.
exercises.description: Seated low cable row using a V‑bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the middle/upper back by driving the elbows back and squeezing the shoulder blades, while also engaging the lats and arm muscles. The neutral V‑grip shortens the lever and increases biceps involvement.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Alternate Chest Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to approximately chest height and attach single-hand handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to approximately chest height and attach single-hand handles.
Stand in the center of the cable machine in a staggered (split) stance with one foot forward and a slight bend in both knees; lean slightly forward from the hips to engage the core.
Grasp both handles with a neutral grip and step forward until there is tension in the cables; hold both arms out to the sides with a slight bend in the elbows and shoulders down and back.
Keep the torso stable and core braced; allow one handle to remain out while you perform the fly with the other arm.

Exhale and bring the working hand in a horizontal arc across your body toward the midline, finishing with the palm facing your opposite shoulder or hands meeting in front of the sternum; maintain that slight elbow bend throughout the movement.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, squeezing the pectorals, then slowly reverse the motion under control until the arm returns to the start position.
Alternate to the other arm after completing the prescribed reps for the first arm, keeping the non-working arm either extended or lightly resisting to maintain symmetry and tension.
Maintain steady controlled tempo, avoid torso rotation or using momentum, and keep shoulders down (not shrugged) throughout the set.
exercises.description: A standing cable fly performed one arm at a time to isolate the chest by bringing a single handle across the body in a controlled arc while the other arm returns. Use a staggered stance and slight forward lean to maintain balance and core stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate decline (about 15–30 degrees) and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate decline (about 15–30 degrees) and choose a pair of dumbbells you can control for the desired reps.

Sit on the high end of the bench, place the dumbbells on your thighs, hook your feet securely under the foot pads, then use your legs to help roll back onto the bench into a declined supine position with your head lower than your hips.
Plant your feet under the pads, keep your hips touching the bench, retract and slightly squeeze your shoulder blades together to create a stable chest position.
Bring the dumbbells up to chest level with a pronated grip (palms facing forward) and elbows at roughly a 30–45° angle from your torso; this is the start position.
Take a full breath in, then press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight but not locked out; exhale as you press.
Pause briefly at the top with control, keeping wrists neutral and shoulders stable (avoid shrugging or flaring the elbows excessively).
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to chest level, stopping when the dumbbells are just above your lower chest or sternum—do not bounce off the chest.
Repeat for the prescribed number of reps while maintaining a tight core and steady foot contact under the pads; use a spotter for heavier sets or if fatigue compromises control.
After finishing, carefully bring the dumbbells to your thighs and use your legs to help sit up rather than twisting off the bench.
exercises.description: A decline dumbbell bench press is a weighted pressing movement performed on a decline bench that emphasizes the lower portion of the pectorals while also engaging the triceps and anterior delts. It is performed lying supine on a declined bench with feet secured under the pads and pressing dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your head, upper back and glutes are supported (supine on an incline bench) and place your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help rack the dumbbells and press them overhead so your arms are extended above your chest, palms facing each other (neutral grip); keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement; avoid excessive lower-back arching.

Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells in a wide, controlled arc out to your sides until you feel a stretch in the upper chest—stop when your elbows are roughly in line with the bench or slightly below shoulder level.
Keep the elbows fixed at the same slight bend throughout the descent; do not let the elbows drop or the wrists bend excessively.
Exhale and bring the dumbbells back together over your chest by squeezing the pectorals, following the same arc path used on the way down.
Pause briefly at the top with the weights close together (but avoid slamming them), then repeat for the desired number of reps.
Use a lighter weight if you feel shoulder joint pain; focus on controlled range of motion and chest contraction rather than heavy load.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench, perform a wide-arc dumbbell fly to emphasize the upper portion of the chest; movement stretches the chest as you lower and contracts the pectorals as you bring the weights back together. Keep a slight bend in the elbows and move in a controlled arc to avoid shoulder strain.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered or shoulder-width stance with knees soft and core braced (slight forward lean at the hips).
Reach down and grab each handle with a neutral or slightly supinated grip, palms facing in or up, elbows slightly bent and fixed in that position throughout the movement.
Begin with the arms out to the sides and low (handles near hips or knees) and inhale.
Exhale and pull the handles upward and inward in a wide arc, keeping the elbows slightly bent, bringing the hands together or in front of the chest/upper sternum.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze the pectorals while avoiding excessive shoulder shrugging.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, allowing the cables to return the arms to the start position while maintaining tension in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions and re-rack safely; keep torso stable and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly is a low-to-high cable chest fly that targets the pectorals by sweeping the arms upward and together from low pulley positions; performed standing with a slight forward lean and a soft bend in the elbows to emphasize the chest contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows bent.
Brace your abs and keep your chest up to maintain a neutral spine; avoid overarching the lower back.
Press one dumbbell overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is nearly or fully straight (do not violently lock the elbow).
Pause briefly at the top, keeping the shoulder engaged and scapula stable (avoid shrugging excessively).
Lower that dumbbell under control back to shoulder height while maintaining core tension.
Immediately press the opposite dumbbell overhead, alternating arms with each repetition in a steady, controlled rhythm.
Breathe out during each press and inhale as you lower; choose a weight that allows full control and good form for the desired rep range.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell shoulder press where you press one dumbbell overhead at a time, alternating arms to build shoulder strength and unilateral stability. Maintain a braced core and controlled tempo while lowering and pressing each weight.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the pulleys to the lowest position and attach single D-handles; select an appropriate weight on both sides.
instructions: Set the pulleys to the lowest position and attach single D-handles; select an appropriate weight on both sides.

Stand centered between the two stations with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral (thumb-up) grip, arms hanging down at your sides.
Cross the cables in front of you if the setup requires it so there is slight tension at the start; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the exercise.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright (no leaning or swinging). Initiate the movement by lifting the arms out to the sides (lead with the elbows) until your hands reach about shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down and back (do not shrug), then slowly lower the handles back to the start under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, exhaling as you lift and inhaling as you lower; avoid using momentum or excessive torso tilt to complete reps.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys that isolates the lateral deltoid by abducting the arms out to the sides to shoulder height while keeping the torso stable. The cable setup provides constant tension through the movement for controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward (or slightly rotated) and elbows bent roughly 90 degrees, wrists stacked over elbows.
Inhale, then exhale as you press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until your arms are nearly extended overhead; avoid fully locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulders down and chest lifted (do not shrug excessively).
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Keep the back pressed against the bench and avoid excessive lower-back arching or leaning; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press performed from a back-supported bench to drive the weights overhead, primarily working the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with triceps and upper traps assisting. The movement emphasizes vertical pressing strength and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.

instructions: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, core braced and knees soft.

Keeping the torso vertical, initiate the movement by driving the elbows up and out while pulling the bar close to the body.
Continue lifting until the bar reaches approximately collarbone/upper-chest level or until elbows are roughly shoulder height; lead the movement with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down and chest tall, avoiding excessive shrugging or leaning back.

instructions: Assume a split stance: place one foot forward and the other back with the back foot on the toes; knees should be slightly bent and weight distributed mainly on the front heel for stability.
Pick up a dumbbell with the working hand and bring it to the rack position at shoulder height (dumbbell beside the shoulder, elbow tucked, palm facing inward or slightly forward).
Brace your abs and squeeze the glutes to create a stable, upright torso; keep hips squared and avoid rotating toward the pressing side.
Press the dumbbell straight up in a controlled path until the arm is fully extended overhead, exhaling as you press; keep the wrist neutral and the elbow close to the plane of the body.
Pause briefly at the top with the weight over or slightly behind the shoulder line (not forward of the head) and avoid overarching the lower back.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the rack position under control while inhaling, maintaining core tension and the split stance balance.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch legs and repeat on the other side.

Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.

instructions: Stand tall (upright) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold an EZ bar in front of your thighs with a pronated (overhand) narrow to shoulder-width grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back, and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout the movement.
