exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing inward), arms fully relaxed.
Brace your core and press your lower back lightly into the bench to maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Slightly bend your elbows (soft arms) and keep the bend constant for the entire movement.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height); lead with the elbows and keep wrists neutral.
Keep shoulders down (do not shrug) and avoid leaning or using torso momentum—if you need to swing, reduce the weight.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Perform repetitions with a steady tempo (e.g., 1–2 seconds up, 1–2 seconds down) and choose a weight that allows strict form.
exercises.description: A seated lateral raise isolates the lateral head of the deltoid by lifting light-to-moderate dumbbells out to the sides from a seated, supported position. Use a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to emphasize shoulder abduction and limit momentum.

instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced (standing position).
Hold a pair of dumbbells together in front of your thighs with a neutral or slightly pronated grip (palms facing each other or slightly down), elbows slightly bent.
Take a breath, then drive the dumbbells upward by contracting the front of the shoulders — lift to about shoulder or eye level with a controlled motion; avoid using your lower back to lean back.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the lift and lead with the elbows rather than the hands.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder tension, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on minimal swinging and steady core engagement to stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Supinated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
Stand facing the machine in a staggered or hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Reach forward and grab the rope ends with a supinated (palms-up) grip so your thumbs point toward you; step back until there is tension in the cable and your arms are nearly extended.
Brace your core, exhale, and pull the rope toward your face by driving your elbows out to the sides and externally rotating the shoulders so the hands move toward the ears.
At the end of the movement squeeze the shoulder blades together and pause 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable (avoid leaning back or shrugging).
Inhale and slowly extend the arms forward to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping tempo controlled and focusing on rear delt and upper-back contraction rather than using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable face pull with a supinated (palms-up) rope grip emphasizes the rear delts while bringing the shoulder blades together and externally rotating the shoulders. Pull the rope toward your face with elbows high, then return under control.

exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.

The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees so the dumbbells are in front of your thighs and palms face down or slightly toward each other.
Keeping the elbows bent and the torso still, raise your forearms and elbows out to the sides until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (elbows stay higher than the hands).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder blade stability; avoid excessive shrugging or leaning the torso.
Slowly lower the weights back to the start under control, keeping tension on the delts and avoiding swinging.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining neutral spine and steady breathing throughout.
exercises.description: A standing lateral raise variation performed with the elbows bent to shorten the lever arm and focus tension on the lateral delts. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides until the upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, then lower with control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

instructions: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (palms facing each other or slightly forward), elbows under or slightly in front of the dumbbells; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Initiate a quick dip by bending the knees ~10–20 degrees and keeping the torso upright — this is a short, controlled knee bend, not a deep squat.
Explosively extend the hips and knees (drive through the legs and glutes) while simultaneously pressing the dumbbells overhead in one continuous motion.
At the top, fully extend the arms overhead with the dumbbells roughly in line with the ears, shoulders packed and core engaged; avoid excessive lower-back arching.
Lower the dumbbells under control back to shoulder height while absorbing force with slightly bent knees, reset your position if needed, and repeat.
Breathing: inhale before the dip, exhale as you drive and press; choose a weight that allows controlled technique and limit leg drive if the goal is strict shoulder strength.

Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.

Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

instructions: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, core braced and knees soft.

Breathe out while lifting and inhale while lowering. Use a controlled range — avoid lifting the elbows higher than shoulder level to reduce shoulder impingement risk.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/ENDURANCE
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Standing Front Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar with a shoulder-width, overhand (palms down) grip using the inner bends of the bar so wrists are comfortable and neutral.
Let the bar hang in front of your thighs with arms extended and shoulders down and back; brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine.
Keeping a slight bend in the elbows, raise the bar straight forward in a smooth motion until your hands reach about shoulder height; lead with the elbows and avoid shrugging.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder tension and a stable torso; do not lean back or use momentum.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with control, keeping the core braced throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping the movement strict; avoid lifting the bar above shoulder level or swinging the hips.
exercises.description: Standing front raise performed with an EZ bar to isolate the anterior portion of the delts by lifting the weight straight in front of the body to shoulder height. The movement uses a controlled tempo to emphasize shoulder flexion while the core and upper back stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cuban Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
Begin the movement by driving your elbows up and out in a controlled upright-row motion, keeping the dumbbells close to the body until your elbows reach roughly shoulder height.

With elbows elevated, externally rotate at the shoulders so your forearms move forward and the dumbbells are brought in front of your face/chest area (upper arms should be roughly parallel to the floor).
From that externally rotated position, press the dumbbells straight overhead until your arms are fully extended, keeping the core tight and avoiding excessive back arch.

Lower the dumbbells back down to the externally rotated position in a controlled manner, then rotate the forearms back to the upright-row position and lower the weights to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows smooth, controlled rotation and full overhead lockout without shrugging the shoulders.
exercises.description: A standing compound shoulder movement that combines an upright row, external rotation, and overhead press to build deltoid strength and shoulder stability while recruiting the traps and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Face Pull
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders so the dumbbells are below chest level and relax the shoulder blades.
Initiate the movement by pulling the elbows up and out (away from the body) so the upper arms move roughly parallel to the floor; lead with the elbows rather than the hands.

As the elbows reach about shoulder height, externally rotate the shoulders so the dumbbells move toward the sides of your face (palms may end up facing you or slightly upward), and squeeze the rear delts and scapular muscles together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for 1–2 seconds, keeping the torso still and chest facing the floor.
Slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the starting hanging position while keeping the torso braced.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you lower; choose a weight that allows strict form and avoid using momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: A bent-over face pull performed with dumbbells that targets the rear delts by pulling the weights toward the face while hinging at the hips to emphasize posterior shoulder and upper-back activation. It combines a high-row with external rotation to hit the posterior shoulder and scapular muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Alternate Front Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the edge of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on the edge of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a firm grip, arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed; palms can face your thighs (neutral) or rotate slightly so the thumb points up.
Brace your core and keep your chest up; avoid leaning back or using the legs to help lift.
Exhale and raise one dumbbell straight in front of you with a slight bend in the elbow, lifting to about shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tall posture and keeping the opposite arm stationary at your side.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with control.
Repeat the movement with the other arm, alternating sides in a steady, controlled rhythm; avoid swinging the torso or using momentum.
Keep repetitions controlled (2–3 seconds up, 2–3 seconds down) and stop the lift before your wrist or shoulder becomes painful; breathe out on the lift and in on the return.
exercises.description: Seated alternate front raises involve lifting one dumbbell at a time from the thigh to about shoulder height while seated, primarily working the anterior deltoid. Maintain an upright posture and controlled tempo to isolate the front delts and minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Hinge your torso forward about 30–45 degrees while keeping your back straight, chest up and neck neutral.
instructions: Sit on the front edge of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees close together; hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging down between your legs.
Hinge your torso forward about 30–45 degrees while keeping your back straight, chest up and neck neutral.
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees and keep your forearms nearly vertical with palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Exhale and lift the dumbbells out to your sides by raising your elbows, leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining the bent-elbow position and avoiding shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the weights back to the starting position under control, keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging.
Repeat for the desired number of reps; choose a weight that allows controlled motion and focus on feeling the delts work rather than using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated bent-arm lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise performed seated with the torso tipped forward and elbows bent, lifting the dumbbells out to the sides to emphasize the lateral deltoids. It minimizes momentum and places more emphasis on the delts and upper scapular muscles.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Alternate Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level (front-rack position) with palms facing forward or slightly inward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level (front-rack position) with palms facing forward or slightly inward.
Keep the wrist neutral and elbow beneath the dumbbell, scapulae stable and chest up; inhale to prepare and engage the abs.
Exhale and press one dumbbell straight overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is fully extended overhead without arching the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with the arm locked out and the dumbbell balanced over the shoulder/hip line, then control the descent back to the shoulder (front-rack) position.
As you lower the pressed arm, immediately repeat the press with the opposite arm so you alternate sides each rep; keep the non-pressing arm stable at rack height to help balance.
Continue alternating reps while maintaining a neutral spine, tight abs, and minimal torso rotation; avoid using leg drive or leaning back to lift heavier weight.
Perform repetitions in a controlled tempo and choose a weight that allows full range of motion while keeping proper form.
exercises.description: A standing single-arm (alternating) dumbbell press that develops shoulder strength and stability by pressing one weight overhead at a time while the other arm remains at rack position. Maintain a tight core and neutral spine to control the movement and prevent torso lean.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cuban Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
Begin the movement by driving your elbows up and out in a controlled upright-row motion, keeping the dumbbells close to the body until your elbows reach roughly shoulder height.

With elbows elevated, externally rotate at the shoulders so your forearms move forward and the dumbbells are brought in front of your face/chest area (upper arms should be roughly parallel to the floor).
From that externally rotated position, press the dumbbells straight overhead until your arms are fully extended, keeping the core tight and avoiding excessive back arch.

Lower the dumbbells back down to the externally rotated position in a controlled manner, then rotate the forearms back to the upright-row position and lower the weights to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows smooth, controlled rotation and full overhead lockout without shrugging the shoulders.
exercises.description: A standing compound shoulder movement that combines an upright row, external rotation, and overhead press to build deltoid strength and shoulder stability while recruiting the traps and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
instructions: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip and take a hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge forward at the hips and place your forehead or chest gently against the bench pad for support so your torso is close to horizontal (bench-assisted bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep a neutral spine and the head supported on the bench.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc — lead with the elbows — until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and you feel the rear delts contract.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position.
Keep the movement strict (no torso rotation or momentum) and maintain head/chest support on the bench throughout the set.
Choose a weight that allows controlled form for the full range of motion and perform recommended reps.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell raise that targets the rear (posterior) delts by moving the arms out to the sides while the torso is stabilized. The head/chest is supported on an incline bench to reduce momentum and isolate the rear deltoids.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Reverse Raise (skier)
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and a dumbbell in each hand held at arm's length.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and a dumbbell in each hand held at arm's length.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (bent-over position), keeping a neutral spine and neck; let the dumbbells hang beneath your shoulders with palms facing each other.
Brace your core and keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a neutral spine, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position under control.
Avoid using momentum or rotating the torso — keep the hips steady and the movement isolated to the shoulder girdle.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining consistent form and controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Standing bent at the hips with a neutral spine, this reverse raise (skier) moves dumbbells outward in a horizontal arc to emphasize the posterior delts and upper upper-back musculature. It strengthens the rear shoulder while promoting scapular retraction and postural control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing the body) and arms resting by your sides.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing the body) and arms resting by your sides.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and maintain a neutral spine and tucked chin throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise both dumbbells straight forward from the hips to about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor), leading with the elbows and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping shoulders down (avoid shrugging) and avoid using body swing or back extension to lift the weight.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, choosing a weight that allows strict form; if form breaks, reduce the weight or perform single-arm raises to maintain control.
exercises.description: A standing resistance exercise where you raise dumbbells straight in front of you to shoulder height to emphasize the anterior delts. It uses a controlled motion with core bracing to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Supinated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
Stand facing the machine in a staggered or hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Reach forward and grab the rope ends with a supinated (palms-up) grip so your thumbs point toward you; step back until there is tension in the cable and your arms are nearly extended.
Brace your core, exhale, and pull the rope toward your face by driving your elbows out to the sides and externally rotating the shoulders so the hands move toward the ears.
At the end of the movement squeeze the shoulder blades together and pause 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable (avoid leaning back or shrugging).
Inhale and slowly extend the arms forward to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping tempo controlled and focusing on rear delt and upper-back contraction rather than using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable face pull with a supinated (palms-up) rope grip emphasizes the rear delts while bringing the shoulder blades together and externally rotating the shoulders. Pull the rope toward your face with elbows high, then return under control.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward (or slightly rotated) and elbows bent roughly 90 degrees, wrists stacked over elbows.
Inhale, then exhale as you press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until your arms are nearly extended overhead; avoid fully locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulders down and chest lifted (do not shrug excessively).
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Keep the back pressed against the bench and avoid excessive lower-back arching or leaning; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press performed from a back-supported bench to drive the weights overhead, primarily working the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with triceps and upper traps assisting. The movement emphasizes vertical pressing strength and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and a slight bend in the elbows.
Keep your torso still and shoulders down (do not shrug) throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by leading with the elbows until your arms are approximately parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a slight elbow bend and neutral wrist alignment.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding swinging or leaning the torso to lift heavier weights.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell lateral raises isolate the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides from a seated, back-supported position. Performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to emphasize the shoulder without using momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor; maintain a neutral spine and neck (bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip.
Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor; maintain a neutral spine and neck (bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep the shoulder blades relaxed but ready to retract.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc (horizontal abduction) leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and at shoulder height.
Squeeze the rear delts and pinch the shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, avoiding momentum or torso swing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement controlled and the torso stable throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell fly that isolates the posterior (rear) deltoids by horizontally abducting the arms from a hinged position. Performed with a hip hinge and slight elbow bend, the movement emphasizes the rear delts while engaging the upper back and traps to stabilize and retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Front Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (palms facing your thighs).
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (palms facing your thighs).
Keep a slight bend in both elbows, shoulders down and back, chest up, and brace your abs to stabilize the torso (neutral spine).
Slowly raise one dumbbell straight in front of you (no swinging or shoulder rotation) until your arm is parallel to the floor and level with the shoulder; exhale as you lift.
Pause briefly at the top, keeping the wrist neutral and the elbow slightly bent, then inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the starting position under control.
Immediately perform the same motion with the opposite arm, alternating sides each repetition while maintaining an upright torso and minimal movement from the hips.
Use a weight you can control for the full range without rocking or using momentum; reduce weight if you find yourself leaning back or swinging.
exercises.description: Standing single‑arm front raises with dumbbells that alternately lift each arm to shoulder height to target the front of the shoulder. Performed slowly and controlled to minimize momentum and maximize deltoid activation.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Start standing upright (not kneeling or prone) with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms facing body/slightly toward you) grip, arms extended at your sides.
instructions: Start standing upright (not kneeling or prone) with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a pronated (palms facing body/slightly toward you) grip, arms extended at your sides.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back, and keep your chest up and torso vertical throughout the movement.
Initiate the lift by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the dumbbells close to the body so they travel in a straight line near the front of your thighs to chest level.

Continue pulling until your elbows reach about shoulder height or slightly above, with elbows higher than your wrists; avoid lifting the dumbbells higher than shoulder level to reduce shoulder impingement risk.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and traps, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control.
Keep the wrists neutral (avoid excessive bending), avoid excessive torso swing or leaning back, and do not use momentum—focus on lifting with the shoulders and upper back.
Choose a weight that allows you to perform the set with good form for the desired reps; if you feel excessive shrugging or shoulder pain, reduce weight or limit range of motion.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell movement where you pull weights up along the front of your body, driving the elbows upward to shoulder level to emphasize the delts and upper traps. Performed with a close grip and elbows leading, it also engages the biceps and forearms for stability.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Single Delt Row
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine at or slightly above shoulder height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine at or slightly above shoulder height and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine in a staggered or hip-width stance with a slight forward lean from the hips, core braced and knees soft.
Reach across with one arm and grasp the handle with a neutral/overhand grip; the working arm should start extended with tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by retracting the scapula and pulling the elbow up and back so the upper arm moves roughly parallel to the floor and toward the ear/upper chest.
Keep the elbow flared slightly outward (not tucked tightly to the side) to emphasize the posterior delt; lead the movement with the elbow rather than the hand.
Avoid rotating the torso or shrugging the opposite shoulder; maintain a stable torso and steady foot position throughout the set.
Squeeze the posterior delt/upper back at the top of the pull for 1 second, then slowly control the cable back to the start position on a 2–3 second eccentric.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric). Switch sides and repeat for the desired reps.
exercises.description: A unilateral high-cable row performed with the elbow elevated to emphasize the posterior deltoid and upper posterior chain; the hand is pulled toward the shoulder while the scapula retracts. Performs controlled concentric pull and a slow eccentric release to load the rear delt and upper back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Arnold Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent at ~90°, and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing your torso and elbows bent.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent at ~90°, and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing your torso and elbows bent.
Keep your chest up and back neutral (avoid excessive lower-back arch) and prepare to press by rotating your wrists outward so your palms will face forward at the top of the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells overhead while simultaneously rotating your palms until the arms are extended overhead and palms face forward; do not lock or hyperextend the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with the weights controlled overhead, maintaining tension in the delts and triceps.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled manner while rotating the wrists back to the starting position with palms facing your torso and elbows returning to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding using leg drive or excessive torso lean to lift the weights.
exercises.description: A seated rotational dumbbell press that works the shoulder muscles through a palms-in to palms-forward rotation, emphasizing the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back. Performed seated to limit torso drive and increase shoulder isolation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Push Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (palms facing each other or slightly forward), elbows under or slightly in front of the dumbbells; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
instructions: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (palms facing each other or slightly forward), elbows under or slightly in front of the dumbbells; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Initiate a quick dip by bending the knees ~10–20 degrees and keeping the torso upright — this is a short, controlled knee bend, not a deep squat.
Explosively extend the hips and knees (drive through the legs and glutes) while simultaneously pressing the dumbbells overhead in one continuous motion.
At the top, fully extend the arms overhead with the dumbbells roughly in line with the ears, shoulders packed and core engaged; avoid excessive lower-back arching.
Lower the dumbbells under control back to shoulder height while absorbing force with slightly bent knees, reset your position if needed, and repeat.
Breathing: inhale before the dip, exhale as you drive and press; choose a weight that allows controlled technique and limit leg drive if the goal is strict shoulder strength.
exercises.description: A standing compound overhead press that uses a short leg drive (push) to help propel dumbbells from shoulder height to full lockout, primarily targeting the shoulder muscles while recruiting the arms and lower body for power and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.

The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.

Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Y Raise
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45 degrees above parallel (bent-over position) with a slight bend in the knees; keep a neutral spine and neck (look at a point on the floor a few feet ahead).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a light dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other (neutral grip).

Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45 degrees above parallel (bent-over position) with a slight bend in the knees; keep a neutral spine and neck (look at a point on the floor a few feet ahead).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with elbows soft (slight bend) and shoulders relaxed away from the ears.

Initiate the movement by externally rotating the shoulders and lifting the dumbbells overhead and outward into a Y shape (hands move diagonally away from the body at about a 30–45° angle from the torso) until arms are roughly in line with the torso or slightly above shoulder level.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top without shrugging; keep the core braced to prevent low-back extension.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting hanging position, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired reps; choose light weights to maintain strict form and avoid momentum or excessive back arching.
exercises.description: A bent-over Y raise is a standing hip-hinged exercise that lifts light dumbbells in a Y shape to train the rear and lateral delts while improving upper-back stability and scapular control. It emphasizes controlled scapular retraction and external rotation through the shoulder joint.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
