exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline One Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to an incline of about 30–45 degrees and sit so the side of your torso is supported by the bench (chest/side against the pad) with feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to an incline of about 30–45 degrees and sit so the side of your torso is supported by the bench (chest/side against the pad) with feet planted on the floor.

Hold a dumbbell in the working hand with a neutral grip (palm facing your body) and let the arm hang straight down at your side with a very slight bend in the elbow; keep your torso pressed into the bench to prevent cheating.
Brace your core, keep your neck neutral, and exhale as you raise the dumbbell out to the side in a controlled arc until your upper arm is roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep the elbow slightly bent and avoid rotating the shoulder or shrugging—lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist, to emphasize the delts and limit trap involvement.
Pause for a brief second at the top with tension on the deltoid, then inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position under control.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch sides; choose a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if the traps begin to dominate.
exercises.description: Performed lying/leaning on an incline bench and raising one dumbbell out to the side, this unilateral lateral raise isolates the lateral head of the deltoid to build shoulder width. The bench support reduces torso movement so the working shoulder does most of the work.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cuban Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
Begin the movement by driving your elbows up and out in a controlled upright-row motion, keeping the dumbbells close to the body until your elbows reach roughly shoulder height.

With elbows elevated, externally rotate at the shoulders so your forearms move forward and the dumbbells are brought in front of your face/chest area (upper arms should be roughly parallel to the floor).
From that externally rotated position, press the dumbbells straight overhead until your arms are fully extended, keeping the core tight and avoiding excessive back arch.

Lower the dumbbells back down to the externally rotated position in a controlled manner, then rotate the forearms back to the upright-row position and lower the weights to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows smooth, controlled rotation and full overhead lockout without shrugging the shoulders.
exercises.description: A standing compound shoulder movement that combines an upright row, external rotation, and overhead press to build deltoid strength and shoulder stability while recruiting the traps and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl to Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward).

Initiate a controlled dumbbell curl by rotating the palms to face upward and bending the elbows to bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level; keep elbows close to your sides and avoid swinging the torso.

At the top of the curl with dumbbells near shoulder height, rotate palms so they face forward and press the weights overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the shoulders until arms are fully extended.
Pause briefly at full extension with the ribs down and core engaged; avoid overarching the lower back.
Lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled motion to shoulder height, then reverse the curl movement to return the weights to your sides in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the press and inhaling as you lower; maintain a steady tempo and avoid using momentum from the hips or legs.
exercises.description: A standing combination exercise that starts with a dumbbell curl and transitions into an overhead press, targeting the shoulder muscles while also working the arms. Perform the curl to bring weights to shoulder level, then press the dumbbells overhead and control the descent back to the start.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline One Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.

exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Face Pull
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Hinge your torso forward about 30–45 degrees while keeping your back straight, chest up and neck neutral.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/ENDURANCE
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Front and Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip.
Keep a small bend in the elbows and maintain a neutral wrist; shoulders down and back (do not shrug) to avoid excess trap activation.
Front raise: exhale and lift both dumbbells straight forward with controlled motion until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (about shoulder height), palms facing down or slightly inward.
Pause briefly at the top (0–1 second), then inhale and lower the weights slowly back to the starting position under control.

Immediately perform the lateral raise: exhale and lift both dumbbells out to your sides with the same slight elbow bend, leading with the elbow, raising to shoulder height so the arms form a T with your torso.
Hold briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the weights slowly back to your sides with control, avoiding swinging or using momentum.
Repeat the sequence (front raise then lateral raise) for the desired number of repetitions, keeping core engaged and torso still throughout.

Progression/cues: use light-to-moderate weight to maintain strict form, keep movements slow (about 1–2 seconds up, 2–3 seconds down), and avoid elevating the shoulders toward the ears to limit excessive trap involvement.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell combination that first raises the weights in front of the body (front raise) then out to the sides (lateral raise) to train the shoulder delts. Use controlled movement and a slight elbow bend to isolate the delts and minimize momentum.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Standing Front Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar with a shoulder-width, overhand (palms down) grip using the inner bends of the bar so wrists are comfortable and neutral.
Let the bar hang in front of your thighs with arms extended and shoulders down and back; brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine.
Keeping a slight bend in the elbows, raise the bar straight forward in a smooth motion until your hands reach about shoulder height; lead with the elbows and avoid shrugging.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder tension and a stable torso; do not lean back or use momentum.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with control, keeping the core braced throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping the movement strict; avoid lifting the bar above shoulder level or swinging the hips.
exercises.description: Standing front raise performed with an EZ bar to isolate the anterior portion of the delts by lifting the weight straight in front of the body to shoulder height. The movement uses a controlled tempo to emphasize shoulder flexion while the core and upper back stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine; place the free hand on your hip or at your side for balance.
Exhale and press the dumbbell straight upward by extending the elbow and lifting the arm until it is fully or nearly fully overhead without overarching the lower back.
Keep the shoulder blade stable and avoid shrugging the working shoulder; do not lean or rotate the torso toward the working side.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Perform the desired number of reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain good posture or if the lower back arches.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm dumbbell shoulder press performed from shoulder height to overhead to target the delts. The movement uses the triceps and serratus anterior for elbow extension and scapular stability while the core resists trunk tilt.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Face Pull
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Hinge at the hips until your torso is bent forward (roughly 30–60° from horizontal), keep a slight bend in the knees and maintain a neutral spine and braced core (avoid rounding the lower back).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders so the dumbbells are below chest level and relax the shoulder blades.
Initiate the movement by pulling the elbows up and out (away from the body) so the upper arms move roughly parallel to the floor; lead with the elbows rather than the hands.

As the elbows reach about shoulder height, externally rotate the shoulders so the dumbbells move toward the sides of your face (palms may end up facing you or slightly upward), and squeeze the rear delts and scapular muscles together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for 1–2 seconds, keeping the torso still and chest facing the floor.
Slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the starting hanging position while keeping the torso braced.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you lower; choose a weight that allows strict form and avoid using momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: A bent-over face pull performed with dumbbells that targets the rear delts by pulling the weights toward the face while hinging at the hips to emphasize posterior shoulder and upper-back activation. It combines a high-row with external rotation to hit the posterior shoulder and scapular muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Hinge your torso forward about 30–45 degrees while keeping your back straight, chest up and neck neutral.
instructions: Sit on the front edge of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees close together; hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging down between your legs.
Hinge your torso forward about 30–45 degrees while keeping your back straight, chest up and neck neutral.
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees and keep your forearms nearly vertical with palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Exhale and lift the dumbbells out to your sides by raising your elbows, leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining the bent-elbow position and avoiding shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the weights back to the starting position under control, keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging.
Repeat for the desired number of reps; choose a weight that allows controlled motion and focus on feeling the delts work rather than using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated bent-arm lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise performed seated with the torso tipped forward and elbows bent, lifting the dumbbells out to the sides to emphasize the lateral deltoids. It minimizes momentum and places more emphasis on the delts and upper scapular muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
instructions: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip and take a hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge forward at the hips and place your forehead or chest gently against the bench pad for support so your torso is close to horizontal (bench-assisted bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep a neutral spine and the head supported on the bench.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc — lead with the elbows — until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and you feel the rear delts contract.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position.
Keep the movement strict (no torso rotation or momentum) and maintain head/chest support on the bench throughout the set.
Choose a weight that allows controlled form for the full range of motion and perform recommended reps.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell raise that targets the rear (posterior) delts by moving the arms out to the sides while the torso is stabilized. The head/chest is supported on an incline bench to reduce momentum and isolate the rear deltoids.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Front and Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.
